do: Earth, fertility, vegetation, the physical ground of existence
at: God of the earth itself — his body IS the land of Egypt. Laughs cause earthquakes. Father of Osiris, Isis, Set, Nephthys. The physical foundation of all creation.
of: Grain, soil, green vegetation, stones placed on the earth, libations poured directly onto the ground
an: Goose (the Great Cackler), snake, bull
co: Green, black (fertile Nile soil), dark brown
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the planet of earth, structure and foundation
cr: Black tourmaline, obsidian, smoky quartz, jasper
bp: Bones, skeletal structure, legs, feet
af: I am rooted. I am held by the earth. I belong here. My body is sacred ground.
sh: Stagnation, inability to grow or move, being buried by the weight of physical existence, hoarding
ar: The Foundation / The Body Itself
cb: Nut (Sky) — Geb (Earth) and Nut (Sky) are the eternal lovers held apart by Shu (Air), their separation creating the space in which all life exists. Earth always counterbalanced by Sky.

do: Sky, stars, cosmos, the vault of heaven, rebirth
at: Her body arches over the earth as the sky itself — she swallows the sun each evening and births it each morning. Mother of all gods. The cosmic womb above us.
of: Stars drawn on blue cloth, stargazing ceremonies, offerings made at dusk and dawn
an: Cow, sow
co: Midnight blue, star-gold, deep purple
dp: Sunday / Sun — she births and receives the sun daily
cr: Lapis lazuli, blue sapphire, star sapphire
bp: The entire body as cosmic vessel, womb, ribcage
af: I am held by something infinite. I am the sky and the stars. I receive and I release.
sh: Overwhelm, cosmic dissociation, feeling untethered from earth, escapism
ar: The Cosmic Mother / The Infinite Container
cb: Geb (Earth) — she arches over him eternally. Sky requires Earth. The infinite requires the grounded. Their polarity creates the space of all life.

do: Earth, primordial mother, foundation of all existence
at: The primordial earth — the first being to emerge from Chaos. Mother of Uranus (Sky), the Titans, the Giants, and all living things. The literal ground of all being.
of: Seeds, grain, soil, fruits of the earth, bare feet on ground, care for living plants
an: Snake (chthonic wisdom), bull, all earth-dwelling creatures
co: Deep green, rich brown, terracotta
dp: Saturday / Earth (its own planet) — she IS the planet
cr: Moss agate, green jasper, brown tourmaline, petrified wood
bp: Bones, skin, intestines, legs, feet, the body as earth
af: I am held. I belong to the earth and the earth belongs to me. I am safe in my body.
sh: Ecological destruction, being cut off from nature, the earth's wrath when ignored
ar: The Great Mother / The Living Planet
cb: Uranus (Sky/Heaven) — as Geb counterbalances Nut, Gaia counterbalances Uranus. Earth and sky, the primordial polarity from which all creation springs.

do: Earth, agriculture, patience, groundedness, sacred land
at: The earth goddess — patient, abundant, endlessly giving. She receives all and complains of nothing. One of the most ancient Vedic deities. Bhumi Devi is the consort of Vishnu.
of: Seeds, grain, ghee poured on earth, sacred soil ceremonies, planting with prayer
an: Cow, elephant
co: Yellow-green, golden brown, earth tones
dp: Saturday / Venus (as Bhumi Devi) — earth's beauty
cr: Yellow jasper, tiger's eye, citrine, brown obsidian
bp: Bones, feet, skin, digestive system
af: I am patient. I am abundant. I give freely and receive freely. The earth supports me.
sh: Martyrdom, excessive giving without receiving, doormat energy, self-neglect
ar: The Patient Giver / Sacred Land
cb: Varuna / Indra (Sky/Rain) — earth requires sky's rain to be fertile. Without water from above, earth becomes desert. The earth's abundance is the sky's gift returned.

do: Earth mother, fertility, agriculture, time, cosmos
at: The supreme deity of the Andean people — Mother Earth and Mother Time simultaneously. She causes earthquakes, presides over planting and harvesting. Still actively worshipped today across Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador. Cannot be separated from the land.
of: Chicha (fermented corn drink) poured into the earth, coca leaves, food buried in the earth, the first bite of every meal given to her
an: Llama, alpaca, condor, snake
co: Brown, terracotta, deep red, green
dp: Saturday / Earth — she is the earth
cr: Red jasper, carnelian, brown tourmaline, obsidian
bp: Womb, bones, feet, digestive system
af: I am rooted in the sacred earth. I give thanks to the mother beneath my feet. I am nourished.
sh: Earthquakes when disrespected, environmental collapse, loss of connection to ancestral land
ar: The Living Earth / Sacred Time
cb: Inti (Sun) — Earth mother counterbalanced by Sky father. Pachamama (earth) and Inti (sun) are the supreme dual polarity of Andean cosmology, together sustaining all life.

do: Earth, fertility, truth, the dead, womanhood
at: She has no shrine — she needs none, because she IS the ground. Second only to Nyame (sky god) in the Akan pantheon. Thursday is her sacred day when the earth must not be worked but honoured. The earth as body, as grave, as womb, as truth.
of: Bare feet on earth, libations poured directly into the ground, first harvest given back, Thursday rest
an: Crow (her sacred bird), all creatures of the earth
co: Brown, deep green, ochre
dp: Thursday — her sacred day
cr: Brown jasper, smoky quartz, obsidian, laterite stone
bp: Womb, bones, feet, the female reproductive system
af: I walk on sacred ground. The truth lives in the earth. I am held by my ancestors beneath me.
sh: Neglect of the earth, dishonouring the dead, cutting off ancestral roots
ar: The Earth as Body / The Ground of Truth
cb: Nyame (Sky Father) — the supreme polarity of Akan cosmology. She is earth-below; he is sky-above. Together they contain all existence.

do: Earth mother, fertility, peace, sacred land
at: Ancient Germanic earth goddess described by Tacitus — she rode a wagon through the land bringing peace, fertility and prosperity wherever she went. Her sacred lake held the deepest mysteries. Possibly an earlier form or counterpart of Njörðr.
of: Grain, ale, seeds buried in earth, offerings at sacred springs or lakes
an: Wild boar, cattle
co: Dark green, brown, black
dp: Friday / Venus — earth's beauty and fertility
cr: Flint, amber, dark green tourmaline
bp: Womb, bones, lower body
af: Peace flows through me like the earth goddess moving through the land. I carry sacred ground within me.
sh: The dark waters that received her slaves — the hidden, shadow aspect of earth's sovereignty
ar: The Sacred Land / The Peace Bringer
cb: Odin / Tyr — earth goddess counterbalanced by sky/war deity. Nerthus (peace, earth) counterbalances Odin/Tyr (war, sky). Earth's peace against heaven's conflict.

do: Underworld, death, hidden wealth, roots beneath the earth
at: King of the underworld — but also god of all mineral wealth beneath the earth (his name means 'the Unseen' or 'the Rich One'). The root chakra's shadow dimension — what lies beneath the surface, the hidden wealth in darkness.
of: Black candles, obsidian, offerings buried in the earth, narcissus flowers, cypress branches
an: Black ram, Cerberus (three-headed dog), serpent
co: Black, deep navy, gold (hidden wealth)
dp: Saturday / Pluto — transformation through the underworld
cr: Obsidian, black tourmaline, jet, onyx
bp: Bones, marrow, deep root structures of the body
af: I am not afraid of my depths. Hidden treasure awaits in the darkness. I claim what is mine.
sh: Hoarding, isolation, death-avoidance, refusal to let what needs to die, release
ar: The Hidden Wealth / The Unseen Depths
cb: Zeus / Jupiter (Sky King) — the three brothers divided the cosmos: Zeus/sky, Poseidon/sea, Hades/underworld. Hades (depth, root) directly counterbalances Zeus (sky, crown). What is below must balance what is above.

do: Death, resurrection, agriculture, the afterlife, divine kingship
at: The first dying-and-rising god — murdered, resurrected, and made king of the dead. He teaches that death is not an ending but a transformation. His green skin represents vegetation and resurrection. The ultimate root chakra deity — literally buried in the earth and risen again.
of: Green candles, grain, barley beer, wheat bread, green plants, Nile water, ochre
an: Bull, scarab, heron (Bennu bird)
co: Green (resurrection), black (Nile soil), white (mummy wrappings), gold
dp: Saturday / Saturn — death and resurrection, endings and beginnings
cr: Green jasper, malachite, emerald, black obsidian
bp: Spine (the Djed pillar), physical body's resurrection
af: I am reborn from my deepest trials. What is buried in me will rise. I am more than my physical death.
sh: Set's principle — fragmentation, dismemberment, denial of wholeness
ar: The Resurrected King / The Dying God
cb: Set — the eternal brother-enemy. Osiris (order, agriculture, resurrection) counterbalanced by Set (chaos, storm, desert). Neither can exist without the other. Set creates the challenge that makes Osiris's resurrection possible.

do: War, protection, lion power, victory, Nubian sovereignty
at: The great lion-headed war god of ancient Nubia/Kush — largely erased from mainstream Western accounts of African spirituality. His temples at Musawwarat es-Sufra and Naqa in Sudan are among the most remarkable sacred sites in Africa. He represents the indigenous African divine fire predating Egypt's influence on the continent.
of: Red cloth, lion imagery, iron weapons, blood of victory, fire ceremonies
an: Lion, elephant, snake
co: Red, gold, black
dp: Tuesday / Mars — warrior fire and victory
cr: Red jasper, carnelian, ruby
bp: Legs (warrior's foundation), spine, adrenal glands
af: I stand in my sovereign power. The lion within me is undefeated. I protect what is sacred.
sh: Unchecked aggression, conquest without wisdom, might without justice
ar: The Sovereign Warrior / The Lion King
cb: Arensnuphis (Nubian companion deity of harmony) — lion-power must be balanced by wisdom and grace. Apedemak's fire balanced by the measured principle of divine order.

do: Underworld, death, the deep earth, shadow, raw power
at: Queen of the Great Below — the most ancient underworld goddess. Her name means 'Queen of the Great Earth.' She is the dark mirror of her sister Inanna/Ishtar. Terrifying in her grief and her power. She teaches the root chakra truth: only by descending into the deepest darkness do we discover our true foundation.
of: Black candles, dark earth, bones, offerings buried deep in the ground, midnight ceremonies
an: Snake, owl, lion
co: Black, deep crimson, charcoal grey
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the deepest underworld
cr: Black obsidian, jet, dark garnet, black tourmaline
bp: Bones, the deepest root structures, marrow
af: I am not afraid of my darkness. My pain has power. I am sovereign even in my grief.
sh: Inconsolable grief, rage without release, the wound that refuses to heal, the shadow that devours
ar: The Dark Queen / The Grief Goddess
cb: Inanna/Ishtar — the supreme sister-polarity. Inanna (ascent, sky, love, power) counterbalanced by Ereshkigal (descent, earth, death, grief). Each requires the other. The famous myth of Inanna's Descent is the story of this polarity resolving into wholeness.

do: Primordial ocean, the first creator, mother of all gods
at: THE FORGOTTEN ONE. Nammu is the most ancient Sumerian deity — the primordial sea, the mother who gave birth to heaven and earth (An and Ki), and then to all the gods including Enki. In the oldest Sumerian texts she is called 'the mother who gave birth to heaven and earth' and 'the mother who gave birth to all the gods.' She predates and supersedes all other Sumerian deities. She was systematically erased as patriarchal Babylonian religion replaced her with male creator gods. She must be resurrected.
of: Salt water, deep blue candles, ocean water ceremonies, blue lotus, lapis lazuli placed in water
an: Sea serpent, the great fish of the deep
co: Deep ocean blue, midnight black, primordial silver
dp: Monday / Moon — the primordial waters from which the moon draws
cr: Lapis lazuli, aquamarine, dark sapphire, ocean jasper
bp: The womb, all water in the body, blood, amniotic fluid
af: I am the mother of all things. I was here before the beginning. I will be here after the end.
sh: The formless chaos before creation — the terror of absolute dissolution
ar: The First Mother / The Primordial Ocean
cb: An (Sky Father) — she gave birth to the sky. The primordial mother-ocean always counterbalanced by the sky above, her child who grew beyond her and is now held in cosmic polarity with her.

do: Earth, fertility, mother of all living things, wild animals, sacred motherhood
at: One of the original four supreme Sumerian deities — equal to Anu, Enlil and Enki. Her name means 'Lady of the Sacred Mountain.' She was the mother of all living things, healer, creator of humans alongside Enki. Systematically demoted and eventually erased as male deities were elevated above the divine feminine. She must be honoured.
of: Mountain soil, wild herbs, milk, butter, grain, offerings to the sacred feminine in all its forms
an: Lion, deer, wild animals of the mountain
co: Green, brown, cream, mountain grey
dp: Friday / Venus — the sacred feminine creative principle
cr: Green jasper, serpentine, malachite, aventurine
bp: Womb, breasts, the entire female reproductive system, bones
af: I am the mother of all living things. My creative power is sacred and sovereign. I am restored.
sh: The wounded mother — the pain of being stripped of power, recognition and sovereignty
ar: The Divine Mother / The Sacred Feminine Erased and Restored
cb: Enki — in the Sumerian myths Ninhursag and Enki are consorts and creative partners. Her earth power counterbalanced by his water wisdom. The divine feminine creative principle balanced by the divine masculine wisdom principle.

do: Wild nature, mountains, the earth, lions, ecstatic ritual, the Great Mother
at: The Great Mother of the Gods from Anatolia (modern Turkey) — one of the oldest known mother goddesses, predating Greek religion. Her worship was wild, ecstatic and transgressive. Adopted by Rome where she became Magna Mater (Great Mother). Her priests (Galli) voluntarily castrated themselves in devotion to her — a radical act of surrendering the ego's masculine drive to the Great Mother's power.
of: Pine cones and pine branches, violets, lion imagery, cymbals and drums, ecstatic dance, mountain offerings
an: Lion (she rides them), bull, bees
co: Black (meteorite stone — her sacred object), deep red, earth brown
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the ancient earth, the pre-Olympian mother
cr: Meteorite, black obsidian, jasper, amber
bp: The womb, adrenal glands, root structures of the body
af: I surrender the ego to something wilder and greater. The Great Mother holds me. I am safe in my wildness.
sh: The destructive grief of the Great Mother — Cybele's mourning for Attis caused all life to cease growing
ar: The Wild Mother / The Ecstatic Earth
cb: Attis (her consort who died and was resurrected) — the dying and rising god who is always reborn through the Great Mother. Life requires death requires life. The eternal cycle. Counterbalanced by Uranus/the sky.

do: The sacred hearth, home, sanctuary, inner fire, foundation of civilisation
at: The most ancient and overlooked of the Olympians — she has no stories because she IS the foundation. The hearth fire that must never go out. Home, safety, belonging, the centre that holds everything else in place. The Vestals tended her flame in Rome for over a thousand years.
of: A candle or fire kept burning, bread, salt, offerings made at the home hearth, daily acknowledgment
an: None — she needs no animal symbol, she is the foundation itself
co: Warm orange, gold, warm white of the flame
dp: Saturday / Vesta (asteroid) — the sacred hearth
cr: Warm orange calcite, fire opal, garnet
el: Earth / Fire (hearth fire is the earth's fire)
bp: The abdomen (as the body's hearth), the home as extended body
af: I am the fire that does not go out. My home is sacred. I am the centre that holds.
sh: The unlived life — Hestia's shadow is the sacrifice she made in remaining always at the hearth, never venturing out
ar: The Sacred Hearth / The Centre That Holds
cb: Hermes / Mercury — Hestia (centre, home, stillness) counterbalanced by Hermes (movement, boundaries, the messenger who crosses all thresholds). The fixed flame counterbalanced by the eternal traveller.

do: Ocean, the womb, motherhood, emotions, the subconscious, all waters
at: Mother of Waters, Mother of all Orishas. She rules the ocean, the womb, emotions and the subconscious mind. Every river on Earth runs to her. She is the great container — the ocean that holds everything. Deeply nurturing, fierce when her children are threatened.
of: Blue and white flowers, watermelon, molasses, blue cloth, seashells, offerings floated on the ocean
an: Fish (all fish are her children), duck, sea turtle
co: Blue, white, aquamarine
dp: Monday / Moon — the ocean is ruled by the moon
cr: Blue topaz, aquamarine, moonstone, pearl
bp: Womb, breasts, the lymphatic system, all bodily fluids
af: I am held by the great mother. My emotions are sacred waters. I am safe in the depths of feeling.
sh: Suffocating motherhood, refusing to let children grow, the ocean that drowns rather than nurtures
ar: The Great Mother / The Ocean Herself
cb: Oshun — the ocean mother counterbalanced by the river daughter. Also Olokun (the terrifying deep) counterbalanced by Yemoja (the nurturing surface). And Ogun (iron, land) counterbalances Yemoja (water, ocean).

do: Water, beauty, seduction, wealth, healing, fertility, the spiritual power of water
at: One of the most widely revered water spirits across the entire African continent and diaspora. She appears as a beautiful woman — often half-fish or holding a snake — who emerges from and returns to the water. She offers great gifts: beauty, wealth, healing, supernatural power. But she also demands devotion and can be dangerous to those who disrespect her or break their promises to her.
of: Perfume, mirrors, combs, sweets, gin or rum, red and white cloth, offerings placed at the water's edge
an: Snake (her most sacred symbol), fish, the python
co: Red and white (her primary colours), blue, silver
dp: Monday / Venus — water and beauty
cr: Clear quartz, selenite, pearl, white coral
bp: Womb, kidneys, sacral plexus, skin (beauty), hair
af: I am worthy of abundance. My beauty is power. I honour the waters within me and around me.
sh: The seductress who takes what you love most in exchange for her gifts — the dangerous face of sacral gifts without boundaries
ar: The Enchantress / The Water Spirit
cb: Ogun (iron, land, the forge) — Mami Wata (water, seduction, the fluid) counterbalanced by Ogun (earth, iron, the solid). Water and iron. The fluid and the forged.

do: Love, luxury, beauty, femininity, sensuality, desire, romantic love
at: The Lwa of love, luxury, beauty and desire. She is associated with pink, gold, jewellery, perfume and sweet things. She embodies the pleasure principle in its most refined, romantic expression. She weeps at the end of every ceremony because no one can love her fully enough — there is always more love to be given than the world can hold.
of: Sweet champagne or wine, pink and gold candles, mirrors, perfume, jewellery, pink roses, sweet cakes
an: None specific — she is too refined for animals
co: Pink, gold, sky blue
dp: Thursday / Venus — love and luxury
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline, moonstone
bp: Womb, heart, skin, the pleasure centres of the body
af: I am worthy of love in its fullest expression. I receive beauty and pleasure with grace. I am enough.
sh: The perpetual grief of not being loved enough — the shadow of attachment to love, the wound of feeling forever incomplete
ar: The Romantic / The Beauty Goddess
cb: Erzulie Dantor — the fierce, protective mother counterbalances the romantic, refined lover. Also Baron Samedi (death) counterbalances Erzulie Freda (love). Life and death in eternal polarity.

do: Fierce motherhood, passionate love, raw female power, protection, vengeance
at: The dark, fierce sister of Erzulie Freda. She is the single mother, the warrior woman, the protector of children and of women who have been betrayed. She was invoked during the Haitian Revolution — the only successful slave revolution in history. Her tongue was cut out by enemies, so she speaks in grunts but her power needs no words.
of: Dark rum, fried pork, black candles, a knife, donations to causes protecting women and children
an: Black pig, serpent
co: Black, red, dark blue
dp: Tuesday / Mars — fierce protection and warrior fire
cr: Red garnet, black tourmaline, obsidian
bp: Womb (as fierce protector), adrenal glands, hands
af: I protect what I love without apology. My power is righteous. I will not be silenced.
sh: Vengeance without mercy, destruction beyond necessity, the mother who smothers rather than protects
ar: The Fierce Protector / The Revolutionary Mother
cb: Erzulie Freda (love, refinement, romance) — the two Erzulies are the great polarity within the divine feminine: fierce love vs. tender love. Also Ogou (warrior) is her counterpart; they are often seen as paired forces.

do: The sea, beauty, music, creative mystery, the unconscious depths
at: The Lwa of the sea, beauty and music. The Haitian expression of Mami Wata — enchanting, deeply feminine, tied to unconscious depths, creativity and the full spectrum of sensory pleasure. She is the muse — the deep water from which creative inspiration rises.
of: Blue and silver offerings, perfume, mirrors, music played at the ocean's edge, rum
an: Mermaid (she IS the mermaid), fish, sea turtle
co: Blue, silver, sea-green
dp: Monday / Moon — the sea and the moon move together
cr: Blue topaz, aquamarine, abalone, moonstone
bp: Sacral plexus, kidneys, creative womb, the ears and creative hearing
af: My creativity flows from the deep ocean within me. I trust the mystery. Beauty surrounds me.
sh: The siren song that lures to destruction — beauty as trap, the creative unconscious that drowns rather than inspires
ar: The Muse / The Deep Creative
cb: Ogou Feray (iron, fire, warrior) — the siren's fluid beauty counterbalanced by the warrior's iron will. Water and fire. The deep and the forge.

do: Love, music, beauty, dance, sensuality, fertility, joy, motherhood
at: The most joyful and pleasure-affirming deity of the ancient world. Goddess of love, music, beauty, dance and the deep joy of being alive. She is the eye of Ra but in her benevolent face. Her worship was celebrated with music, dance, alcohol and sexual expression. She holds the sistrum (sacred rattle) that drives away evil.
of: Red wine, beer, mirrors, flowers (particularly lotus), sistrum rattles, dancing in her honour, music
an: Cow (she often wears cow horns), lioness (her fierce face), falcon
co: Gold, turquoise, red
dp: Friday / Venus — love and beauty
cr: Turquoise, rose quartz, amber, carnelian
bp: Womb, heart, breasts, the sense organs (sight, hearing, smell — all pleasure)
af: Joy is my birthright. I celebrate my body and my senses. Love and beauty are sacred.
sh: Sekhmet — when Hathor's joy is refused and she becomes the destroyer. The shadow of the love goddess is the rage of the unloved.
ar: The Joy Goddess / The Sacred Celebrant
cb: Sekhmet — Hathor and Sekhmet are literally the same goddess in different aspects. Hathor (love, joy, pleasure) counterbalanced by Sekhmet (wrath, solar fire, destruction). The most complete cosmic polarity in Egyptian cosmology — the two faces of divine feminine power.

do: Love, beauty, sensuality, desire, the sea (her origin), creative power
at: Born from the sea foam where Uranus's severed genitals fell — she is literally the sacral made divine, arising from the sacred sexual waters. She governs all love: romantic, erotic, creative, and divine. Her golden girdle makes all who wear it irresistible. She causes wars and makes peace with equal ease.
of: Red and pink roses, myrtle, sea water, sea shells, mirrors, perfume, copper offerings (her metal), apples
an: Dove, swan, sparrow, dolphin, bee
co: Red, pink, gold, sea-green
dp: Friday / Venus — the entire planet is named for her
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline, emerald, copper
bp: Womb, ovaries, kidneys, the pleasure centres, skin
af: I am the ocean of love. I am beautiful and I know it. Desire is sacred in me.
sh: Love as vanity, the wound of rejection, the goddess who destroys those who spurn her — Narcissus, Hippolytus, Psyche's trials
ar: The Love Goddess / The Sacred Desire
cb: Ares/Mars — love and war are the supreme polarity in Greek mythology. They are lovers precisely because they are opposites. Aphrodite (love, beauty, water) counterbalanced by Ares (war, violence, fire). Also counterbalanced by Artemis (chastity, the wild, the unmarried).

do: Magic, fertility, motherhood, healing, the divine feminine, resurrection, wisdom
at: The greatest of the Egyptian goddesses — her worship spread across the entire ancient world from Egypt to Rome. Mistress of magic, devoted mother, faithful wife who reassembled Osiris after his murder. She is the template for the divine mother across Western civilisation. Her wings shade and protect. Her magic has no limit.
of: Lapis lazuli, turquoise, blue cloth, water from the Nile, lotus flowers, kohl, incense of myrrh
an: Kite (hawk), cow, scorpion, snake
co: Blue, green, gold, white
dp: Friday / Moon — the great feminine
cr: Lapis lazuli, moonstone, turquoise, emerald
bp: Womb, reproductive system, the entire female body, breasts
af: My magic is real. I can reassemble what has been broken. I am both mother and priestess. I am whole.
sh: The wound of Isis is the grief of losing what you love most — and the shadow is the one who refuses to reassemble herself after loss
ar: The Great Mother / The Magician Queen
cb: Nephthys — the two sisters are the great Egyptian feminine polarity. Isis (life, light, manifest magic) counterbalanced by Nephthys (death, shadow, hidden magic). Together they attend all births and all deaths. Neither is complete without the other.

do: Creativity, art, music, knowledge, wisdom, the sacred river, speech
at: Goddess of everything that flows — music, speech, learning, art, creativity, rivers. She sits on a white lotus, playing the veena (sacred lute). She is the divine intelligence that gives form to creative impulse. The river Saraswati is considered lost underground — like the sacred creative waters of the sacral.
of: White flowers (particularly lotus and jasmine), white cloth, books, musical instruments, pens, honey, milk
an: Swan (discernment — the swan drinks milk from water), peacock
co: White, gold, cream, pale blue
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — communication, learning, eloquence
cr: White quartz, moonstone, aquamarine, blue topaz
bp: Tongue, vocal cords, hands (for creating), brain, sacral creative centre
af: My creative voice is sacred. Knowledge flows through me. I express truth with beauty.
sh: The river gone underground — suppressed creativity, the voice that will not speak, the gift that lies dormant
ar: The Creative Muse / The Wisdom Goddess
cb: Lakshmi — Saraswati (knowledge, creativity, the immaterial arts) counterbalanced by Lakshmi (abundance, material wealth, beauty). The traditional tension: Saraswati and Lakshmi are said not to dwell in the same house — wisdom and wealth are in polarity. Also counterbalanced by Kali (destruction that clears for new creation).

do: Abundance, beauty, fortune, pleasure, prosperity, sacred enjoyment, grace
at: Goddess of abundance, beauty, prosperity and the sacred enjoyment of life's gifts. Stands on a lotus rising from water (the sacral's element). She governs material and spiritual abundance simultaneously. She is drawn to beauty, cleanliness and gratitude — she leaves where there is ingratitude or neglect.
of: Gold and yellow flowers (lotus, marigolds), gold coins, rice, milk, honey, saffron, camphor, Lakshmi Yantra
an: Elephant (royal abundance), owl (her night aspect), lotus
co: Gold, yellow, pink, red
dp: Friday / Venus — beauty and abundance
cr: Citrine, yellow sapphire, gold topaz, pyrite
bp: Kidneys, sacral plexus, skin, the organs of pleasure and fertility
af: I am worthy of abundance. Grace and beauty flow to me. I receive all of life's gifts with gratitude.
sh: Greed, the worship of money over spirit, the person who chases Lakshmi and drives her away
ar: The Abundance Goddess / The Grace of Life
cb: Saraswati — wealth and wisdom in eternal polarity. Also Alakshmi (the goddess of misfortune, Lakshmi's shadow-sister) counterbalances her. And Kali (stripping away) counterbalances Lakshmi (building abundance).

do: Primordial creative force, Kundalini, the feminine divine principle, power itself
at: Not merely a goddess — the fundamental creative force of the universe. Without Shakti, Shiva is inert. She is the energy in every heartbeat, every creative act, every breath. In the body she is Kundalini — coiled at the base of the spine. The Sacral Chakra is fundamentally Shakti territory: the place where cosmic creative force takes up residence in the body.
of: Red hibiscus flowers, red candles, kumkum (red powder), pomegranate, coconut, Sri Yantra, sandalwood and rose incense
an: Lion (she rides Durga's lion), tiger, peacock
co: Red, gold, deep crimson
dp: Tuesday / Mars (fierce) and Friday / Venus (creative) — both faces
cr: Red garnet, ruby, carnelian, red jasper
el: Fire / Water — the creative force that is both
bp: The entire body — she is the life-force in all of it. Specifically: womb, spine (Kundalini channel), sacral plexus
af: I am the creative power of the universe. I am Shakti. The force that creates worlds moves through me.
sh: Power without wisdom — the creative force unleashed without direction or containment can destroy as readily as it creates
ar: The Creative Force Itself / The Feminine Divine
cb: Shiva — the supreme polarity of all existence. Shakti (creative power, the dynamic) counterbalanced by Shiva (pure consciousness, the still). Their union IS creation. Their separation IS the universe. The most fundamental cosmic polarity in Hindu thought.

do: Rain, water, fertility, agriculture, lightning, the emotional nourishment water brings
at: The ancient rain god of Mesoamerica — predating even the Aztec civilisation. He governs the water that makes life possible: rain, lightning, rivers, springs. Children were sacrificed to him, and their tears were considered sacred — because tears (like rain) brought life. His realm Tlalocan was a paradise of abundant water.
of: Water, blue candles, rain water ceremonies, jade, copal incense, blue-green offerings
an: Frog, toad, serpent, heron
co: Blue, blue-green, turquoise, jade
dp: Monday / Moon — rain and the moon govern water
cr: Turquoise, jade, aquamarine, blue topaz
bp: Sacral plexus, kidneys, the entire water system of the body
af: I allow nourishing rain into my life. My emotions sustain growth. I welcome what the rain brings.
sh: Flood, drought — the water that is too much or too little. The god who demands sacrifice to give
ar: The Rain Father / The Nourisher
cb: Huitzilopochtli (solar fire, war) — rain counterbalanced by sun. Water and fire. The creative tension that makes agriculture possible. Also Xipe Totec (renewal through stripping away) counterbalances Tlaloc's nourishment.

do: Rivers, lakes, seas, flowing water, fertility, feminine creative power, childbirth
at: 'She of the Jade Skirt' — the Aztec goddess of all flowing bodies of water. She governs rivers, lakes, seas and streams. Patron of childbirth, protector of women in labour, guardian of children. In the Aztec creation myth, she ruled over the Fourth Sun (age) whose destruction came through a great flood.
of: Jade, water from rivers, blue-green cloth, offerings of food floated on water, turquoise jewellery
an: Fish, frog, water serpent, heron
co: Blue-green (jade colour), turquoise, aqua
dp: Monday / Moon — water and the feminine
cr: Jade, turquoise, blue-green fluorite, aquamarine
bp: Womb, kidneys, amniotic waters, the sacral region
af: I flow with sacred power. My feminine creative force is protected and sovereign. I am the jade river.
sh: The great flood — the water that destroys an entire age of creation. The emotion that overwhelms completely.
ar: The River Goddess / The Jade Waters
cb: Tlaloc (rain from above) — the sky water and the earth water in polarity. Also counterbalanced by Huitzilopochtli (solar fire), who is the perpetual opposite of all water deities in Aztec cosmology.

do: Everything that flows — water, music, eloquence, creativity, time, knowledge
at: One of the Seven Lucky Gods of Japan — the only woman among them. She governs all flowing things: rivers, the sea, music, eloquence, art, wisdom, and time itself. She plays the biwa (lute) and is associated with serpents and dragons. Her shrines are always on or near water.
of: Water, music played near water, white serpent imagery, biwa (lute), white candles, rice wine
an: White serpent (her messenger and companion), dragon
co: White, gold, red
dp: Friday / Venus — creativity, music, beauty
cr: Pearl, white jade, aquamarine, clear quartz
bp: Vocal cords, tongue, hands (for music), sacral creative centre
af: Creativity flows through me like a sacred river. My voice is music. All that flows is sacred.
sh: The jealous lover — Benzaiten's shadow is possessive love and creative jealousy
ar: The Flowing Creative / The Music Goddess
cb: Raijin (thunder, storm) or Fūjin (wind) — the flowing feminine counterbalanced by the wild masculine storm forces. Music counterbalanced by thunder.

do: The sea, drowning, the emotional depths, the collection of the dead
at: The Norse sea goddess who catches the drowned with her net. She is the ocean's dark face — the goddess who takes sailors down. But also the owner of tremendous wealth in her underwater hall. The Norse understood that the sacral's gift and danger are the same: the emotional depths that can nurture or drown.
of: Gold (sailors would carry gold so that Ran would welcome them in death), silver, sea water, offerings at the ocean
an: Fish (all fish swim in her net), seal, sea serpent
co: Deep sea green, black, silver
dp: Monday / Neptune — the deep ocean
cr: Aquamarine, dark sea glass, abalone, pearls from the deep
bp: Lungs (drowning/breath), the emotional body, the deep unconscious
af: I acknowledge the power of the deep. I swim with the current rather than against it. I am not afraid of depth.
sh: The net that captures — the emotional undertow that pulls under rather than supports. The grief that drowns.
ar: The Dark Ocean / The Collector
cb: Odin (the sky, the wanderer on land and air) — sea counterbalanced by sky and land. Also Njörðr (the sea's benevolent face) counterbalances Ran's destructive face. The life-giving sea and the death-dealing sea are her polarity with herself.

do: Love, war, fertility, the morning and evening star, political power, the sacred descent
at: THE MOST IMPORTANT ERASED GODDESS. Queen of Heaven and Earth — the supreme deity of ancient Sumer, who was systematically downgraded as patriarchal Babylonian religion developed. Her myth of the Descent to the Underworld is the oldest written myth in human history — a sacred journey of stripping away all power to reach the root of being. Her story was buried and distorted. She must be fully restored.
of: Dates, reeds, lapis lazuli, gold, the evening star (Venus) ceremony, roses, cedar incense
an: Lion (she stands on lions), serpent, dove
co: Gold, lapis blue, red
dp: Friday / Venus — she IS the planet Venus (both morning and evening star)
cr: Lapis lazuli, carnelian, ruby, gold
el: Fire / Water — she bridges both in her descent and ascent
bp: Womb (love), spine (sovereignty), sacral plexus, the entire body in the descent ritual
af: I descend willingly and I rise in full power. I am the Queen of Heaven and Earth. I am not diminished. I am restored.
sh: The descent without return — the ego that cannot survive the stripping away. Her shadow was Ereshkigal, her own dark sister.
ar: The Descent and Return / The Sovereign Queen
cb: Ereshkigal — the sisters are the supreme polarity of Sumerian cosmology. Inanna (love, ascent, sky, power) counterbalanced by Ereshkigal (death, descent, earth, grief). The myth of their reconciliation is the story of wholeness being restored. Also counterbalanced by Enlil (sky, patriarchal order) whom she frequently outsmarted and overcame.

do: Solar fire, healing, war, plague and its cure, the fiercest face of the divine feminine
at: The lioness goddess — both the disease and the cure, the destroyer and the healer. The Eye of Ra in its most terrifying aspect. Priests of Sekhmet were the greatest healers in the ancient world precisely because they understood that the power to destroy is the same as the power to heal. She was sent to destroy humanity and became so ecstatic in destruction she almost ended the world.
of: Red wine (symbolic of blood), red candles, lioness imagery, gold, solar incense, offerings at noon under the full sun
an: Lioness (she is the lioness), cobra
co: Red, gold, solar yellow
dp: Sunday / Sun and Tuesday / Mars — solar fire and warrior power
cr: Red jasper, carnelian, ruby, garnet, sunstone
bp: Solar plexus, digestive fire, the immune system (she governs disease and its cure), adrenal glands
af: My fire heals. My power destroys only what must be destroyed. I am the lion that roars truth.
sh: Sekhmet's shadow is destruction without restraint, the power that turns on its own, the healer who becomes the plague
ar: The Healing Warrior / The Solar Lioness
cb: Bast / Bastet — the two lioness goddesses are the supreme polarity. Sekhmet (solar fire, destruction, the desert sun) counterbalanced by Bast (moonlight, nurturing, protection, the domestic cat). The same solar power in its terrifying and loving faces.

do: The sun, light, creation, kingship, the defeat of darkness each night
at: The supreme solar deity — he travels through the sky by day and the underworld by night, defeating Apophis (chaos serpent) each night to rise again each morning. He is the demonstration that darkness can be defeated. Every sunrise is his victory. The source of all warmth, light and life.
of: Solar ceremonies at dawn, gold, frankincense, solar water (water charged in the sun), yellow flowers
an: Falcon (Ra-Horakhty), ram (Amun aspect), scarab beetle (the morning sun)
co: Gold, yellow, orange-red
dp: Sunday / Sun — the sun is his body
cr: Sunstone, citrine, amber, yellow topaz, gold
bp: Solar plexus, eyes (the sun as the eye), the entire body's vital force
af: I defeat my darkness every single night and I rise every single morning. My light cannot be permanently extinguished.
sh: Apophis — the serpent of chaos that Ra must defeat nightly. The shadow of the sun is the darkness that surrounds it.
ar: The Victorious Sun / The King of All
cb: Apophis (Apep) — the eternal chaos serpent. Ra (order, light, the sun's journey) counterbalanced by Apophis (chaos, darkness, dissolution). Also Osiris (the sun's underground journey) counterbalances Ra (the sun's sky journey). The two aspects of the solar cycle.

do: Invincible feminine power, victory over evil, the protection of righteousness
at: Invincible feminine power — she exists specifically to destroy what defeats all the male gods combined. She was created by the combined power of all the gods when the demon Mahishasura could not be killed by any male deity. She rides a lion, carries weapons in her ten arms, and cannot be conquered. She is the solar plexus at its most sovereign.
of: Red hibiscus flowers, red cloth, red candles, Durga puja ceremonies (especially Navratri), chanting Om Dum Durgayei Namaha 108 times
an: Lion (her mount), tiger
co: Red, orange, gold
dp: Tuesday / Mars — warrior power, victory
cr: Red garnet, ruby, bloodstone, carnelian
bp: Solar plexus, adrenal glands, the immune system, the warrior's body
af: I am invincible to what truly threatens me. The combined power of the universe stands behind my will. Nothing can conquer me.
sh: The warrior who never lays down her weapons — power that cannot rest, that sees threat everywhere
ar: The Invincible One / The Supreme Warrior Goddess
cb: Lakshmi — the warrior and the lover, power and beauty. Also Saraswati (wisdom) and Durga (power) form the Hindu triple goddess with Lakshmi (wealth) — all three must be in balance. Counterbalanced by Shiva (stillness, the witness).

do: Ego death, liberation, time, transformation through the destruction of the false self
at: The darkest and most liberating face of the divine mother. She stands on Shiva's chest (to stop her own destruction). She wears a garland of severed heads (the ego's thoughts) and a skirt of severed arms (the ego's actions). She cuts through illusion with absolute ruthlessness. She is not about death — she is about the death of what is false so that what is true can live.
of: Dark blue and red flowers, midnight ceremonies, skull imagery, hibiscus, red wine, fierce mantra chanting
an: Jackal, crow, vulture (creatures that consume the dead)
co: Black, deep red, crimson
dp: Tuesday / Ketu (South Node) — dissolution and liberation
cr: Black tourmaline, obsidian, black kyanite, dark garnet
bp: Solar plexus (ego centre she dissolves), the immune system, adrenal glands
af: I release what is false in me without grief. I welcome the liberation of what must die. I am not my ego. I am free.
sh: Kali's shadow is nihilism — the destruction that becomes an end in itself, rather than a clearing for new life
ar: The Liberator / The Destroyer of Illusion
cb: Lakshmi — the supreme polarity in Hindu goddess traditions. Kali (stripping away, liberation, darkness, time) counterbalanced by Lakshmi (building, abundance, beauty, timelessness). You cannot build true abundance without Kali first clearing away the false. Also counterbalanced by Saraswati (wisdom to direct the destruction).

do: Sacred fire, the divine witness, transformation, purification, the vehicle of all offerings
at: The sacred fire itself — the first and most fundamental Vedic deity. Every Vedic ritual requires Agni because he is the bridge between humanity and the divine: all offerings pass through him to reach the gods. He has seven tongues, three forms (domestic, sacrificial, lightning) and two mothers (the fire-sticks). He is transformation through divine witness.
of: Fire itself (Havan/Yajna), ghee poured into sacred fire, red hibiscus, Sanskrit mantras spoken into flame, sesame seeds
an: Ram, horse (fire's speed)
co: Red, orange, gold, all colours of fire
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Sunday / Sun — fire's two planetary rulers
cr: Fire opal, carnelian, sunstone, garnet
bp: Digestive fire (Agni is literally the digestive fire in Ayurveda), solar plexus, metabolism
af: I transform what no longer serves me through sacred fire. My inner fire purifies and illuminates. I am witnessed by the divine.
sh: The fire that consumes rather than transforms — wildfires, fever, digestive disease, the anger that burns the one who carries it
ar: The Divine Witness / The Sacred Transformer
cb: Varuna (water, cosmic order, the ocean) — fire and water are the primal elemental polarity. Agni (fire, transformation, action) counterbalanced by Varuna (water, law, reflection, the deep ocean of cosmic order). Also counterbalanced by Soma (moon, coolness, the nectar of immortality).

do: The sun, consciousness, vitality, health, the eye that sees all, the twelve Adityas
at: The solar deity — the direct embodiment of the sun. Twelve aspects (the twelve Adityas) corresponding to the twelve months. He rides a golden chariot pulled by seven horses (the seven colours of light, the seven chakras). Surya Namaskar (Sun Salutation) is the most widely practised act of worship in the modern world — 400 million people greet him daily.
of: Surya Namaskar at sunrise, Gayatri Mantra, solar yantra, water offered to the sun at dawn, saffron, gold flowers
an: Horse (seven white horses pull his chariot), eagle, lotus
co: Gold, orange, bright yellow, saffron
dp: Sunday / Sun — the sun is his body
cr: Ruby, sunstone, yellow topaz, amber, chrysoberyl
bp: Solar plexus, eyes (the sun sees all), the bones, vitality and immune system
af: The sun within me never sets. I see clearly. My vitality is radiant. I greet this day as sacred.
sh: The unrelenting sun — the solar plexus that cannot rest, that must always shine, perform, produce
ar: The Radiant Sun / The Life-Giver
cb: Chandra (Moon) — sun and moon are the primary cosmic polarity of the Hindu system. Surya (solar, heat, light, masculine) counterbalanced by Chandra (lunar, cool, reflection, feminine). Together they govern the rhythm of all earthly life.

do: Thunder, lightning, justice, royal power, dance, drumming, masculine solar fire
at: The supreme Orisha of thunder — king, warrior, dancer, lover. His double-headed axe (Oshe) represents his power to both protect and destroy. He embodies royal fire: power that is beautiful, justice that is passionate, authority that dances. His cry 'Kawo Kabiyesi!' means 'Welcome, Your Majesty — none can challenge you.' The perfect expression of solar plexus energy.
of: Bata drumming, red and white cloth, apples, red wine, double-headed axe imagery, Saturday ceremonies, lightning water
an: Ram (his sacred animal), horse
co: Red and white (his sacred colours)
dp: Saturday / Jupiter — royal power and justice
cr: Garnet, red jasper, ruby, meteorite (lightning stone)
bp: Solar plexus, heart (royal seat), adrenal glands, the spine of sovereignty
af: Kawo Kabiyesi! My power is sovereign. I am the thunder that cannot be silenced. I dance in the storm of my own authority.
sh: Shango's shadow is royal arrogance — the king who abuses his power, the ego of authority without accountability
ar: The Thunderking / The Royal Fire
cb: Obatala — the supreme polarity in the Yoruba system. Shango (thunder, red, fire, passion, justice through force) counterbalanced by Obatala (peace, white, purity, patience, wisdom through stillness). Also Oya (the storm he made) counterbalances him — his own creative power becomes his balance.

do: Storm, wind, transformation, death, the marketplace, radical change
at: The Orisha of wind, storm, and transformation. She carries a sword and a crown of keys — she opens what is locked and cuts what must be severed. She stands at the doorway between the living and the dead. She teaches that personal power and radical change are the same thing. Her storms are not destruction — they are acceleration of necessary transformation.
of: Nine purple aubergines, copper and burgundy offerings, cemetery gate work, wind ceremonies, nine pennies at a crossroads
an: Buffalo (her original form), eagle, lightning bugs
co: Burgundy, dark purple, all rainbow colours (she contains them all)
dp: Wednesday / Uranus — revolutionary transformation
cr: Amethyst, dark garnet, labradorite, obsidian
bp: Lungs, the breath, the nervous system, the colon (the body's transformation organ)
af: I am the wind of necessary change. I cut what must be severed with love and precision. I transform.
sh: Oya's shadow is the storm that destroys for its own sake — the change-addict who cannot allow anything to be stable
ar: The Storm of Transformation / The Wind Queen
cb: Obatala (peace, stillness, white calm) directly counterbalances Oya's storm. Also Yemoja (the still ocean's surface) counterbalances Oya (the winds that churn it). And Oshun (the still river) counterbalances Oya (the storm above it).

do: Iron, war, labour, the forge, clearing paths, warrior discipline, primal will
at: The primordial deity of iron — the Orisha who first cleared a path through the primordial forest so the others could enter the world. He governs iron and all iron instruments: knives, axes, surgical scalpels, vehicles, weapons. He demands absolute discipline, absolute integrity, and absolute commitment to one's work.
of: Iron objects, palm oil poured on iron, rum, green and black altar cloth, cigars, machetes and knives
an: Dog (his companion on the warrior's road), snake, bird
co: Green and black
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the warrior's fire and discipline
cr: Iron meteorite, black tourmaline, magnetite, hematite
bp: Muscles, bones, the skeletal-muscular system, the surgeon's hands
af: I clear the path with discipline and precision. My will is iron. I work with sacred commitment.
sh: The warrior who turns his sword on himself or on those who love him — Ogun's shadow is violence without purpose, the forge that destroys
ar: The Iron Warrior / The Sacred Forge
cb: Oshun — iron and water. Ogun (iron, earth, the hard forge) counterbalanced by Oshun (sweet water, flow, love). The mythological reconciliation of Ogun and Oshun — she drew him from the forest with her honey — is one of the great sacred stories of the Yoruba tradition. Also Obatala (peace) counterbalances Ogun (war).

do: Creation, purity, patience, white solar light, wisdom, the sculpting of human form
at: The arch-deity of creation — Olodumare (the supreme) sent Obatala to sculpt the human form from clay. He is the father of all Orishas. His white robes represent pure consciousness before it becomes anything. He teaches that the highest form of personal power is absolute stillness and clarity. His patience is not weakness — it is the most refined form of strength.
of: White cloth, white food (coconut, rice, white yam), cool water, silver imagery, palm wine (he is forbidden alcohol — it was his drunkenness that caused him to create deformed humans)
an: White snail, white dove
co: White (and all shades of white)
dp: Sunday / Uranus — pure consciousness and creation
cr: White quartz, howlite, selenite, white moonstone
bp: Bones (he sculpts bones), the head (ori — personal consciousness), the crown
af: My highest power is absolute clarity. I create from stillness. I am the white light before it divides.
sh: Obatala's shadow is rigidity — the purity that becomes perfectionism, the stillness that becomes paralysis
ar: The Father Creator / The Pure Consciousness
cb: Shango — the supreme polarity in practical Yoruba devotion. Obatala (white, stillness, purity, patience) counterbalances Shango (red, storm, passion, fire). Also Oya (wind and storm) counterbalances Obatala (absolute stillness). And Ogun (iron, primal force) counterbalances Obatala (refined purity).

do: Personal destiny, the divine flame within, individual divine identity, the soul's purpose
at: The most intimate of all deities — not a being external to you but your own personal divine flame. Every person has a Chi that is uniquely theirs — their portion of the divine, their destined path, their inner god. Chineke (God creates through Chi). Chukwu (the great Chi, the supreme). Chi is the solar plexus's most personal face: the divine fire that is specifically, irreplaceably YOURS.
of: Prayer and honest self-examination, living in alignment with your true purpose, morning and evening acknowledgment of your Chi
an: Personal to each person's Chi
co: Personal — whatever colours call to your Chi
dp: Sunday — the day of personal solar power
cr: Your personal power stone — whatever resonates
bp: Solar plexus (seat of personal power), the ori (the head, personal consciousness)
af: My Chi is awake and alive. I walk in alignment with my divine purpose. I claim my unique fire.
sh: The Chi that is ignored — the unlived life, the purpose denied, the inner flame that flickers and nearly dies from disuse
ar: The Inner Flame / The Personal Divine
cb: Chukwu (the Great God, the supreme Chi) — the personal counterbalanced by the universal. My flame counterbalanced by the infinite fire. Also Ogbunabali (the night killer) counterbalances Chi (the life flame) — each human life is in perpetual polarity with its own death.

do: The sun, gold, abundance, agricultural prosperity, royal authority, the Sapa Inca
at: The father sun of the Inca civilisation — second only to Viracocha (the creator) in the Andean pantheon. His gold fills the earth. The Sapa Inca (emperor) was his direct son. The great Coricancha temple in Cusco was entirely covered in gold in his honour. He is abundance and authority as one — the solar plexus principle of personal power expressed through cosmic scale.
of: Gold offerings (no gold can leave the sun's domain), chicha (corn beer), white llamas, solar ceremonies at Inti Raymi (June solstice)
an: Condor (the messenger between the sun and earth), llama
co: Gold, brilliant yellow, orange
dp: Sunday / Sun — he is the sun
cr: Gold, sunstone, yellow sapphire, citrine, pyrite
bp: Solar plexus, eyes, the bones (gold is the sun's mineral in the body)
af: I am the sun's abundance made flesh. My authority comes from the highest source. I shine without apology.
sh: The sun-king's shadow is divine right turned to tyranny — the god-emperor who mistakes his office for his soul
ar: The Sun King / The Golden Father
cb: Mama Quilla (Moon Mother) — the supreme Andean polarity. Inti (sun, masculine, gold, the upper world) counterbalanced by Mama Quilla (moon, feminine, silver, the regulator of time and tides). Also Pachamama (earth mother) counterbalances Inti (sky father).

do: Volcanic fire, creation through destruction, lava, new land, the power of the earth's core
at: She who creates new land from destruction. Pele lives in the Kīlauea volcano and she IS the volcanic fire — the creative-destructive force that builds new earth from the planet's deepest core. She is not metaphorically fire — she is literal fire. Her lava creates new land that did not exist before. She is the solar plexus principle taken to its geological extreme: creation through fire.
of: Red flowers (especially red lehua ohia — she is believed to inhabit them), fire ceremonies, offerings at volcanic sites, red candles, sand from volcanic beaches
an: White dog (she often appears as an old woman with a white dog), hawk
co: Red, orange, black (cooled lava), gold
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Sunday / Sun — volcanic fire and solar creation
cr: Obsidian (volcanic glass), red garnet, lava stone, peridot (found in Hawaiian lava)
bp: Solar plexus (creative fire), adrenal glands, the spine (the volcanic channel)
af: I create new ground from my own fire. What I destroy makes way for what has never existed before. I am the fire at the centre of the earth.
sh: The unstoppable lava — the creative force that cannot be redirected once it flows, that burns everything regardless of value
ar: The Creative Destroyer / The Volcano Goddess
cb: Nāmaka (her sister, the sea goddess) — the great Polynesian elemental polarity. Pele (fire, lava, the land) counterbalanced by Nāmaka (ocean, the sea). Their eternal conflict — Nāmaka driving Pele from island to island — is the story of fire and water's eternal dance. Also Hi'iaka (her beloved youngest sister) counterbalances her fire with gentle healing.

do: The sun, sovereignty, light, weaving, the Japanese imperial line
at: The supreme deity of the Shinto pantheon — the sun goddess who rules the heavens. When her brother Susanoo shamed her, she retreated into a cave and the world fell into darkness — until the goddess Ame-no-Uzume danced so wildly and joyfully that the gods laughed, and Amaterasu's curiosity drew her back. She teaches the solar plexus lesson: the sovereign who chooses not to shine withdraws her light from the world.
of: Mirrors (the sacred mirror Yata no Kagami is one of Japan's three imperial treasures), white silk, silk weaving, solar offerings at dawn
an: White horse, rooster (announces the dawn)
co: Gold, white, brilliant yellow
dp: Sunday / Sun — she is the sun
cr: Sunstone, yellow jasper, amber, clear quartz
bp: Eyes (the sun that illuminates), solar plexus, the crown
af: I choose to shine. When I withdraw my light the world grows dark — my radiance is a gift I give freely.
sh: The great cave retreat — the solar plexus that shrinks from the world in wounded pride, withdrawing its gifts in response to shame
ar: The Sovereign Sun / The Goddess Who Chose to Shine
cb: Tsukuyomi (Moon God, her estranged husband) — sun and moon in perpetual polarity. They live apart because of a profound disagreement (Tsukuyomi killed the food goddess Ukemochi). Their separation is why day and night never meet. Also Susanoo (storm, her brother) counterbalances Amaterasu (solar order).

do: Fire, healing, smithcraft, poetry, the sacred feminine fire, the triple creative flame
at: Triple goddess of fire in three forms: the fire of the forge (smithcraft), the fire of the hearth (healing), and the fire of inspiration (poetry). Her sacred flame at Kildare burned continuously for over a thousand years tended by nineteen priestesses, one for each year of the Great Year. The early Christian church could not extinguish her fire — they made her a saint instead.
of: A candle or flame kept burning in her honour, bread and mead, herbs and medicines, poetry written or spoken aloud, Imbolc ceremonies (February 1st — her feast day)
an: Serpent (her sacred animal of healing), cow (abundance), oystercatcher (her bird)
co: Orange, gold, white, red
dp: Wednesday / Venus (creative feminine) — the forge, the poem, the healing
cr: Carnelian, fire opal, garnet, amber
el: Fire / Water (healing wells are sacred to her)
bp: Solar plexus (creative fire), hands (for smithcraft and healing), voice (poetry)
af: I tend the sacred fire of my creativity. I forge with purpose. My healing comes from within.
sh: The fire that goes out — the sacred flame of creativity abandoned, the poet silenced, the healer who cannot heal herself
ar: The Triple Creative / The Eternal Flame
cb: The Cailleach (the winter hag, goddess of cold and storms) — Brigid (warmth, spring, creative fire) counterbalanced by the Cailleach (cold, winter, stripping away). The Celtic year is governed by their eternal alternation. Also counterbalanced by the Dagda (her father, cosmic abundance) and Morrigan (death, war, the dark feminine).

do: Solar fire, prophecy, music, truth, healing, beauty, poetry, the arts
at: Solar fire expressed as beauty, truth and creative brilliance. God of the sun (though originally a sun-aspect alongside Helios), of music, poetry, medicine, archery and prophecy. The Oracle at Delphi spoke his truth. His twin is Artemis (the moon). He represents the solar plexus elevated to its highest frequency — fire as illumination, as truth-telling, as creative mastery.
of: Bay laurel, lyre, sunstone and amber, honey, frankincense, dawn and noon ceremonies, music and poetry offered in his honour
an: Raven/crow (his sacred messenger), dolphin, wolf, swan
co: Gold, white, silver
dp: Sunday / Sun — the radiant sun
cr: Sunstone, amber, clear quartz, labradorite
bp: Solar plexus, eyes, the voice (prophecy and music), the hands (for the lyre)
af: I speak truth. My creativity is illumined by solar fire. I see clearly and I share what I see with beauty.
sh: Apollo's shadow is the ruthless perfection of the sun — the creative tyrant, the truth-speaker who destroys rather than illuminates
ar: The Solar Artist / The Truth-Speaker
cb: Dionysus — the supreme Greek polarity. Apollo (solar order, reason, form, sunlight) counterbalanced by Dionysus (ecstasy, dissolution, wine, darkness). The two gods actually shared Delphi — Apollo ruled it in summer, Dionysus in winter. Their polarity IS Greek civilisation's creative tension. Also counterbalanced by Artemis (his moon twin).

do: Protection, truth, the sword of divine will, spiritual warfare, solar fire of righteousness
at: The warrior archangel — commander of the heavenly armies, slayer of the dragon/serpent, protector of the faithful. His name means 'Who is like God?' — the rhetorical question that asserts no one can match the divine. He carries a sword or a scale. He is the solar plexus principle in its most purely protective form: the boundary-keeper, the truth-defender.
of: Blue and gold candles, prayer and invocation, sword imagery, midday prayer in sunlight, frankincense
an: Eagle, lion (the four archangels each have a face — Michael's is the lion)
co: Blue, gold, deep cobalt
dp: Sunday / Sun — the warrior of divine light
cr: Blue lace agate, blue kyanite, sugilite, lapilazuli
bp: Solar plexus (protection of personal power), the throat (truth-speaking), the right arm (sword arm)
af: I am protected by divine will. I cut through illusion with the sword of truth. My power serves the highest good.
sh: The divine warrior's shadow is spiritual militarism — righteous certainty that becomes persecution, the sword that doesn't know when to be sheathed
ar: The Holy Warrior / The Defender
cb: Archangel Raphael (healing, peace, the physician) counterbalances Michael (warrior, protection, the sword). The sword and the healing staff. Also Gabriel (the message-bearer) counterbalances Michael (the action-taker). In some traditions, Lucifer (the lightbearer who fell) is Michael's supreme counterbalance.

do: Unconditional love, devotion, the capacity to reassemble what is broken, divine grief transformed to power
at: Isis at the heart chakra — her most poignant and powerful face. She searched the entire world for the pieces of her murdered husband Osiris, found all but one (the phallus, eaten by a fish), and reassembled him with the power of her love. She breathed life back into the dead. This is the heart chakra's supreme demonstration: love that literally resurrects.
of: Blue lotus, myrrh, turquoise, blue cloth, winged imagery, love letters offered to the flame
an: Kite (her mourning cry), cow, swallow
co: Blue, gold, green
dp: Friday / Moon — the devoted mother and wife
cr: Moonstone, turquoise, malachite, emerald
bp: Heart, lungs (the breath that resurrects), the wings of the shoulder blades
af: My love is powerful enough to reassemble what has been broken. I do not abandon those I love.
sh: The grief that becomes obsession — the love that will not release even what cannot be resurrected
ar: The Devoted Heart / The Love That Resurrects
cb: Nephthys — the two sisters hold the space of death and life, grief and love. Isis (life-bringing love) counterbalanced by Nephthys (death-accompanying love). Together they attend all transitions.

do: Unconditional maternal love, emotional healing, the heart's capacity to hold all feeling
at: Yemoja at the heart chakra — the great mother whose love is so vast it encompasses the entire ocean. She teaches the heart chakra that true love is not about possession or preference but about the capacity of the ocean to receive every river without refusing any.
of: Blue and white flowers, watermelon, offerings at the ocean at full moon
an: Fish, sea turtle
co: Blue and white
dp: Monday / Moon
cr: Moonstone, aquamarine, blue topaz
bp: Heart, breasts, lungs
af: My love is as vast as the ocean. I hold space for every feeling. I love without possession.
sh: Suffocating love — the ocean that drowns instead of nourishing
ar: The Ocean Heart / The Maternal Love
cb: Ogun (iron, hard boundaries, the warrior path) — the boundless love counterbalanced by the one who knows when to forge a boundary.

do: Compassion, swift action for liberation, the mother who protects all beings
at: Green Tara — the Bodhisattva who vowed to achieve enlightenment in female form and to remain in the world until all beings are liberated. She acts swiftly in compassion. She is born from Avalokiteshvara's tears of compassion for all suffering beings. The heart that acts without hesitation.
of: Green candles, green foods, mantras (Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha), white lotus, turquoise
an: Lotus (she sits on it), the animals she protects
co: Green, gold
dp: Friday / Venus — compassion and beauty
cr: Emerald, green tourmaline, jade, malachite
bp: Heart, thymus gland, the immune system of love
af: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha. I act swiftly from compassion. I am the green light of the heart.
sh: Compassion fatigue — the bodhisattva who burns out; the heart that cares for all while neglecting itself
ar: The Compassionate Action / The Swift Heart
cb: White Tara (longevity, peaceful compassion) — swift action counterbalanced by patient endurance. Also Vajrayogini (fierce red dakini) counterbalances Green Tara's compassion with wrathful liberation.

do: Mercy, compassion, the hearing of all cries, the sacred feminine principle of liberation
at: She Who Hears the Cries of the World. Originally male (Avalokiteshvara), transformed in Chinese Buddhism into the supreme expression of the compassionate feminine. She stands on a lotus, pouring the water of compassion from her vase. She refused final liberation until the last being was free.
of: White lotus flowers, incense, clean water in a vessel, vegetarian foods, white candles, jade
an: Dragon (wisdom), white crane, willow (her tree),
co: White, pink, pale green
dp: Friday / Moon — the compassionate feminine
cr: Rose quartz, clear quartz, jade, white jade
bp: Heart, hands (her thousand arms of compassion), ears (she hears all cries)
af: I hear the suffering around me and I answer with compassion. My mercy is without limit. I am the sound of one heart hearing another.
sh: The compassion that enables suffering — the mercy that rescues people from the consequences that would grow them
ar: The Mercy Goddess / She Who Hears
cb: Manjushri (the Bodhisattva of Wisdom, who cuts through illusion with his sword) — compassion counterbalanced by wisdom. The sword of discernment balancing the ocean of mercy.

do: The love that transcends the physical — Eros as cosmic force, love as the binding principle of the cosmos
at: Aphrodite's heart chakra face — not the sensuality of the sacral but the deeper cosmic Eros: love as the principle that holds the cosmos together. Plato distinguished between Aphrodite Pandemos (earthly love) and Aphrodite Ourania (heavenly love). The heart chakra is her Ourania face.
of: Pink roses, myrtle, the dedication of one's love to the highest
an: Dove
co: Pink, rose gold
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline, rhodochrosite
bp: Heart, lungs, arms (the embrace)
af: Love is the force that holds the cosmos together. I am part of that force. I love freely and fully.
sh: The beautiful face of love that conceals possessiveness — the heart that loves only when it feels loved in return
ar: The Cosmic Love / The Heavenly Eros
cb: Ares (war, the force that divides) — love counterbalanced by war. They are lovers precisely because they are opposites. Also Artemis (solitary, self-sufficient, the unloved love) counterbalances Aphrodite (the fully loving and loved).

do: Marriage, motherhood, fate, grief, wisdom kept secret, love that endures loss
at: The queen of Asgard, wife of Odin — she sits at her loom weaving fate but she never tells what she knows. Her grief for Baldr (her son) is the most moving in Norse mythology — she went to every creature in the nine worlds asking them to swear not to harm him. She knew his fate and she loved him anyway.
of: Linen and wool offerings, candles, hearth-keeping, prayers for marriages and children, silver
an: Hawk, heron, ram
co: Deep blue, silver, white
dp: Friday (Frigg's Day) / Venus — the day is named for her
cr: Moonstone, silver, pearl, aquamarine
bp: Heart, womb (as the holder of fate), hands (as weaver of destiny)
af: I love knowing the full weight of what love costs. My heart is strong enough to hold both joy and grief.
sh: The keeper of secrets — the grief that cannot be shared, the knowing that isolates
ar: The Sovereign Queen / The Grieving Mother
cb: Odin (the wanderer, seeker of wisdom through sacrifice) — Frigg (who keeps wisdom and weeps) counterbalanced by Odin (who seeks wisdom and suffers). Both know fate; both endure it differently.

do: The love that is never enough — the wound of the heart that longs for more love than the world can give
at: Erzulie Freda weeps at the end of every ceremony. She is the heart that loves so much that the world's love will never be sufficient. She teaches the heart chakra's deepest wound and deepest gift: the aching capacity to love more than you will ever receive, and to love anyway.
of: Pink and gold candles, sweet champagne, roses, mirrors, perfume
an: None
co: Pink, gold
dp: Thursday / Venus
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline
bp: Heart, tear ducts, the throat that holds the sob
af: My capacity to love is not a wound — it is my greatest power. I love even knowing the cost.
sh: The love that consumes — the romantic grief that becomes the only identity
ar: The Sacred Romantic / The Infinite Heart
cb: Baron Samedi (death, the end of all love, the laughter of the grave) — the love that never ends counterbalanced by death that ends all things. Their polarity contains the whole of existence.

do: Self-love, the love that flows as naturally as a river, the heart's sweetness
at: Oshun at the heart chakra — the principle that love must first flow inward before it can flow outward. Her river runs because she is full. She teaches that self-love is not selfish — it is the source from which all other love is possible.
of: Honey offered to oneself, gold, yellow flowers, self-care rituals
an: Peacock, bee
co: Gold, yellow
dp: Saturday / Venus
cr: Amber, citrine, rose quartz
bp: Heart, kidneys, the sweetness in the blood
af: I am sweet to myself first. Love flows through me as naturally as a river. I am worthy.
sh: The self-love that becomes self-obsession — the narcissism that is love turned entirely inward
ar: The Self-Love / The Sweet River
cb: Yemoja (the mother's love, love directed outward to all) — self-love counterbalanced by universal love.

do: Love, beauty, flowers, art, sexuality, weaving, the heart's creative pleasures
at: Goddess of love and beauty — her name means 'Precious Feather Flower.' She governs the pleasures of the heart: romantic love, artistic creation, the sensory beauty that the heart delights in. She is the Aztec Venus — associated with the planet Venus and with all the heart-opening pleasures of existence.
of: Flowers (marigolds, roses), butterflies, chocolate, gold, beautiful fabric, weaving, art-making as prayer
an: Hummingbird, butterfly, quetzal bird
co: Red, orange, gold, vivid colours of tropical flowers
dp: Friday / Venus — beauty and love
cr: Jade, rose quartz, carnelian, turquoise
bp: Heart, womb, hands (for creating beauty), the senses
af: I create beauty as a form of prayer. My heart opens to what is beautiful. Love is sacred.
sh: Tlazolteotl — the dark goddess of filth who swallows sin, Xochiquetzal's shadow self. The beauty that conceals decay.
ar: The Flower Heart / The Sacred Artist
cb: Tlazolteotl (filth, purging, the shadow of beauty) — the flower goddess counterbalanced by the purger. Beauty counterbalanced by the necessity of purification.

do: Devoted love, the feminine principle of devotion, the love that transforms through constancy
at: The devoted wife of Shiva — she is the model of the heart that loves through all seasons. She waited for him, she meditated to win him, she transformed the universe with her love. She is Shakti in her most tender form. The heart that loves consistently and deeply, not through passion alone but through devotion.
of: Red and pink flowers, bilva leaves, sandalwood, ghee, prayers for love and marriage
an: Lion, tiger
co: Pink, red, gold
dp: Friday / Venus — devotion and love
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline, moonstone, pink sapphire
bp: Heart, womb, the soft tissues of the body
af: My devotion is my power. I love through all seasons. My heart is both tender and unbreakable.
sh: The martyr — the heart that loves so devotedly it loses itself in the other
ar: The Devoted Heart / The Transformative Love
cb: Shiva (the self-sufficient ascetic who must be wooed into love) — the one who loves and the one who must be taught to love. Their cosmic partnership is the supreme model of heart chakra completion.

do: Writing, language, magic, the moon, divine record-keeping, divine speech
at: The divine scribe — god of writing, language, magic and the moon. He invented hieroglyphs. He recorded the weighing of the heart ceremony. His words created reality (Heka). He is the principle that language is a sacred and magical act — that what is spoken becomes real. The throat chakra's supreme deity.
of: Pens and written offerings (burn them in ceremony), ibis feathers, lunar ceremonies, books and scrolls
an: Ibis (he is depicted with an ibis head), baboon
co: Silver, blue, white, lunar colours
dp: Wednesday / Mercury and Monday / Moon — writing and the moon's wisdom
cr: Lapis lazuli, blue kyanite, celestite, sodalite
bp: Throat, tongue, vocal cords, brain, the written hand
af: My words carry the weight of sacred truth. Every word I speak creates my reality. I write the world into being.
sh: The cosmic accountant's shadow — the one who keeps records but never acts, who knows everything but lives nothing
ar: The Divine Scribe / The Magic of Language
cb: Sekhmet / Ra — truth spoken (Thoth) must be balanced by truth enacted (Ra's solar power). Also Ma'at counterbalances Thoth — her feather is what he weighs against the heart. Truth-recording (Thoth) balanced by truth-as-cosmic-principle (Ma'at).

do: Truth, justice, cosmic order, balance, the right way of things
at: The goddess of truth and cosmic order — her feather weighs against the heart at death. If the heart is heavier than her feather, it is consumed by Ammit. She is the principle that keeps the cosmos functioning. She is not a goddess of laws — she is the principle of the right order of things, the cosmic harmony that makes existence possible.
of: A white feather, scales, offerings of truth — a commitment to honesty in all things
an: Ostrich (her feather is an ostrich feather)
co: White, gold, turquoise
dp: Sunday / Saturn — cosmic order and the balance of all things
cr: Blue kyanite (perfect alignment, never needs cleansing), clear quartz, white calcite
bp: Throat (truth-speaking), heart (the organ weighed against her), the spine (the Djed of uprightness)
af: I speak truth because the cosmos requires it. My heart is lighter than the feather of Ma'at. I live in right order.
sh: The cosmic weight of untruth — the heart that has accumulated the lead of lies, performance and self-deception
ar: The Truth Itself / The Cosmic Order
cb: Isfet (chaos, the principle of disorder) — Ma'at (order, truth, the right way) counterbalanced by Isfet (disorder, lies, the wrong way). Also Thoth (who records truth) counterbalances Ma'at (who IS truth). Set counterbalances Ma'at in the cosmic sense.

do: Sacred speech, the Vedic word, mantra, the divine sound that creates
at: Saraswati at the throat chakra — the principle of Vak (sacred speech). In Vedic understanding, the spoken word is a creative act — sound precedes form. The Vedas were preserved through oral tradition precisely because the spoken word carries spiritual power that the written word does not. She teaches that the voice is a sacred instrument.
of: White flowers, musical instruments played in her honour, mantras recited correctly and with devotion
an: Swan, peacock
co: White, pale blue
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the sacred word
cr: Blue lace agate, aquamarine, white opal
bp: Throat, tongue, vocal cords, ears
af: My voice is sacred. I speak the truth that wants to speak through me. Sound is my prayer.
sh: The voice silenced by fear — the goddess whose music goes unplayed, whose river runs underground
ar: The Sacred Voice / The Sound of Creation
cb: Kali (who silences the ego's words) counterbalances Saraswati (who gives the divine word voice). The silence that precedes the sacred word counterbalanced by the sacred word itself.

do: The oath, the sword of truth, the word as binding commitment
at: Ogun at the throat — he is the Orisha upon whom oaths are sworn in Yoruba courts of law. Iron is placed on the tongue as an oath is taken. He is the principle that words must match actions — that speech is a form of covenant. In this aspect he governs the throat chakra's most demanding lesson: speaking only what you will enact.
of: Iron placed on the tongue during oath-taking, palm wine, honest commitment spoken aloud
an: Dog
co: Green and black
dp: Tuesday / Mars
cr: Hematite, magnetite, iron
bp: Tongue, throat, the hands that forge what the mouth promises
af: I speak only what I will do. My word is iron. My oath is sacred.
sh: The oath-breaker — in Ogun's world, this is the gravest of all failures
ar: The Sacred Oath / The Word as Iron
cb: Oshun (the soft word of love) counterbalances Ogun (the hard word of commitment). Sweet water and iron.

do: Communication, language, travel, trade, boundaries, messages between worlds, magic
at: The divine messenger — the only Olympian who can freely travel between the worlds of gods, humans and the dead. He is the principle of communication, of crossing boundaries, of words that carry meaning across all distances. He invented language, writing, music (the lyre), and is the patron of all who deal in words, knowledge, and commerce.
of: Offerings at crossroads, coins, the caduceus symbol, eloquence offered in speech or writing, travel charms
an: Ram, tortoise (his lyre was made from a tortoise shell), rooster, hare
co: Yellow, orange, bright blue
dp: Wednesday (Mercury's day) / Mercury — the planet of communication
cr: Labradorite, blue agate, chrysoprase, citrine
bp: Hands, tongue, feet (his winged sandals), nervous system
af: My communication crosses all boundaries. I carry messages between worlds. My words land with precision.
sh: Hermes Trickster — the word used to deceive, the message distorted, the merchant who lies, the crossroads that misleads
ar: The Messenger / The Boundary Crosser
cb: Hestia (the fixed centre, the hearth that never moves) counterbalances Hermes (the wanderer who is always moving). The stillness counterbalancing the movement. Also Apollo (truth) counterbalances Hermes (the tricky use of words).

do: Stories, wisdom through trickery, the cunning word, the tale that teaches
at: The spider god of stories — perhaps the most widely known African deity in the diaspora, carried through slavery to the Caribbean and Americas. He stole all the stories from the Sky God and gave them to humanity. He teaches that wisdom is not always found through the obvious path but through the cunning re-framing of what is possible. The throat chakra's trickster face.
of: Stories told in his honour, spiders never harmed in the home, offerings of grain and sweet things, the telling of tales that teach
an: Spider (he is the spider)
co: Gold and black (the spider's colours)
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the trickster's wit
cr: Obsidian (the spider's dark wisdom), amber (the trap that preserves)
bp: Tongue, brain, the cunning mind, the hands that weave webs
af: I weave my story with wisdom and wit. I find the unexpected path. My tongue is my most powerful tool.
sh: The trickster who goes too far — the cunning that becomes deception, the story that misleads rather than teaches
ar: The Storyteller / The Divine Trickster
cb: Nyame (the Sky God, the solemn, the law-giver) counterbalances Anansi (the trickster, the story-thief). Order counterbalanced by wit. The solemn truth counterbalanced by the comic truth.

do: Wisdom, divination, the sacred word of fate, the witness to all destinies
at: The Orisha of wisdom and divination — he was present at the creation of all human destinies. He does not change fate but he reveals it, and through revelation, shows the paths to navigate it. Ifa divination is his sacred system — the most sophisticated divination tradition in the world, with 256 odu (signs) each containing stories, remedies and wisdom.
of: Palm nuts, the Ifa oracle (consulted through a diviner/babalawo), kola nuts, wisdom offered in his honour
an: Parrot, chameleon
co: Green and yellow
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — wisdom and the expansion of understanding
cr: Green jade, yellow citrine, amber
