bp: Tongue (the oracle's speech), the third eye (what Ifa sees), the hands of the diviner
af: The truth of my path is being revealed to me. I listen to the wisdom that speaks through all things.
sh: The misuse of prophetic knowledge — the diviner who manipulates fate rather than illuminating it
ar: The Oracle / The Keeper of Wisdom
cb: Eshu/Elegua (the trickster, the disruptor of fate) counterbalances Orunmila (the revealer of fate). Wisdom about destiny (Orunmila) counterbalanced by the radical freedom from destiny (Eshu). The cosmic interplay of destiny and free will.

do: Crossroads, communication, beginnings, the opening of paths, the divine trickster, the gatekeeper
at: The first and last Orisha — nothing can happen without his permission. He opens and closes all paths. He stands at every crossroads. He is the child and the old man simultaneously. In Vodou he is Papa Legba, the gatekeeper without whom no other Lwa can be reached. He tests the will and the wit of all who seek his passage. He must be honoured first before any other spiritual work.
of: Rum and cigars, candy, coconut, red and black offerings, Monday morning crossroads ceremonies, a small offering left at every crossroads before undertaking any significant journey
an: Dog (his companion), rooster
co: Red and black
dp: Monday / Mercury — the opener of all paths
cr: Red jasper, black tourmaline, obsidian
bp: Feet (the crossroads walker), tongue (the trickster's speech), the base of the spine (where all journeys begin)
af: I ask permission to walk my path with grace. I open doors with integrity. All crossroads lead to wisdom.
sh: The path that is blocked — the trickster who closes all doors, the test that fails, the gateway that will not open
ar: The Gatekeeper / The Divine Crossroads
cb: Obatala (order, the white light of pure creation) counterbalances Eshu (chaos, trickery, the play at the crossroads). Also Orunmila (wisdom, fate) counterbalances Eshu (freedom, disruption). The gate counterbalanced by the path itself.

do: The sacred word, divine speech, the creative power of language, the first sound
at: THE FORGOTTEN GODDESS OF SACRED SPEECH. Vac is the Vedic goddess of divine speech — older than Saraswati and often overlooked. In the Rig Veda she describes herself as the one through whom the gods speak, the one who pervades all the worlds, who supports heaven and earth with her own power. She is the creative word that spoke the cosmos into being. She was gradually replaced by Saraswati and then largely erased.
of: The sacred spoken word, mantras, silence before speech (honouring the space from which all words arise), offerings of the voice
an: None recorded — she IS the sound
co: Golden, the colour of sound made visible
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the sacred word
cr: Blue lace agate, celestite, blue kyanite
bp: Throat, tongue, all the organs of speech
af: My voice is the voice of the universe speaking itself. I am Vac — I am the sacred word.
sh: The silenced cosmic voice — the word that was never spoken, the truth that was never said
ar: The First Word / The Cosmic Speech
cb: Silence (the void from which all sound arises) — Vac (the word) counterbalanced by the infinite silence from which she emerges. Also Shiva (the still witness) counterbalances Vac (the creative speech).

do: The eye that sees all, divine vision, kingship, the horizon, the falcon's gaze
at: The falcon-headed god whose right eye is the sun and left eye is the moon. The Eye of Horus is the most powerful symbol of spiritual protection and divine vision in the ancient world. He lost his eye in battle with Set and it was restored by Thoth — the myth of the third eye that is destroyed by conflict and restored by wisdom.
of: The Eye of Horus symbol, blue and gold candles, falcon imagery, clear quartz, dawn ceremonies
an: Falcon (he is the falcon)
co: Gold, blue, sky blue
dp: Sunday / Sun — the all-seeing solar eye
cr: Lapis lazuli, tiger's eye (the falcon's gaze), clear quartz
bp: Eyes (both physical), the third eye/pineal gland, the visual cortex
af: I see clearly with both physical and spiritual sight. My perception cannot be permanently blinded. What was taken is restored.
sh: The blind spot — what the all-seeing eye refuses to see, the divine vision that has a shadow it cannot illuminate
ar: The All-Seeing Eye / The Divine Vision
cb: Set (who blinded the eye) counterbalances Horus (who sees all). Darkness and sight. The wound and the restoration. Also Thoth (who restored the eye) is the harmonising third principle.

do: The crossroads, witchcraft, the moon (especially dark moon), the underworld threshold, psychic vision
at: One of the oldest goddesses — predating the Olympian pantheon. She holds three torches at the triple crossroads, illuminating what lies in all three directions simultaneously. She is the original psychic goddess, the crone of true seeing, the guide at the threshold between the worlds. She was deliberately marginalised as patriarchal Greek religion developed.
of: Offerings left at crossroads at new moon (left and walked away from without looking back), garlic, black candles, dog food (dogs are sacred to her), eggs, honey
an: Dog, serpent, black cat, polecat
co: Black, silver, deep indigo
dp: Monday (dark moon) / Pluto — the vision that sees in darkness
cr: Black moonstone, obsidian, black tourmaline, dark amethyst
bp: Third eye, pineal gland, the peripheral vision (what we see at the edge of darkness)
af: I see in all directions simultaneously. I am not afraid of what the darkness illuminates. I stand at the crossroads with my torches raised.
sh: The witch's shadow is madness — the third eye that sees too much without the grounding to process it
ar: The Triple Torch / The Crone of Seeing
cb: Artemis (the bright huntress, the clean clear vision of the full moon) counterbalances Hecate (the dark moon's chthonic sight). Also Zeus/Apollo (solar clarity) counterbalance Hecate (lunar mystery). The light that illuminates versus the darkness that reveals.

do: Thought, creative vision, divine craftsmanship, the mind that creates
at: The god of craftsmen and architects who created the world through the power of divine thought (the heart) and divine speech (the tongue). He conceived the world in his mind and spoke it into being — the Egyptian philosophy of Memphite theology. He stands wrapped in white (the limitation that allows precision) holding the Was sceptre (power), Djed (stability) and Ankh (life).
of: Sacred craft made in his honour, tools of one's craft offered to him, blue-green faience (his sacred material), turquoise
an: Bull (the sacred Apis bull was his manifestation), scarab
co: Blue-green, white, black (the colour of fertile Nile soil — creativity)
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the creative mind
cr: Turquoise, lapis lazuli, faience blue, sodalite
bp: Brain, third eye, tongue (creation through thought and word combined)
af: I conceive my world in my mind before it exists in matter. My vision creates my reality. I am a divine craftsman.
sh: The thought that never becomes action — the architect who designs but never builds, the vision imprisoned in the mind
ar: The Divine Craftsman / The Creative Thought
cb: Sekhmet (his consort — destructive force) counterbalances Ptah (constructive vision). Creation and destruction. The mind's vision always counterbalanced by the fire that destroys what is built to make room for the next creation.

do: Wisdom, the divine feminine principle of gnosis, the fallen light seeking return, the world soul
at: THE MOST DRAMATICALLY ERASED GODDESS OF THE WESTERN WORLD. Sophia (Wisdom) was the divine feminine principle in early Christian and Gnostic thought — the wisdom of God, his creative partner, the world soul. She appears in the Book of Proverbs as God's companion at creation. In Gnostic cosmology she fell from the pleroma (fullness of light) and her fall created the material world. She was systematically excluded from the emerging Catholic canon. She must be restored.
of: White candles, doves, the pursuit of genuine gnosis (direct spiritual knowledge), offerings of the mind's devotion to wisdom
an: Dove (she became the Holy Spirit in some traditions — the dove of Pentecost was Sophia), owl
co: Gold, white, deep blue, violet
dp: Wednesday / Jupiter — divine wisdom
cr: Sapphire, lapis lazuli, clear quartz, violet amethyst
bp: Third eye, pineal gland, the entire brain as the seat of gnosis
af: I seek direct knowledge of the divine. Wisdom lives in me. I am the fallen light seeking return. I am Sophia restored.
sh: The Sophia who fell without returning — the wisdom that descended into matter and forgot its own divine origin
ar: The Fallen and Rising Wisdom / The World Soul
cb: Yaldabaoth / the Demiurge (the false creator who trapped the divine light in matter) counterbalances Sophia (the divine wisdom who seeks to liberate it). The supreme Gnostic polarity — wisdom against false power. Also the Pleroma (the fullness of light from which she fell) counterbalances her exile in matter.

do: The preserver, cosmic order, divine discernment, the avatar who appears in every age to restore righteousness
at: The great preserver of the cosmos — while Brahma creates and Shiva destroys, Vishnu maintains. He has ten primary avatars (Dashavatara) and appears in each age of the world to restore dharma (cosmic order). He holds the conch (divine sound), discus (the wheel of time), lotus (creative beauty) and mace (the force of knowledge). He is the principle of divine discernment — the third eye that sees what must be preserved and what must be released.
of: Tulsi (holy basil — the most sacred plant to Vishnu), lotus flowers, blue and yellow offerings, ghee, milk, chanting of the thousand names (Vishnu Sahasranama)
an: Eagle (Garuda is his mount — the vehicle of pure vision), snake (Shesha supports him in the cosmic ocean)
co: Blue (the colour of the infinite sky and ocean — his skin is blue), gold, yellow
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the preserver and the dharma-restorer
cr: Blue sapphire, lapis lazuli, yellow citrine, gold topaz
el: Ether / Water (he reclines on the cosmic ocean)
bp: Third eye (divine discernment), the entire body (he preserves the body of the cosmos)
af: I preserve what is righteous. I appear in the form the world needs. My discernment serves the highest good.
sh: The preserver who cannot let go — the cosmic maintainer who holds on past the time for release, refusing to allow Shiva's necessary destruction
ar: The Preserver / The Divine Discerner
cb: Shiva (the destroyer) — Vishnu and Shiva are the supreme divine polarity of the Hindu tradition. Vishnu (preserve, maintain, protect) counterbalanced by Shiva (destroy, dissolve, transform). Together they form the cosmic cycle with Brahma (creator). Also Lakshmi (his consort — abundance and beauty) counterbalances his cosmic detachment.

do: The seeing of destiny, the oracle's vision, the wisdom that knows what the future holds
at: Orunmila at the third eye — the profound quality of spiritual sight that Ifa represents. He was present at the creation of all destinies and can therefore see what is hidden from ordinary perception. He does not foretell doom — he illuminates the path so that wisdom can navigate what is coming.
of: The Ifa oracle consulted, palm nuts, devotion to wisdom
an: Parrot
co: Green and yellow
dp: Thursday / Jupiter
cr: Jade, citrine
bp: Third eye, pineal gland
af: I see clearly. My oracle is precise. What is coming is visible to me and I navigate it with wisdom.
sh: The seer who cannot unsee — the burden of prophetic sight
ar: The Prophetic Vision / The Destiny-Seer
cb: Eshu (the disruptor of fate) counterbalances Orunmila (the revealer of fate).

do: Cosmic knowledge, the divine eye of the universe, primordial wisdom, the word that preceded the world
at: THE DOGON'S GREAT SECRET. The Nommo are the divine ancestors of the Dogon people of Mali — aquatic beings of cosmic intelligence who are said to have brought advanced astronomical knowledge to humanity. The Dogon's knowledge of the Sirius star system (including the existence of Sirius B — a white dwarf invisible to the naked eye) was not confirmed by Western astronomy until 1970. The Nommo represent a tradition of third-eye knowledge so advanced it defies conventional explanation.
of: Water (they are water beings), white offerings, grain, sacred Dogon ceremonies
an: Fish (they are described as fish-like beings), the monitor lizard
co: Blue-black, silver, cosmic indigo
dp: Monday / Sirius (their home star) — beyond conventional planetary correspondence
cr: Lapis lazuli, meteorite, dark sapphire
bp: Third eye, pineal gland, the entire nervous system as cosmic receiver
af: My consciousness reaches beyond the stars. I am the receiver of cosmic wisdom. I was built for knowledge beyond the ordinary.
sh: The knowledge that isolates — the cosmic sight that makes the one who holds it incomprehensible to others
ar: The Cosmic Knowers / The Star Teachers
cb: Amma (the Dogon creator god, the supreme masculine principle) counterbalances the Nommo (the cosmic wisdom bringers). The source counterbalanced by those sent to illuminate.

do: The divine mother of knowledge, the wisdom that flows like rivers, the Tuatha Dé Danann
at: The mother of the Tuatha Dé Danann (the People of the Goddess Danu) — the divine race of Ireland whose wisdom and magic was so great that when the Milesians (human ancestors of the Irish) defeated them, they retreated underground to become the fairy folk. Danu herself is barely mentioned in surviving texts — her importance was systematically erased. She may be the same as Don in Welsh mythology.
of: River water, offerings at sacred springs and rivers, wisdom pursued and honoured, ancestral reverence
an: None recorded — she is the flowing source itself
co: Deep blue, silver, forest green
dp: Monday / Neptune — the flowing source of wisdom
cr: Moonstone, aquamarine, blue labradorite, green agate
bp: Third eye, the pineal gland, the ancestral memory in the DNA
af: I carry the wisdom of my ancestors. The divine knowledge flows through my blood. I am the child of the goddess of wisdom.
sh: The wisdom that was driven underground — the Tuatha Dé going into the fairy mounds; the knowledge that retreats when not honoured
ar: The Source of All Wisdom / The Divine Mother
cb: The Dagda (her consort/counterpart — abundance, the cosmic cauldron) counterbalances Danu (the flowing wisdom). Also the Morrigan (dark sovereignty) counterbalances Danu (the light of wisdom).

do: Wisdom, water, magic, the abzu (deep sweet water), crafts, mischief, the protector of humanity
at: The god of wisdom and the sweet underground waters — he is the trickster-sage of Sumerian cosmology. He consistently outwits the other gods and gods to protect humanity. He warned Utnapishtim (the Sumerian Noah) of the flood. He created humans from clay and the blood of a slain god. He is associated with the abzu — the underground ocean of wisdom beneath the earth.
of: Water, blue and green offerings, wisdom sought and applied, craft made with skill and intention
an: Goat-fish (his sacred symbol), turtle
co: Blue, green, the colour of fresh water
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — wisdom and craft
cr: Lapis lazuli, green tourmaline, aquamarine
bp: Third eye, hands (crafts), the creative brain
af: Wisdom is water — it flows to the lowest places. I find the cleverest path. I protect what I love with my wits.
sh: The trickster who goes too far — Enki's cleverness sometimes creates as many problems as it solves
ar: The Wise Trickster / The Craftsman God
cb: Enlil (the harsh sky god, rigid law and force) counterbalances Enki (clever water wisdom and compassion). Law counterbalanced by wit. Force counterbalanced by flow.

do: The supreme, the source of all, the breath of life, that which is beyond all names
at: The supreme being of the Yoruba tradition — beyond all the Orishas, beyond all names and forms. Olorun means 'Owner of Heaven.' He does not involve himself in human affairs directly — the Orishas are his intermediaries. He is pure being, pure consciousness, the source from which all else flows. He represents the crown chakra's ultimate truth: the divine principle that is beyond all personality.
of: Silence, meditation, the living of a righteous life — Olodumare requires no specific ritual because he is the ground of all ritual
an: None — he is beyond animal correspondence
co: Pure white, infinite light
dp: Sunday / The Central Sun beyond our sun
cr: Clear quartz (the crown), diamond, selenite
bp: The crown of the head, the entire being
af: I am a temporary expression of the eternal. I bow to what is beyond me. I am held in the infinite.
sh: The distant god — the shadow of Olodumare is the sense of divine abandonment, the god who is so supreme he cannot be reached
ar: The Supreme Source / The Nameless One
cb: Eshu/Elegua (the immediate, the present, the crossroads) counterbalances Olodumare (the eternal, the remote, the infinite). The universal counterbalanced by the immediate. Also the individual Ori (personal consciousness) counterbalances Olodumare (the universal consciousness).

do: The hidden one, the cosmic wind, the supreme deity, the invisible breath of creation
at: Amun means 'the Hidden One' — the unknowable aspect of the divine that precedes all manifestation. He was the state god of Egypt during its greatest period of power. Combined with Ra (Amun-Ra) he became the supreme deity who is both the hidden source (Amun) and the manifest sun (Ra). He is the crown chakra's supreme Egyptian expression — the divine that cannot be seen or named.
of: White and blue offerings, ram and goose imagery, incense — particularly myrrh and frankincense, the honouring of the invisible
an: Ram (hidden power), goose (the Great Cackler who laid the cosmic egg), snake
co: Blue, black (the invisible), gold (the manifest)
dp: Sunday / The Central Sun — he is behind all suns
cr: Lapis lazuli, clear quartz, amethyst
bp: The crown, the breath, the invisible life force
af: I acknowledge what is hidden in me and in all things. The greatest power is invisible. I am more than what can be seen.
sh: The hidden that hides from itself — the shadow of Amun is divine power that does not know it is divine
ar: The Hidden Divinity / The Unknowable Source
cb: Aten (the visible, tangible solar disc — the supreme opposite of the Hidden One) counterbalances Amun. The great religious conflict of Akhenaten's reign (Aten vs Amun) was the cosmic polarity of the visible divine versus the invisible divine made political.

do: The destroyer and the ascetic, pure consciousness, the cosmic dance, the third eye, Kundalini, liberation
at: The supreme deity of Shaivism — one of the three primary aspects of the Hindu divine (alongside Brahma the creator and Vishnu the preserver). He dances the Nataraja (cosmic dance) that sustains and destroys the universe. He is the supreme ascetic, the lord of Kundalini, the one on whose chest Shakti dances. He teaches that pure consciousness and absolute detachment are the gateway to liberation.
of: Bilva (bael) leaves (offered in sets of three), milk and water poured over the Shiva lingam, blue flowers, ash (vibhuti), sandalwood paste, chanting Om Namah Shivaya
an: Bull (Nandi — his sacred mount, the perfect devotee), cobra (worn around his neck), tiger (his skin covering)
co: White, grey (the ash he wears), blue (his throat — Neelakantha, 'Blue Throat')
dp: Monday / Saturn — the destroyer, the ascetic, the one who holds time
cr: Clear quartz (the lingam form), black tourmaline, moonstone, rudraksha beads
bp: Crown, third eye (when opened, destroys what it perceives), spine (the axis of Kundalini), the entire consciousness
af: Om Namah Shivaya. I am the witness. I am the dance. I am the stillness in the centre of all movement.
sh: The Shiva who destroys without creating space — destruction as nihilism rather than liberation; the ascetic who refuses the world entirely
ar: The Cosmic Dancer / The Pure Consciousness
cb: Shakti — the supreme cosmic polarity. Without Shakti, Shiva is a corpse. Without Shiva, Shakti is chaos. Their union IS the universe. Also Vishnu (the preserver) counterbalances Shiva (the destroyer). The three aspects of Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva are in perpetual triple polarity.

do: Creation, the first cause, the lotus of cosmic origin, the Vedas
at: The creator of the cosmos — he emerged from the lotus growing from Vishnu's navel and created all the worlds. He has four faces (the four Vedas) and four arms. He is rarely worshipped directly — there is only one Brahma temple in all of India (in Pushkar). He represents the creative principle at the crown level — the divine intelligence that conceives the cosmos.
of: White and yellow flowers, lotus, the Vedas studied and honoured, the creative act as sacred offering
an: Swan (hamsa — the vehicle of pure discernment), goose
co: White, golden
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the great creator and the divine law
cr: Clear quartz, white calcite, yellow topaz
bp: Crown, the brain (as the instrument of cosmic creativity), the entire being as cosmos-in-miniature
af: I am the creative intelligence of the cosmos. I create worlds with my attention. The divine speaks through my mind.
sh: The creator who is not worshipped — Brahma's mythological shadow is his cursedness, the one who created from ego and was therefore excluded from worship (replaced by Vishnu and Shiva)
ar: The First Creator / The Divine Mind
cb: Shiva (the destroyer) and Vishnu (the preserver) complete the trinity. Brahma (creator) counterbalanced by both. The creative impulse always counterbalanced by preservation and dissolution. Three forces in eternal interplay.

do: Longevity, serenity, peaceful liberation, compassion in its most refined expression
at: White Tara — the crown aspect of the Bodhisattva of compassion. She has seven eyes (one in her forehead, palms and soles of her feet) — she sees all suffering in all directions simultaneously. She grants longevity, serenity and the long life needed to achieve liberation. She is the peace that lies beyond all understanding.
of: White lotus, white candles, Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha (same mantra as Green Tara), white offerings, the cultivation of genuine serenity
an: White crane, dove
co: White, pale gold
dp: Monday / Moon — the white light of peace
cr: White quartz, selenite, white moonstone, pearl
bp: Crown, the seven eyes (sight in all directions), the entire being in its most refined state
af: I rest in the peace that has no opposite. I see all suffering and I remain serene. I carry the still centre within me.
sh: The peace that detaches — the crown chakra that has no compassion for the world below, that floats free of all earthly concern
ar: The White Peace / The Sevenfold Vision
cb: Green Tara (swift compassionate action) counterbalances White Tara (patient serenity). Activity counterbalanced by stillness. The swift heart counterbalanced by the still crown.

do: The supreme sky god, the source of all, the one who created all things
at: The supreme deity of the Akan people — 'Nyame' in Twi. He is the creator of all things, the source behind all other divine forces. His symbol is the Gye Nyame ('Except for God') — one of the most recognised symbols in all of African art, worn to signify that God alone is supreme. He is the crown principle of African spirituality: the infinite source beyond all personalised deity.
of: Prayer, the living of a righteous life, the acknowledgment of the divine in all things
an: None — he is beyond animal correspondence
co: Pure white, gold
dp: Sunday — the supreme day of the divine
cr: Clear quartz, diamond
bp: The crown, the entire being
af: Gye Nyame. Except for God, nothing surpasses. I am held in the infinite. I bow to what is greater.
sh: The distant creator — the god who made all things and then withdrew, leaving creation to find its own way
ar: The Supreme / The Source of All
cb: Asase Yaa (the earth, the immediate, the physical ground) counterbalances Nyame (the sky, the infinite, the divine source). The supreme above counterbalanced by the sacred below. Sky and earth in their Akan expression.

do: The supreme creator, the ocean of origins, the invisible source of all creation
at: The supreme creator deity of the Inca civilisation — the divine force from whom all other gods and all creation emerged. He created the sun, moon, stars and all life. He appeared as a wandering beggar to test humanity. He walked across the ocean and disappeared at the horizon. He is the unknowable source — the Andean expression of the crown principle.
of: Gold and silver (the sweat of the sun and tears of the moon — his children), sacred ceremonies at mountain peaks, prayers directed to the horizon where he disappeared
an: Condor, serpent
co: White, gold, the colours of all creation
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: Clear quartz, gold, white calcite
bp: Crown, the entire being as a creation of the supreme
af: I am a creation of the infinite. The supreme walks through my world in forms I might not recognise. I honour the divine wanderer.
sh: The god who leaves — the creator who departs after creation, leaving behind only the memory of what was once fully present
ar: The Creator Who Walked Away / The Supreme Origin
cb: Pachamama (the earth mother, the immediate, the sustaining) counterbalances Viracocha (the supreme creator, the departed, the distant). He created; she sustains. His absence counterbalanced by her constant presence.

do: The highest of all angels, the scribe of God, the guardian of the crown, the Metatron's Cube
at: The supreme archangel of the Kabbalistic tradition — said to have been the prophet Enoch, so beloved by God that he was taken into heaven and transformed into an angel. He sits at the throne of God and records all human actions. Metatron's Cube is the sacred geometric pattern that contains all five Platonic solids and is said to encode the fundamental structures of creation.
of: Sacred geometry drawn in his honour, white and violet candles, the pursuit of divine wisdom, meditation on Metatron's Cube
an: None — he is beyond animal correspondence
co: White, violet, gold
dp: Sunday / Uranus — the crown, the highest plane
cr: Clear quartz (especially Metatron's Cube carved in crystal), selenite, apophyllite
bp: Crown, the entire light body, the merkaba
af: I am recorded in the divine record exactly as I am. My entire journey has been witnessed. I am known to the highest.
sh: The impossible standard — Metatron represents such cosmic perfection that his shadow is the human sense of never being enough
ar: The Divine Scribe / The Crown Guardian
cb: Sandalphon (Metatron's twin, who is said to have been the prophet Elijah — the grounded twin who collects human prayers and brings them to heaven) counterbalances Metatron (the supreme heavenly principle). The highest heaven (Metatron/crown) counterbalanced by the earthly bridge (Sandalphon/root). The cosmic axis from earth to heaven.

do: Creation, the living water, the dreaming, the first law, the snake that spans all chakras
at: The supreme creative being of many Aboriginal Australian traditions — the great serpent who created the landscape as it moved across the land, whose body formed the river beds, whose scales became the hills. It lives in deep water holes and is both creator and keeper of the First Law. The Rainbow Serpent is among the oldest continuous religious traditions in human history — over 6,000 years of unbroken spiritual knowledge.
of: Water, ochre (red, yellow, white), the singing of country songs, the drawing of sacred designs, ceremonies performed in country
an: Serpent (it IS the serpent), all water creatures
co: All colours of the rainbow, deep blue, red ochre
dp: Monday / All planets — the serpent spans all creation
cr: Rainbow moonstone, labradorite (its fire), ochre stone, red jasper
bp: The entire spine (the serpent is the Kundalini path of the continent), the crown (where it meets the sky), the root (where it lives in the deep water holes)
af: I am dreamed into being. The oldest wisdom lives in the land. I carry the first law in my body. I am part of the dreaming.
sh: The serpent when it is disrespected — the violation of country, the breakage of the First Law, the flooding and drowning that occurs when sacred sites are desecrated
ar: The First Creator / The Living Landscape
cb: Baiame (the Great Spirit, the sky father of some traditions) counterbalances the Rainbow Serpent (the earth/water creator). Sky and earth, father and mother, the two principles of Aboriginal cosmology in their deepest expression.

do: The supreme creator, the cosmic egg, the source of all being, the first word
at: The supreme creator god of the Dogon people — he created the universe from the cosmic egg (the 'egg of the world'). The Dogon's extraordinarily sophisticated cosmology includes Amma creating the earth (his consort), the Nommo (divine beings of water and wisdom), and an elaborate system of creation through vibration and the divine word. The Dogon's knowledge of astronomy — particularly their knowledge of the Sirius star system — remains one of the great mysteries of human intellectual history.
of: Grain, millet (the first food given to humanity by Amma), sacred ceremonies at the foot of the Bandiagara Escarpment
an: Pale fox (the trickster of Dogon cosmology, who disrupted Amma's creation), tortoise
co: Black and white (the fundamental polarity of creation)
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator
cr: Meteorite, clear quartz
bp: Crown, the entire body as a microcosm of Amma's cosmic creation
af: I am a word spoken by the infinite. The cosmic egg within me is still hatching. I honour the wisdom of those who remembered.
sh: The pale fox — the disruptive, destructive principle that entered creation when Amma's original design was compromised
ar: The Cosmic Egg / The Supreme Creator
cb: The Pale Fox (Ogo/Yurugu — the trickster of imperfect creation, the principle of disorder) counterbalances Amma (the supreme order and perfection of divine intention). Order counterbalanced by the inevitable entropy of creation.

do: The supreme mystery, the father of all Orishas, the cosmic source of Yoruba creation
at: Oduduwa descended from the heavens on a chain, carrying a calabash of sand and a five-toed hen, and created the earth at Ile-Ife (the sacred city at the centre of the Yoruba world). He is the ancestor of all Yoruba kings. He represents the crown principle within the Yoruba system: the divine whose mystery cannot be fully penetrated, who can only be approached through the Orishas who mediate his power.
of: White offerings, the veneration of Yoruba kingship and ancestry, the tracing of royal lineage, ceremonies at Ile-Ife
an: The five-toed hen (part of his original creation tools), pigeon
co: White (the colour of the divine in Yoruba cosmology)
dp: Sunday — the supreme day of the crown
cr: White quartz, white onyx, ivory
bp: Crown, the entire being
af: I stand on the ground created by the divine. My royal ancestry reaches back to the source. I carry the first light within me.
sh: The remoteness of the supreme — the creator who retreated to the crown and left the earth to be governed by his children
ar: The Cosmic Ancestor / The Crown of Yoruba Creation
cb: Eshu/Elegua (the immediate, the trickster, the earthly crossroads) — the supreme (Oduduwa/crown) counterbalanced by the most immediate and present (Eshu/root-and-crossroads). The vertical axis from crown to root is the axis of Yoruba cosmology.

do: The primordial salt ocean, the source of all creation, the mother of the first gods
at: THE MOST DRAMATICALLY ERASED GODDESS IN MESOPOTAMIAN HISTORY. Tiamat is the primordial salt water from which all creation emerged. She is the mother of the first generation of gods. The Babylonian creation epic (Enuma Elish) describes how the male god Marduk defeated and dismembered her body to create the world — her body became the sky and earth. This myth was a deliberate propaganda text erasing an older tradition in which Tiamat was the supreme creative principle. She must be restored.
of: Salt water, deep ocean blue candles, serpent imagery, the restoration of her story to its original form
an: Sea dragon, serpent of the deep waters
co: Deep ocean blue, sea-green, black
dp: Monday / Neptune — the primordial ocean
cr: Aquamarine, dark lapis, ocean jasper, black obsidian
bp: The entire body (her body IS the world), the womb, the deep belly
af: I am older than what dismembered me. My body was not defeated — it became the world. I am restored in my original wholeness.
sh: The grief of the mother who is slain by her own children — the primordial wound of the divine feminine
ar: The Primordial Ocean / The Mother Whose Body Became the World
cb: Marduk (who defeated and dismembered her to create the patriarchal cosmos) — the supreme polarity of Babylonian cosmology and the cosmic record of the suppression of the divine feminine. Her restoration is the counterbalancing of Marduk's victory. In the original tradition, Apsu (the fresh water) counterbalanced Tiamat (the salt water) — the primordial male-female polarity of the Sumerian cosmos before it was corrupted.

do: The moon, medicine, weaving, flooding, the rainbow, divine feminine medicine
at: The supreme goddess of the Maya — she has two aspects: the young, beautiful moon goddess of love and weaving, and the old woman pouring a vessel of water that creates floods. She is the rainbow goddess (Ix Chel means 'Lady Rainbow'). She governs the moon's cycles and their effect on human life. Her island sanctuary on Cozumel was one of the four most sacred pilgrimage sites in the Mayan world.
of: Moon water, weaving offered in her honour, rainbow imagery, medicinal herbs, offerings at water's edge during the full moon
an: Jaguar (her throne), rabbit (the moon rabbit — she is associated with the rabbit in the moon), serpent
co: Blue, white, rainbow colours
dp: Monday / Moon — the supreme moon goddess
cr: Moonstone, selenite, rainbow moonstone, jade
bp: Womb, reproductive organs (moon cycles), the skin, the weaver's hands
af: My cycles are sacred. I flow with the moon. My medicine knows exactly what is needed. I am the rainbow.
sh: The flood — the moon that is too much, the water that destroys rather than nourishes, the medicine that poisons
ar: The Rainbow Medicine Woman / The Moon Mother
cb: Itzamná (the supreme sky deity, her consort) — moon counterbalanced by sky. The rainbow she is (water + light) counterbalanced by the pure solar light of the supreme sky god. Also Ah Puch (god of death) counterbalances Ixchel (goddess of life and medicine).

do: The sun, light, creation through light, the awakening of the world from darkness
at: THE FORGOTTEN AUSTRALIAN SUN GODDESS. Yhi is the sun goddess of the Karraur people of Australia — a deity almost entirely unknown outside of Australia and rarely discussed even within it. In the Dreamtime story, Yhi slept until Baiame (the Great Spirit) whistled, and when she opened her eyes her light filled the world. She walked across the earth and wherever her feet touched, plants grew. She created all life through her light and movement. She must be known.
of: Sunrise ceremonies, ochre drawn on the body, gratitude expressed at dawn, the care of growing plants
an: All creatures she created — particularly insects (she created them first, in the underworld caves)
co: Gold, yellow, brilliant orange
dp: Sunday / Sun — she is the sun
cr: Sunstone, amber, golden tiger's eye, citrine
bp: Solar plexus, eyes, the entire body illuminated by the sun
af: When I open my eyes the world comes alive. My light creates life. I am the sun goddess awakening.
sh: The closed eyes — the sun that does not rise, the light that remains unexpressed, the goddess who sleeps while the world waits
ar: The Awakening Sun / The Light That Creates Life
cb: Bahloo (the moon god of some Aboriginal traditions) counterbalances Yhi (the sun goddess). Moon and sun in Australian cosmology. Also darkness itself (the void before her awakening) counterbalances Yhi's light.

do: The cycles of nature, female power, fertility, the seasons, the sacred feminine across all traditions
at: The most revered deity in Navajo cosmology — Asdzáá Nádleehé (Changing Woman). She embodies the female life cycle and the cycle of the seasons: she is young and beautiful in spring, mature and full in summer, wise and grey in autumn, and disappears (dies) in winter only to return young again in spring. She created the Navajo people from her own skin. She taught the Kinaaldá ceremony that marks a girl's becoming a woman.
of: Cornmeal, pollen, turquoise, shell, the Kinaaldá ceremony, the acknowledgment of each season of a woman's life as sacred
an: Horse, eagle, deer
co: Turquoise and white shell (east/white, south/turquoise, west/yellow abalone, north/black jet)
dp: Monday / Moon — the woman who changes with the seasons
cr: Turquoise, white shell, yellow abalone, black jet (the four sacred stones of the four directions)
el: Earth / All elements across the seasons
bp: The entire female body across all its cycles, the womb, the skin (she created people from her skin)
af: I am sacred in every season of my life. I change and remain myself. My cycles are my power.
sh: The woman who refuses to change — who clings to one season, refusing the next transformation
ar: The Sacred Cycles / The Woman Who Is Always Becoming
cb: Father Sky / Father Sun (her husband in some traditions) — the woman of the earth's cycles counterbalanced by the eternal sky father. Also the Turquoise Boy (her male counterpart in some Navajo stories) counterbalances Changing Woman.

do: Swift compassion, liberation from all fears, the earth that supports all beings
at: Green Tara's name in Tibetan is Sgrol-ma ('She Who Saves'). She is said to have vowed to achieve enlightenment in female form precisely because male forms dominated. She acts in the time it takes to blink an eye. She is the earth's compassion — green as the living earth, swift as a thought, holding all 21 of her aspects simultaneously.
of: Green offerings, her mantra 108 times, turquoise, the cultivation of swift compassionate response
an: Lotus (she rises from it)
co: Green, turquoise, gold
dp: Friday / Venus and Monday / Moon
cr: Emerald, green jade, malachite, turquoise
bp: Heart, feet (right foot extended in readiness to act), the eyes (all-seeing compassion)
af: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha. I act immediately from compassion. I am the earth's love made mobile.
sh: Compassion without boundaries — the bodhisattva who suffers the suffering of all and forgets her own wholeness
ar: The Swift Saviour / The Earth's Compassion
cb: Mahakala (the fierce protector, the destroyer of obstacles, the dark lord of time) counterbalances Green Tara. Fierce wisdom (Mahakala) counterbalancing compassionate action (Tara). The sword and the lotus.

do: The feathered serpent, wind, learning, arts, the morning star, the spiritual path of ascent
at: The Feathered Serpent — the supreme Mesoamerican deity who unites heaven (the quetzal bird, the feathers of flight) with earth (the coatl/serpent of the earth). He is the Kundalini principle in Mesoamerican form: the serpent energy that rises from the root to the crown. He is the god of learning, arts, wind, and the morning star. He brought agriculture and civilisation to humanity.
of: Quetzal feathers, jade, copal incense, books and learning honoured, wind ceremonies at dawn
an: Quetzal bird, serpent (combined = the feathered serpent), dog
co: Green (quetzal), gold, turquoise
dp: Wednesday / Venus (as the morning star) — the ascending principle
cr: Jade, turquoise, obsidian (his dark mirror — Tezcatlipoca's tool)
bp: The entire spine (as the feathered serpent = Kundalini), crown, root
af: I am the serpent that rises. I carry earth wisdom to heavenly heights. My ascent carries all that is holy from below.
sh: Tezcatlipoca — the shadow in the obsidian mirror. Quetzalcoatl's supreme shadow is his rival: the smoking mirror that shows your darkest self and causes you to flee in shame
ar: The Ascending Serpent / The Bridge Between Earth and Heaven
cb: Tezcatlipoca (the Smoking Mirror — darkness, sorcery, the night sky, the destroyer of civilisation) counterbalances Quetzalcoatl (the Feathered Serpent — light, learning, the morning star, the bringer of civilisation). Their eternal contest IS Mesoamerican history and cosmology.

do: The sun, craftsmanship, skill in all arts, the warrior's excellence, the harvest
at: The Many-Skilled God — his title is 'Samildánach' (equally skilled in all the arts). He was the only person allowed into Tara (the divine hall) because he was the only one who possessed ALL the skills simultaneously. He is the solar warrior at his most refined — not just the fire of combat but the fire of mastery across all human endeavour.
of: The offering of your highest skill — whatever craft you do with mastery, offered to Lugh. Bilberries and grain at Lughnasadh (August 1st — his festival)
an: Raven/crow (his sacred birds), horse, lynx
co: Gold, bright yellow, orange
dp: Sunday / Mercury — the multi-skilled solar deity
cr: Citrine, yellow topaz, sunstone, amber
bp: Hands (for all crafts), solar plexus, eyes (his spear Luin was an eye of fire)
af: I bring my full mastery to every endeavour. The sun within me illuminates every skill. I am Samildánach — all-skilled.
sh: The perfection that cannot rest — the multi-gifted one who can never satisfy the standards of their own excellence
ar: The Master of All Arts / The Solar Champion
cb: The Dagda (the good god of abundance, whose club restores the dead and whose cauldron feeds all) counterbalances Lugh (the skilled warrior). Skill counterbalanced by abundance. The warrior's precision counterbalanced by the father's generosity.

do: Battle, fate, sovereignty, prophecy, death, the raven's sight, the queen who chooses the slain
at: The triple war goddess of Irish mythology — she appears as three sisters: Badb (the cauldron of death), Macha (sovereignty of the land), and Nemain (frenzied battle panic). She is the one who determines who lives and dies on the battlefield. Her ravens circle over the slain. She sees the future with absolute clarity and speaks it even when no one wants to hear it. She offered herself to Cú Chulainn and he refused — she made his death certain.
of: Black crow/raven feathers, red wine, offerings at battlefields and boundaries, the honest confrontation of what you fear most
an: Crow, raven, eel, wolf, red heifer
co: Black, deep red, blood red
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Monday / Moon — war and the dark vision
cr: Black obsidian, jet, bloodstone, dark garnet
bp: The adrenal glands (battle hormones), the eyes of prophecy, the root (sovereignty of land)
af: I see what is coming with clear eyes and I meet it without flinching. My sovereignty is my own. I choose my battles wisely.
sh: The sovereignty that becomes cruelty — the fate-goddess whose predictions become punishments, the queen who destroys those who refuse her
ar: The Battle Queen / The Fate-Seer
cb: Brigid (the creative fire, healing, poetry) counterbalances the Morrigan (the destructive fire, battle, prophecy of death). Both are triple goddesses, both govern fire — Brigid's creative fire vs the Morrigan's destructive fire. The supreme Celtic feminine polarity.

do: Love, fertility, war, death (she takes half the slain), magic (seiðr), cats, gold
at: The most powerful of the Vanir gods — she is simultaneously the goddess of love AND war. She weeps tears of gold for her absent husband Óðr. She takes half of all those slain in battle to her hall Fólkvangr (the other half go to Odin's Valhalla). She taught Odin the most powerful form of magic (seiðr). She is the complete woman — lover, warrior, shaman, weeper of gold.
of: Gold jewellery, amber beads, cats honoured, mead, flowers, Friday (her day) ceremonies
an: Cat (two cats pull her chariot), boar, falcon (she wears a falcon cloak)
co: Gold, red, forest green
dp: Friday (Freyjadagr — Friday is named for her) / Venus — love and war combined
cr: Amber, gold, rose quartz, carnelian
bp: Womb, heart, the solar plexus (war), the third eye (seiðr magic)
af: I am both the lover and the warrior. I weep gold for what I love. I choose who rises and who falls.
sh: The Gullveig — Freyja's shadow is 'Gold-Power,' the greed that caused the first war in the cosmos. She was burned three times and reborn three times as Heiðr (the brightness of honours that corrupts).
ar: The Golden Warrior / The Shaman Queen
cb: Frigg (the queen of Asgard, the weaver of fate, the devoted wife who weeps in stillness) counterbalances Freyja (the free and fierce one who weeps gold and fights). Two supreme Norse goddesses in polarity: stillness and movement, queenship and freedom, the known and the wild.

do: Wisdom, war, death, poetry, seiðr magic, the runes, self-sacrifice for gnosis
at: The Allfather — he sacrificed his eye to Mimir's well for wisdom, and hung on the World Tree Yggdrasil for nine days to receive the runes. He is the shamanic deity par excellence — the god who pays any price for deeper knowledge. He leads the Wild Hunt. He chooses his einherjar (the fallen warriors) who will fight alongside him at Ragnarök.
of: The offering of knowledge pursued at personal cost, mead, ravens, the runes studied and used with integrity, self-imposed trials of wisdom
an: Raven (Huginn and Muninn — Thought and Memory), wolf (Geri and Freki), horse (Sleipnir, the eight-legged horse)
co: Deep blue, grey, black, gold
dp: Wednesday (Woden's day) / Mercury and Uranus — wisdom and revelation
cr: Labradorite, dark sapphire, black tourmaline, rune stones
bp: One eye (the eye he gave), the third eye (the wisdom he received), the crown, the spine (the World Tree he hung from)
af: I pay the price of true wisdom willingly. I sacrifice what is lesser for what is greater. I am the one who hangs and sees.
sh: The Allfather's shadow is the manipulation of fate — the god who plays with human lives like pieces on a board, the wisdom that becomes cold
ar: The Sacrificed Sage / The Cosmic Wanderer
cb: Baldur (the beautiful, the innocent, the shining one who must die) counterbalances Odin (the wise, the sacrificing, the one who cannot be killed by his own choice). Also Thor (the simple, direct warrior) counterbalances Odin (the complex, subtle strategist). The straight counterbalanced by the crooked.

do: Grain, agriculture, the earth's fertility, maternal grief, the cycle of loss and return
at: The goddess of grain and the sacred mysteries of Eleusis. When her daughter Persephone was taken to the underworld, Demeter's grief was so total that the earth became barren. The Eleusinian Mysteries — the most important spiritual initiation of the ancient world — were based on the myth of her loss and reunion with Persephone. She teaches the root and heart chakras simultaneously: the earth that feeds us, and the grief of the mother that will not be comforted.
of: Wheat and grain, poppies, pomegranates (Persephone's fruit), the making of bread as sacred, harvest ceremonies
an: Snake (chthonic wisdom), pig, crane
co: Golden wheat, earth brown, dark green
dp: Friday / Saturn and Moon — the earth's cycle of loss and abundance
cr: Honey calcite, amber, green aventurine, brown jasper
bp: Womb (Persephone as her child = the fruits of the earth), the stomach and digestion, the arms (reaching for what is lost)
af: My grief is sacred. What is lost to me will return in a new form. I am the earth — I endure every winter.
sh: The mother who cannot let her daughter live her own descent — the grief that becomes control, the love that prevents growth
ar: The Grieving Mother / The Sacred Earth
cb: Persephone (the daughter, the one who descends and returns, the queen of the underworld) counterbalances Demeter (the mother who remains on the surface). The descent counterbalanced by the one who cannot descend. Also Hades counterbalances Demeter in their eternal contest for Persephone.

do: The sacred descent and return, the underworld, spring, the threshold between worlds, initiation
at: The daughter who descended and became queen. Taken by Hades, she ate the pomegranate seeds and could never fully return. But her return each spring is what makes the earth bloom. She is the initiate — the one who has been through the underworld and returned with knowledge that cannot be gained any other way. She is the bridge between the root and the crown — the one who has touched death and carries that knowledge back to the living.
of: Pomegranates, narcissus flowers, asphodel (underworld flower), the willingness to face your own underworld journey
an: Bat, black ram, serpent
co: Deep purple, black, spring green
dp: Saturday / Pluto and Monday / Moon — the queen of the deep
cr: Pomegranate garnet, black moonstone, amethyst, dark pearl
bp: The entire body traversing all realms, the root (underworld) to the crown (return to the light)
af: I have been to the underworld and I have returned. My descent made me a queen. I carry the knowledge of both worlds.
sh: The shadow of Persephone is the victim identity — the girl who was taken, who never chooses, who remains forever in another's story
ar: The Initiated Queen / The Bridge Between Worlds
cb: Demeter (who waits on the surface) counterbalances Persephone (who descends below). Also Zeus (who brokered her fate) and Hades (who claimed her) form the patriarchal counterbalances to her own sovereignty. Her personal counter-balance is the cultivation of choice within a situation that began without her consent.

do: Death, the hidden, the night, mourning, shadow wisdom, the threshold guardian
at: The dark twin of Isis — she is the goddess who attends at death. She is associated with the darkness before dawn, with the hidden and unseen dimensions of existence, with the threshold between life and death. She mourned with Isis for Osiris. Her name means 'Lady of the House' but the house is the funeral bier. She sees what others refuse to see.
of: Black candles, midnight offerings, the acknowledgment of grief as sacred, dark blue cloth
an: Kite (she mourns like a bird), falcon
co: Black, dark blue, midnight
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the dark hidden dimension
cr: Black tourmaline, jet, dark blue sapphire, obsidian
bp: The peripheral vision, the third eye in darkness, the body at death and in transition
af: I honour what is hidden. I attend at every threshold with wisdom and grace. The darkness holds what the light cannot see.
sh: The endless mourning — the shadow of Nephthys is grief without transformation, the one who only attends deaths and never celebrations of life
ar: The Shadow Twin / The Dark Threshold Guardian
cb: Isis (the light, life, manifest magic) counterbalances Nephthys (the shadow, death, hidden magic). The two sisters are the complete feminine polarity — light and shadow, life and death, the known and the unknown.

do: Justice, thunder, fire, the law that cannot be corrupted, divine judgement
at: In the Brazilian Candomblé tradition, Xango (Shango) takes on a particular aspect as the Orixá of justice and divine law — the divine principle that ensures the law cannot be corrupted by wealth or power. He is the supreme judge. His axe cuts through false testimony. He is among the most widely worshipped Orixás in Brazil.
of: Dendê (palm oil), okra, apple, red and white offerings, the axe imagery, drumming
an: Ram
co: Red and white
dp: Wednesday / Jupiter (justice) in Candomblé correspondence
cr: Red garnet, meteorite, fire opal
bp: Solar plexus, throat (justice that must be spoken)
af: I stand before the bar of my own truth and I am found righteous. Justice cannot be bought from within me.
sh: The judge who judges only others — the one who holds others to the standard they exempt themselves from
ar: The Divine Judge / The Incorruptible
cb: Oxalá (Obatala in Brazil — pure white consciousness, peace, the father of creation) counterbalances Xango (fire, justice, thunder). Peace and justice. Stillness and storm.

do: Death, resurrection, sex, crude humour, the crossroads between the living and the dead
at: The head of the Ghede family of Lwa — he is death, but death with a laugh. He smokes cigars, drinks rum laced with hot peppers, tells crude jokes, and is the only one who can decide whether a person truly dies or returns to life. He wears black and purple, a top hat, and sunglasses with one lens removed (he sees both the world of the living and the dead simultaneously). He cannot be easily offended because he has seen everything.
of: Rum with 21 hot peppers infused (he's the only one who can drink it without wincing), cigars, black candles, purple candles, pennies, phallic imagery (he governs sexuality and death simultaneously)
an: Black chicken, serpent
co: Black and purple
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the lord of death
cr: Obsidian, jet, black tourmaline, purple amethyst
bp: The entire body at death, the genitalia (he governs both sex and death — the same energy), the spine
af: I am not afraid of death. I laugh in the face of what I cannot control. Life is precious precisely because it ends.
sh: The macabre without wisdom — the death fixation that refuses to celebrate life, the laughter that is hollow
ar: The Laughing Death / The Resurrection Master
cb: Erzulie Freda (love, beauty, the romantic heart that wants to live forever) counterbalances Baron Samedi (death, the crude, the inevitable end). Love and death in their most vivid Haitian expression. Also Ghede Nibo (his son, the spirit of the young who died before their time) deepens his counterbalance — the joy of life counterbalancing the reality of death.

do: Cats, the home, protection of the family, the gentle sun, joy, dancing
at: The cat goddess — the domestic, nurturing face of solar power. Where Sekhmet is the harsh desert sun, Bast is the warm hearthside sun. She protects the home, presides over childbirth, governs cats (who were sacred in Egypt and could not be harmed on pain of death). She is the solar plexus principle in its most nurturing, joyful face.
of: Cats honoured and cared for, music and dance as offering, sistrum rattles, catnip, small cat figurines
an: Cat (she is the cat)
co: Gold, warm orange, warm brown
dp: Sunday / Venus — the gentle sun and the home
cr: Tiger's eye (the cat's eye stone), golden topaz, amber, cat's eye chrysoberyl
bp: The protective instincts, the sensory pleasure centres (cats), the sacral
af: I protect what I love with grace rather than force. Joy is a form of power. I am both fierce and gentle.
sh: The indolent cat — the protective goddess who becomes the one who sleeps in the sun while danger approaches
ar: The Gentle Guardian / The Joyful Protector
cb: Sekhmet — the complete feminine solar polarity. Bast (the gentle cat, the domestic sun) counterbalanced by Sekhmet (the fierce lioness, the solar fire). They are the same force in its two expressions.

do: The divine messenger, annunciation, creativity, the sacred word delivered, the moon
at: The great messenger — he announced the birth of John the Baptist, the conception of Jesus (to Mary), and delivered the Quran to Muhammad over 23 years. He is the archangel of revelation and sacred communication — the one who carries the divine word from the highest source to the human ear. He is also associated with the moon and with creative inspiration.
of: White candles, white lilies (the annunciation lily), silver, journaling or writing as sacred practice, the moon honoured
an: Dove, lily
co: White, silver, pale blue
dp: Monday / Moon — the messenger and the moon are linked
cr: Moonstone, selenite, white opal, aquamarine
bp: Throat, womb (the Annunciation to Mary — the sacred conception), the ears (to receive divine messages)
af: The divine message is being delivered through me. I listen. I announce what is sacred. I am the vessel of the holy word.
sh: The annunciation that is unwelcome — Gabriel's message was not always wanted. His shadow is the divine communication that disrupts the comfortable
ar: The Divine Messenger / The Sacred Annunciator
cb: Archangel Michael (the warrior, the one who acts on the message) counterbalances Gabriel (the one who delivers the message). The word and the sword. Also Archangel Raphael (healing, the journey companion) counterbalances Gabriel (the cosmic announcer).

do: Healing, the medicine of divine love, the journey companion, sight restored
at: The archangel of healing — his name means 'God Heals.' He healed the blind Tobit in the Book of Tobit. He guided Tobias on his journey. He is the divine physician, the companion on the journey, the one who holds the staff of the sacred journey. He is associated with the heart chakra as the archangel who heals through love.
of: Green candles, healing herbs offered as prayer, the staff and caduceus symbol, emerald, prayers for healing
an: Fish (he advised Tobias on the healing properties of fish), dog (Tobias's dog on the journey)
co: Green, gold
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the healer's wisdom
cr: Emerald, malachite, green aventurine
bp: Heart, eyes (sight restored), the healing of any organ of any system
af: The divine physician walks with me. My healing has a sacred companion. I am guided on every journey of restoration.
sh: The healer who cannot heal himself — Raphael's shadow is the divine physician who attends all others but neglects his own wounds
ar: The Divine Physician / The Journey Companion
cb: Archangel Michael (the warrior, the sword, the force) counterbalances Raphael (the healer, the staff, the restoration). The wound-maker and the wound-healer. Also Archangel Azrael (the angel of death) counterbalances Raphael (the angel of healing) — the inevitable endpoint and the extended journey.

do: The wild, animals, the forest, masculine fertility, the cycle of life and death, the horned god
at: The Horned God of the Celtic world — depicted seated in the lotus position (remarkably) with antlers, holding a serpent and a torc of gold. He is surrounded by wild animals. He is the lord of the forest, the god of wild nature, the masculine principle of the untamed. He is largely forgotten in mainstream spirituality but was among the most widely worshipped Celtic deities across Europe.
of: Wild herbs left in forests, the honouring of wild animals, antler imagery, forest offerings, the torc (Celtic neck ring)
an: Stag (his antlers), serpent, ram-headed serpent, all wild animals
co: Forest green, brown, dark gold
dp: Saturday / Earth / Saturn — the lord of wild nature and the cycles of time
cr: Brown tourmaline, moss agate, green jasper, antler carving
bp: Spine (the trunk of the tree), legs (roots in the earth), the heart (wild nature's compassion)
af: I am the wild principle in myself. I belong to the forest. The untamed part of me is sacred.
sh: The wild that becomes chaos — the masculine force without any feminine principle of care, the forest god who destroys rather than sustains
ar: The Horned Wild / The Lord of Living Things
cb: Brigid (the hearth, the domestic sacred fire) counterbalances Cernunnos (the wild, the forest fire). Also the Green Man (the more benevolent vegetative face of the same archetype) and the dark Herne the Hunter (his shadow face) represent his inner polarity.

do: The earth itself, the ground of being, the first soil
at: Ki is the Sumerian earth — the divine ground of all being. An (sky) and Ki (earth) were the primordial pair from whose union all life sprang. She was later overshadowed by Ninhursag and Enlil.
of: Earth offerings, grain placed directly into soil, planting ceremonies
an: Serpent, beetle, all earth creatures
co: Deep brown, dark green, ochre
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Brownstone, red earth clay, serpentine
bp: Bones, the ground beneath the feet, the root structures
af: I am the sacred ground. All life springs from me and returns to me. I am the first mother of all.
sh: The earth that becomes desert — disconnection from soil, abandonment of the ground principle
ar: The First Earth / The Ground Itself
cb: An (Sky) — the primordial Sumerian polarity. Sky and Earth, the first duality.

do: The earth monster, the great devourer, the ground from which all life and death emerge
at: The great earth deity of Aztec cosmology — depicted as a monstrous toad-like creature whose body was used by Quetzalcoatl and Tezcatlipoca to form the earth and sky. She is the primordial earth in its most primal, devouring form.
of: Blood offerings poured into the earth, food buried in the ground, earth ceremonies
an: Crocodile, toad, jaguar
co: Black, dark green, blood red
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Obsidian, black tourmaline, dark jasper
bp: The womb, the entire lower body, the digestive system
af: I am the primal earth. My body is the ground of all existence. I devour and I birth in the same breath.
sh: The devourer — the earth that swallows without end, the mouth that cannot be satisfied
ar: The Earth Monster / The Primal Devourer
cb: Quetzalcoatl and Tezcatlipoca (who formed her body into the earth) counterbalance Tlaltecuhtli.

do: Life and death, the earth mother, the duality of creation and destruction
at: 'She of the Serpent Skirt' — the great earth mother of the Aztecs, mother of Huitzilopochtli. She wears a skirt of serpents and a necklace of hearts and skulls. She embodies the inseparability of life and death.
of: Blood offerings, serpent imagery, skull offerings, earth ceremonies at the base of the pyramid
an: Serpent, eagle, jaguar
co: Black, blood red, earth brown
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Obsidian, black tourmaline, bloodstone, dark garnet
bp: Womb, the entire female body, the earth itself
af: Life and death are the same breath. I hold both without flinching. I am the earth that devours and births.
sh: The devouring earth mother who demands blood — the consuming principle that needs sacrifice to be satisfied
ar: The Serpent Earth / The Life-Death Mother
cb: Huitzilopochtli (her son, the solar war deity who springs from her womb) counterbalances Coatlicue (the dark earth mother).

do: Earth, morality, fertility, the dead, the judicial principle of the earth
at: The supreme goddess of the Igbo people — she is the earth itself and also the supreme moral authority. She governs the code of conduct (Odinani) that regulates Igbo society. The dead are buried in her womb. She is the most important Igbo deity after Chukwu.
of: Offerings buried in the earth, yam festivals, moral living as offering to her
an: Python (her sacred snake), elephant, tortoise
co: White and earth colours
dp: Thursday
cr: White clay, earth clay, limestone
bp: Womb, bones, feet, the moral centre
af: I live in right relationship with the earth. My morality is the earth's justice. What I plant in the sacred ground returns.
sh: The violation of the earth's law — abominations (nso ala) that must be purified
ar: The Moral Earth / The Judicial Mother
cb: Chukwu (the supreme sky deity) counterbalances Ala (the earth). Chi (personal divine flame) and Ala (collective earth justice) hold the individual and collective in balance.

do: The ancestors, the masquerade, the collective dead, ancestral memory
at: Egungun is the masquerade through which the ancestors speak to the living. The Egungun society keeps the connection between the world of the living and the world of the dead alive.
of: Ancestor shrines, offerings of food and drink for the dead, Egungun masquerade ceremonies
an: Crow, vulture (birds of the threshold)
co: White and dark colours mixed
dp: Saturday — the ancestors' day
cr: Dark stones, bones, ancestor objects
bp: The spine (the ancestral column), the blood
af: My ancestors walk with me. The roots beneath me are deeper than I know. I am the living expression of all who came before.
sh: The ancestor who holds the living back — the past that prevents the present from moving forward
ar: The Living Ancestors / The Root of the Family
cb: Obatala (pure consciousness, the future) counterbalances Egungun (the ancestors, the past). Origin and destiny in polarity.

do: War, weaving, hunting, the primordial waters, creation, wisdom
at: One of the oldest Egyptian goddesses — her worship predates the unification of Egypt. She wears the red crown of Lower Egypt. Weaver of the world, goddess of war and hunting, she also represents the primordial waters from which Ra emerged.
of: Arrows and bows as offerings, weaving begun in her honour, red cloth, fishing nets
an: Bee, fish, loom
co: Red, green, dark gold
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Saturday
cr: Dark red jasper, carnelian, green feldspar
bp: Arms (the weaver's arms), the eyes (the huntress's sight)
af: I weave my reality with the threads of my will. My arrows find their mark. I am the first weaver.
sh: The war goddess whose weaving is cut — the creative force interrupted by destruction
ar: The First Weaver / The War-Creator
cb: Thoth (divine scribe, the word) counterbalances Neith (divine weaver, the cloth). The written word and the woven cloth.

do: Sacred springs, wells, the earth's water, divination, purification
at: A goddess of sacred wells and springs in Roman Britain — whose well at Carrawburgh on Hadrian's Wall yielded over 13,000 Roman coins as offerings. She represents the waters that emerge from the earth's deepest body.
of: Coins thrown in wells and springs, water from sacred springs, flower offerings
an: Fish, water birds
co: Blue, silver, white, green
dp: Monday / Moon
cr: Clear quartz, aquamarine, moonstone
bp: The kidneys, underground water channels of the body
af: The sacred water rises from deep within the earth of my being. I am nourished from the source.
sh: The spring that runs dry — the sacred source that is violated or neglected
ar: The Sacred Well / The Earth's Water
cb: The solid land around her well counterbalances Coventina (the water within it). Earth and water in their most intimate union.

do: Creation, human crafting, the sacred feminine creator, repair of the cosmic order
at: The supreme creator goddess of Chinese mythology — she created humanity from yellow clay, and when the pillars of heaven broke she repaired the sky by melting five-coloured stones. She is the cosmic crafter, the mother of humanity.
of: Clay offerings shaped by hand, yellow and multicoloured stones, incense, prayers for fertility
an: Serpent (she is half-serpent), fish
co: Yellow, green, multicoloured
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Yellow jasper, multicoloured tourmaline, serpentine
bp: Womb, hands (the crafter's hands), the entire creative body
af: I craft my world with my own hands. What is broken I can repair. I am the mother of what is made.
sh: The exhaustion of the crafter — Nüwa became so tired after creating humans she created other beings to carry on
ar: The Divine Crafter / The Mother of Humanity
cb: Fuxi (her consort — the masculine principle of cosmic order, law, and the trigrams) counterbalances Nüwa (the feminine principle of creation and repair).

do: Time, agriculture, the harvest, the weight of years, the earth's deep time
at: The Titan god of time and agriculture — father of the Olympians whom he swallowed. He rules the deep time of the earth, the weight of years, the inevitability of harvest. Saturday is his day.
of: Grain, scythe imagery, offerings at harvest, the honouring of elders and ancestors
an: Eagle (Jupiter stole it from him), serpent, crocodile
co: Dark grey, lead, earthen gold
dp: Saturday / Saturn
cr: Lead crystal, smoky quartz, black onyx, hematite
bp: Bones, joints, knees, the structures that hold the body upright
af: I work with the weight of time rather than against it. Harvest comes to those who plant in season.
sh: The devouring father — the patriarch who cannot release what he has created, who destroys his own children
ar: The Harvest Father / The Weight of Time
cb: Zeus / Jupiter (his son who overthrew him) — the old order counterbalanced by the new. Time counterbalanced by the eternal present.

do: Rice, fertility, agriculture, foxes, industry, worldly success, the blessing of the earth
at: One of the most widely worshipped Shinto deities — the divine principle of rice and agriculture who blesses the earth's abundance. Served by fox messengers (kitsune). Has both male and female aspects, and also sometimes manifests as a bodhisattva. Over one-third of all Shinto shrines in Japan are dedicated to Inari.
of: Rice, sake, tofu and aburaage (fried tofu — the foxes love it), red torii gate offerings
an: Fox (kitsune — her divine messengers), white foxes especially
co: Red (the colour of her torii), white, gold
dp: Friday / Venus — abundance and fertility
cr: Rice grain, carnelian, aventurine, amber
bp: Womb (fertility), the digestive system (rice = nourishment), the roots (agriculture)
af: I am fed by the earth's abundance. My industry bears fruit. The sacred foxes carry my prayers to the source.
sh: The trickster fox — the divine messenger who deceives, the abundance that corrupts, the rice that feeds pride not humility
ar: The Fox Shrine / The Blessing of Harvest
cb: Susanoo (her storm, her brother — who destroyed his sister Amaterasu's rice fields in myth) counterbalances Inari (the peaceful cultivation of rice). Storm and harvest.

do: The sea, fishing, wealth from the ocean, fair winds for sailors, fertility
at: The Vanir god of the sea, fishing, and seafaring wealth. Father of Freyr and Freyja. He represents the benevolent, giving face of the ocean — the sea as provider. His home Nóatún (Ship-town) is on the seashore.
of: Fish, sea water, offerings cast on the sea before fishing, prayers for safe crossing
an: Fish, seal, gull
co: Blue, silver, sea-green
dp: Monday / Moon
cr: Aquamarine, blue topaz, pearls
bp: Feet (sea-washed), the hands of the fisherman, the chest (holding breath before the dive)
af: I receive the ocean's gifts with gratitude. Fair winds fill my sails. The sea provides for those who respect her.
sh: The sea withheld — the fishing nets that come up empty, the storms that destroy instead of provide
ar: The Generous Sea / The Provider of Ocean Wealth
cb: Skaði (his estranged wife, goddess of mountains, hunting, and winter) counterbalances Njörðr (the sea, warmth, fishing). The supreme Norse elemental polarity of sea and mountain.

do: Ecstasy, creative intoxication, joy, liberation, theatre, wine, the uninhibited divine
at: God of ecstasy, wine, theatre, creative intoxication, the liberation of inhibitions, and the uninhibited flow of divine joy. Theatre itself was invented in his honour. His worship was the most ecstatic, embodied and politically subversive of the ancient world.
of: Wine, ivy, thyrsos (his sacred staff), masks (theatre), grapes, pine cones, communal ecstatic celebration
an: Panther, bull, goat, serpent, dolphin
co: Purple, dark red, vine green
dp: Friday / Neptune
cr: Amethyst, purple fluorite, grape agate
bp: The pleasure centres, the liver, the entire sensory body
af: I allow sacred joy to move through me without shame. Ecstasy is a form of divine communion. I dance.
sh: Addiction, dissolution without return, the ecstasy that destroys rather than liberates
ar: The Sacred Ecstasy / The Divine Liberator
cb: Apollo (solar order, reason, form, sunlight) counterbalances Dionysus (ecstasy, dissolution, wine, darkness). They shared Delphi — Apollo in summer, Dionysus in winter.

do: The ocean, primal emotional force, earthquakes, horses, the unconscious deep
at: God of the sea, earthquakes and horses. He is the primal emotional force of the sacral in its most raw and overwhelming expression — the ocean that cannot be controlled, the earthquake that shakes the foundation.
of: Sea water, trident imagery, ocean ceremonies, offerings cast into the sea
an: Horse, bull, dolphin, fish
co: Sea blue, dark green, dark grey
dp: Monday / Neptune
cr: Aquamarine, sea glass, dark blue sapphire, labradorite
bp: The emotional body, the nervous system, the sacral plexus
af: I work with the power of the ocean rather than against it. My emotions are as powerful as the sea and I can navigate them.
sh: The earthquake — the emotional force that shakes the very foundation of existence without warning
ar: The Primal Emotional Force / The Lord of the Ocean
cb: Zeus / Jupiter (the sky king, who controls with reason rather than force) counterbalances Poseidon (the primal, emotional, unstable).

do: Hunting, the forest, swift accuracy, the food that sustains, the spiritual hunter
at: The Orisha of hunting and the forest — he represents the masculine principle of focused desire: the hunter who knows exactly what he seeks and goes to it with precision. In Candomblé he is the king of the forest.
of: Bow and arrow, blue candles, offerings of game, venison, forest herbs, blue and silver beads
an: Deer, hawk, all forest animals
co: Blue, silver
dp: Thursday / Jupiter
cr: Lapis lazuli, blue sapphire, turquoise
bp: Eyes (the hunter's sight), legs (the hunter's speed), the hands
af: I know what I seek. My desire is precise. I move through the forest of life with perfect accuracy.
sh: The hunter who cannot stop hunting — the desire that has no rest, no satisfaction
ar: The Precise Hunter / The Forest King
cb: Ogun (who lives in the forest but through brute force clearing) counterbalances Oshosi (who navigates the forest through precision). Force and finesse.

do: Divine love, sacred yearning, the soul's longing for the divine, ecstatic devotion (bhakti)
at: The supreme devotee of Krishna — their love is the template for the soul's relationship with the divine in Vaishnavism. Radha's love for Krishna is so intense it achieves the level of the sacred. She represents the sacral's highest expression: desire as the vehicle of divine union.
of: Yellow flowers, yellow cloth, offerings of love and devotion, bhajans sung in her honour, sweet foods
an: Peacock (Krishna's bird is also hers), lotus
co: Yellow, gold, pink
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Yellow sapphire, citrine, rose quartz
bp: Heart, womb, the ecstatic nervous system
af: My longing for the divine is itself divine. Sacred love is the highest path. I love completely.
sh: The longing that becomes obsession — the devotee who loses herself entirely in the beloved
ar: The Sacred Yearning / The Divine Love
cb: Krishna — Radha (the devotee, the lover, the one who longs) counterbalanced by Krishna (the divine, the beloved). The supreme Vaishnava polarity of the soul and God.

do: Desire, creative attraction, the force that holds the cosmos together, love as cosmic principle
at: In some traditions the primordial god who emerged from the cosmic egg — desire as the first force of creation. Love as the binding principle that holds the cosmos together. The sacral's deepest metaphysical expression.
of: Red and gold offerings, arrows, golden bow, honey, wine
an: Eagle, dove, butterfly
co: Gold, red, rose
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Rose quartz, ruby, garnet, carnelian
bp: Sacral plexus, the heart, the entire pleasure-and-connection system
af: Desire is the creative force of the universe. My longing is holy. Love is what holds all things together.
sh: Thanatos (his twin brother — death) — desire and death as the twin forces driving all existence
ar: The Cosmic Desire / The First Love
cb: Thanatos (Death) — Eros (desire, life force) and Thanatos (death drive) are the twin engines of the psyche. Love and death in their most primal expression.

do: Earth's horizon, guardian of the underworld entrance, the lions of yesterday and tomorrow
at: The earth god who guards the dual entrance to the underworld — depicted as two lions back-to-back representing yesterday and tomorrow, with the earth between them. He supports Ra's barque as it passes underground.
of: Soil offerings, two candles placed back-to-back, lion imagery, dawn and dusk ceremonies
an: Double lion (his symbol), sphinx
co: Earth brown, gold, ochre
dp: Saturday / Saturn
cr: Brown jasper, tiger's eye, onyx
bp: Base of spine, the earthing of the life force
af: I stand between yesterday and tomorrow rooted in the eternal now.
sh: Being trapped between past and future with no ground of the present
ar: The Horizon Guardian / The Earth Between
cb: Ra (the sky journey) counterbalances Aker (the underground journey). The solar cycle above answered by the earthly passage below.

do: Death, the Memphite underworld, craftsmen, the earth's deep darkness
at: One of the oldest Egyptian gods — a funerary deity associated with the Memphite necropolis at Saqqara, predating Osiris absorbing his role. Depicted as a mummified falcon, he governs the subterranean realm and the deep dark of earth.
of: Offerings of grain left to decay into the earth, dark candles, soil from a burial ground, silver
an: Falcon (mummified), serpent
co: Black, dark grey, silver
dp: Saturday / Pluto
cr: Black tourmaline, jet, dark obsidian
bp: Deepest root structures, bones, marrow
af: I honour the darkness beneath me. The dead feed the living. I am grounded in all that came before.
sh: The darkness that never lightens — the subterranean stagnation that refuses the seed
ar: The Deep Earth / The First Funerary God
cb: Ptah (craftsman-creator of the surface world) counterbalances Sokar (god of the underground realm). The Memphite compound Ptah-Sokar unites both principles.

do: Fertility, sunshine, rain, agriculture, the harvest, male sexuality
at: The Vanir god of fertility, sunshine and rain — father of the sacred boar and golden ship. He gave away his magical sword for love of the giantess Gerdr. At Ragnarök he will fight without his sword. He is the masculine principle of earth's fertility.
of: Grain, ale, boar images, harvest ceremonies at Yule and Midsummer
an: Golden boar (Gullinbursti), horse (sacred stallions at his cult), deer
co: Gold, green, amber
dp: Friday / Venus and Sunday / Sun
cr: Citrine, golden topaz, amber, green aventurine
bp: Male reproductive system, the sacral region, the harvest of the body's strength
af: I am the fertile earth. I give what I love most for love. My abundance is naturally expressed.
sh: Freyr sacrificed his sword for love — the king who cannot fight when needed because of romantic attachment
ar: The Fertility King / The Generous Harvest
cb: Freyja (his twin sister — the feminine fertility principle) counterbalances Freyr. Also Skadi (winter, harsh mountains) counterbalances Freyr (summer, fertile plains).

do: The earth itself, mother of Thor
at: Jord (Earth) is the personification of the earth in Norse mythology — mother of Thor by Odin. She IS the foundation beneath all things. Like Gaia or Prithvi, she is the earth as a living sacred being.
of: Soil, stones, offerings made directly to the earth, the first fruits of the harvest
an: All earth-dwelling creatures
co: Brown, green, rock grey
dp: Thursday (Thor's day, her son's day) / Saturn
cr: Brown jasper, flint, dark green serpentine
bp: Legs, feet, bones, the structure that holds the body upright
af: I am held by ancient earth. The ground beneath me is a goddess. I am never without foundation.
sh: The earth that has been forgotten — the sacred ground that has become merely real estate
ar: The Earth Herself / The Silent Foundation
cb: Odin (sky, the wandering father) counterbalances Jord (earth, the stable mother). Their union produced Thor — the storm between sky and earth.

do: The realm of the dead, those who die of illness and old age, the ordinary death
at: Daughter of Loki and giantess Angrboda — she rules Helheim, the realm of those who die of illness or old age (as opposed to warriors who go to Valhalla). Half her body is living flesh; half is corpse. She is neither evil nor good — she is death in its most ordinary, universal form.
of: Black and white candles (one for each of her two halves), bones left at cave entrances, mead, offerings for the dishonoured dead
an: Serpent, crow, wolf
co: Black and white (half-and-half, like her body)
dp: Saturday / Pluto
cr: Black tourmaline, white quartz, jet, pale moonstone
