bp: The entire body — she reminds us the body will return to earth
af: I honour ordinary death. I am not afraid of my own mortality. All that lives returns to earth.
sh: The half-dead who cannot commit to life — paralysis between living and dying
ar: The Ordinary Death / The Universal Queen
cb: Frigg (the mother who mourns) counterbalances Hel (the ruler who receives). Baldr's death sent him to Hel while Frigg pleaded for his return — love counterbalanced by the necessity of death.

do: Earth, fertility, fate, weaving, women's crafts, moisture
at: The supreme Slavic goddess — the only female deity named in the Primary Chronicle alongside major male Slavic gods. She is earth's moisture, the spinner of fate, guardian of women's work. She survives in Russian folklore as Mokosha.
of: Flax and wool offerings, ceremonies on Fridays, offerings of bread and salt, embroidery dedicated to her, the first sheaf of the harvest
an: Sheep (she guards their wool), ram, water birds
co: Moist earth brown, deep red, silver
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Magnetite, dark carnelian, red jasper, amber
bp: Womb, hands (weaving and spinning), the female body's moisture and fertility
af: I weave my fate with my own hands. My work is sacred. The earth's moisture runs in my veins.
sh: The tangled thread — fate knotted beyond repair by neglect of the sacred feminine
ar: The Spinner of Fate / The Earth Mother of the Slavs
cb: Perun (the sky thunder god) counterbalances Mokosh (the earth goddess). The great Slavic polarity: sky (masculine) and earth (feminine).

do: Underworld, cattle, wealth, magic, poetry, the chthonic
at: The great Slavic underworld deity — god of cattle (earthly wealth), magic and the underworld. He is the chthonic counterpart to Perun. Associated with wisdom gained through the roots rather than the sky, and patron of bards and poets.
of: Cattle offerings, the last sheaf of grain left in the field for Veles, offerings at roots of great trees, mead
an: Bear, serpent, wolf
co: Black, dark green, deep forest shadow
dp: Saturday / Pluto and Wednesday / Mercury
cr: Black obsidian, dark smoky quartz, serpentine
bp: The root system of the body — bones, digestive tract, the deep underground of the physical
af: I draw wisdom from the roots. The knowledge beneath the earth is mine to receive.
sh: The underworld that swallows and doesn't return — the chthonic that takes without giving back
ar: The Chthonic Poet / The Root Wisdom
cb: Perun (sky, lightning, war, the high) counterbalances Veles (earth, magic, poetry, the low). The supreme Slavic cosmic polarity — their eternal conflict structures Slavic cosmology.

do: Thunder, lightning, war, oak, the sky, the law
at: The supreme Slavic thunder god — rides across the sky fighting the serpent Veles who steals his cattle. He is the upholder of cosmic law, the warrior who drives chaos underground. Associated with the oak tree and the eagle.
of: Oak wood burnt as offering, eagle imagery, thunderstorm ceremonies, warrior offerings
an: Eagle, bull, oak tree
co: Silver-blue, gold, deep blue-grey of storm clouds
dp: Thursday / Jupiter
cr: Blue kyanite, labradorite, grey-blue chalcedony, storm meteorite
bp: The right arm (the striking arm), adrenal glands, nervous system in warrior mode
af: I strike with precision and justice. I drive chaos underground. My law is the law of righteous action.
sh: The righteous warrior who becomes a bully — thunder without wisdom, law without compassion
ar: The Thunder Father / The Upholder of Law
cb: Veles (the underworld, the serpent, chaos below) counterbalances Perun (the sky, the eagle, order above). Their eternal conflict is Slavic cosmology's creative engine.

do: Abundance, protection, the cauldron of plenty, earth magic, fatherhood
at: The Good God of the Tuatha Dé Danann — his cauldron never empties, his club kills with one end and resurrects with the other, and his magical harp controls the seasons. He is the earth father — powerful, generous, a little comical in his enormous appetite.
of: The first harvest offered, food prepared with love as prayer, cauldron imagery, oak wood, ale
an: Horse, boar
co: Deep green, brown, gold
dp: Thursday / Jupiter
cr: Moss agate, green jasper, amber, brown tourmaline
bp: The belly (his enormous appetite — root of physical abundance), hands, body's strength
af: My cauldron is never empty. I give abundantly. I have enough and I am enough.
sh: The comically undignified aspect — the all-powerful father vulnerable to manipulation through his own desires
ar: The Generous Father / The Cauldron of Plenty
cb: The Morrigan (war, death, dark sovereignty) counterbalances the Dagda (life, abundance, generous power). Their union at Samhain is what protects Ireland for the coming year.

do: The terrifying aspect of Shiva, the guardian of sacred sites, time and death
at: The fierce form of Shiva who governs the root chakra's primal terror. He carries a skull cup and is accompanied by a dog. He guards the eight cardinal directions and the entrances to sacred sites. He is worshipped at cremation grounds. He burns away all that is false with absolute ruthlessness.
of: Red and black offerings, cremation ground ceremonies, midnight worship, fierce mantras
an: Dog (his vehicle — the untouchable made sacred), crow, serpent
co: Black, blood red, indigo
dp: Saturday / Saturn and Rahu (North Node)
cr: Black tourmaline, onyx, red garnet, smoky quartz
bp: The root chakra itself, the adrenal glands in their most primal state
af: I face the root of all fear. I stand at the cremation ground of my own illusions. What is false burns away.
sh: Primal terror without the wisdom to transmute it — the root chakra's darkness without liberation
ar: The Terror That Liberates / The Guardian of the Threshold
cb: Shiva in his peaceful aspect (the serene ascetic) counterbalances Bhairava (the terrifying guardian). The same divine force in its most gentle and most fierce faces.

do: Foundation, stability, the cosmic support beneath all creation, second avatar of Vishnu
at: Vishnu's second avatar — the great tortoise who supported the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan) on his shell. When the cosmic mountain sank during the churning, Kurma dove beneath to provide his shell as the stable foundation. He is the root chakra's cosmic principle of supporting what seems too heavy to bear.
of: Turtle imagery, blue and gold candles, ocean water ceremonies, churning meditations
an: Tortoise / Turtle
co: Blue-green, ocean teal, deep gold
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the cosmic foundation
cr: Ocean jasper, turquoise, blue-green fluorite, citrine
bp: The spine as the axis mundi, the back and shoulders bearing the cosmic load
af: I am the stable foundation beneath the churning. I support what others cannot. I endure.
sh: The weight-bearer who never asks for relief — the one who holds everything and collapses alone
ar: The Cosmic Foundation / The Support Beneath
cb: Ananta Shesha (the infinite serpent — the foundation in the cosmic ocean) — the tortoise below meets the serpent above. Earth and water as the two modes of cosmic support.

do: The coiled serpent power, the dormant fire at the base of the spine, the earth's intelligence in the body
at: The Kundalini — the coiled serpent of primal creative-sexual energy that rests at Muladhara, coiled 3.5 times around the central channel, and rises through the seven chakras when awakened. She is the root chakra's supreme intelligence — the earth's consciousness that lives in the body.
of: Root chakra meditations, Muladhara mantra (LAM), red candles, grounding practices, barefoot earth contact
an: Serpent (coiled 3.5 times around the central channel)
co: Red (the colour of Muladhara)
dp: Saturday / Saturn and Earth
cr: Red jasper, garnet, ruby, obsidian
bp: Base of spine, the perineum, the pelvic floor
af: LAM. I am rooted. The serpent within me is awakening. I am safe in my body on this earth.
sh: Kundalini risen without proper grounding — the fire that burns the unprepared vessel
ar: The Sleeping Serpent / The Earth's Intelligence in the Body
cb: Sahasrara (the crown chakra) — Kundalini (the root fire) rises through all seven chakras to unite with Shiva at the crown. Root counterbalanced by crown. Earth counterbalanced by cosmic consciousness.

do: Earth, morality, fertility, the dead, the law of the land
at: The supreme earth goddess of the Igbo people — the most important deity in the Igbo pantheon. She governs the earth, the moral law (ala-aru — the abomination), fertility, and the dead who are buried in her body. She is depicted holding a child. The land is her body. Her law is absolute.
of: Kola nuts, white clay (nzu), yam, palm wine, libations of palm oil poured on the earth, Mbari houses (elaborate clay sculptures built in her honour)
an: Python (sacred — pythons are not killed in Igbo tradition), tortoise
co: White and earth brown
dp: The earth itself has no day — she is every day
cr: White calcite, brown jasper, clear quartz
bp: Womb, the entire lower body, the bones, the dead in the earth
af: I live in accordance with the law of the earth. I am held in the arms of the earth mother. I honour what lies beneath.
sh: Ala-aru — the abomination — when her laws are broken the land becomes infertile and cursed
ar: The Earth Law / The Moral Ground
cb: Chukwu (the supreme god above) counterbalances Ala (the earth below). The supreme masculine sky principle balanced by the feminine earth law.

do: The earth, local protection, agriculture, community, the boundaries of place
at: The Earth God worshipped at every neighbourhood, village and field in Chinese folk religion. He is the divine administrator of a specific piece of earth — not a cosmic deity but the guardian of THIS field, THIS neighbourhood. He protects crops and communities.
of: Incense, fruit, rice, candles at the earth god shrine, prayers for the local community and crops
an: Tiger (his guardian animal in some regions)
co: Red and gold (prosperity and luck)
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Citrine, brown jasper, earth stones
bp: Feet (the earth we walk on), the bones, the local physical body
af: I am protected by the spirit of this earth, this place, this specific ground I walk on.
sh: The tutelary deity who becomes bureaucratic — the local god who merely administers without genuine care
ar: The Local Earth Guardian / The Spirit of Place
cb: Tian (Heaven/Sky) counterbalances Tu Di Gong (Earth). The celestial bureaucracy above counterbalanced by the terrestrial administration below.

do: The earth, the royal earth goddess, the yin principle of the earth
at: Hou Tu (Empress of the Earth) is the supreme earth goddess in Chinese cosmology — the counterpart of the Jade Emperor (heaven). She is one of the most ancient Chinese deities, receiving imperial worship for thousands of years. She represents the yin (receptive, feminine) principle of the earth itself.
of: Incense and offerings at earth altars, earth ceremonies at the equinoxes
an: Yellow ox (the animal of the earth in Chinese cosmology), dragon (as earth force)
co: Yellow (the colour of the earth in Chinese tradition), brown
dp: Saturday / Saturn
cr: Yellow jasper, citrine, amber
bp: The womb, the digestive system, the bones, the entire yin body
af: I receive all things with the patience of the earth. I am the yin principle that holds all life.
sh: The earth that has been forgotten — the divine feminine principle of the earth erased by sky-father religion
ar: The Yin Earth / The Imperial Earth Empress
cb: The Jade Emperor (Yù Huáng Dàdì — the supreme heaven deity) counterbalances Hou Tu (the supreme earth deity). Heaven and Earth — the great Chinese cosmological polarity.

do: The Great Spirit, the sky father, creation, law, initiations
at: The supreme creator god of the Kamilaroi and Wiradjuri peoples — the sky father who sits in his crystal throne in the sky watching over humanity. He sent totemic animals to teach the first people. He receives the dead. He initiated the first ceremonies. An extraordinarily complete deity largely erased from Western spiritual knowledge.
of: Ceremonies in country, drawings of the Milky Way (his camp), ochre marks on the body, respect for totemic animals
an: Eagle (his messenger), emu, kangaroo
co: White (his crystal throne), deep blue (the sky), ochre
dp: Sunday — the supreme sky father
cr: Clear quartz (crystal throne), white calcite, meteorite
bp: Crown, the eyes (he watches from above), the entire body in ceremony
af: The Great Spirit watches over me. I am held in the eye of the eternal witness. I am in country.
sh: The sky god who becomes the distant god — the father who watches but does not intervene as his children suffer
ar: The Great Witness / The Sky Father of the Oldest Tradition
cb: Rainbow Serpent (the earth/water creator) counterbalances Baiame (the sky creator). The most ancient version of the sky-earth polarity on Earth.

do: Sacred law, the pipe ceremony, the seven sacred Lakota rites, divine feminine teaching
at: The most sacred figure in Lakota spirituality — she appeared to two hunters, brought the sacred pipe (Chanunpa) and taught the Seven Sacred Rites, then transformed into a white buffalo calf and disappeared. The white buffalo calf is still considered among the most sacred signs in Lakota tradition.
of: The pipe ceremony (Chanunpa), sage, cedar, sweetgrass, the seven sacred rites performed with respect
an: White buffalo calf (her transformed form), eagle
co: White, sacred red (the pipe), blue (sky), yellow (earth), black (west)
dp: Sunday — the sacred feminine's most holy time
cr: White quartz, turquoise, white shell
bp: The entire body as a sacred ceremony, the mouth (the pipe), the breath
af: I carry the sacred law within me. My body is a ceremony. I walk in a sacred manner.
sh: The lust that caused one hunter's death — the shadow of not relating to the sacred feminine with respect
ar: The Sacred Law Bringer / The Pipe Woman
cb: The Great Spirit / Wakan Tanka counterbalances White Buffalo Calf Woman (the specific and personal divine feminine messenger). The infinite counterbalanced by the particular.

do: Creation, the web of life, wisdom, the connector of all things, the first teacher
at: Spider Grandmother — one of the most important creative deities in Hopi cosmology. She helped create the world by singing it into existence. She lives in the earth (the root) but her web connects everything (the crown). She is the guide of the newly dead and teacher of the first humans.
of: Spider webs left undisturbed, cornmeal offerings, prayer sticks (pahos), offerings made while acknowledging the connectedness of all life
an: Spider (she is the spider), eagle
co: Earth brown, grey-silver, web-white
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Obsidian (her dark wisdom), white quartz (the web of light), amber (preservation)
bp: Hands (weaving), the nervous system (the web of the body), spine
af: I am woven into the great web. Every connection is sacred. I spin wisdom from the earth.
sh: The web that traps rather than connects — the teacher whose wisdom becomes a cage
ar: The Web Weaver / The First Teacher
cb: Sotuknang (her nephew, the heart of the sky) counterbalances Spider Grandmother. The earth wisdom and the sky heart working together.

do: The cycles of nature, female power, fertility, the seasons, the sacred feminine across all traditions
at: The most revered deity in Navajo cosmology — she embodies the female life cycle and the cycle of the seasons. Young and beautiful in spring, mature in summer, wise and grey in autumn, she disappears in winter only to return young again. She created the Navajo people from her own skin. She taught the Kinaaldá ceremony.
of: Cornmeal, pollen, turquoise, shell, the Kinaaldá ceremony, acknowledgment of each season of a woman's life as sacred
an: Horse, eagle, deer
co: Turquoise and white shell (east/white, south/turquoise, west/yellow abalone, north/black jet)
dp: Monday / Moon
cr: Turquoise, white shell, yellow abalone, black jet (the four sacred stones of the four directions)
el: Earth / All elements across the seasons
bp: The entire female body across all cycles, the womb, the skin (she created people from her skin)
af: I am sacred in every season of my life. I change and remain myself. My cycles are my power.
sh: The woman who refuses to change — who clings to one season, refusing the next transformation
ar: The Sacred Cycles / The Woman Who Is Always Becoming
cb: Father Sky / Father Sun (her husband in some traditions) — the woman of earth's cycles counterbalanced by the eternal sky father.

do: The underworld (Ukhu Pacha), the dead, underground minerals
at: The Incan deity of the underworld — Ukhu Pacha (the inner world beneath the earth). He governs the dead and the rich mineral wealth that lies underground. The Spanish colonisers identified him with the Devil, corrupting his original role as the necessary divine principle of the underground world.
of: Offerings buried in the earth, first fruits given underground, chicha poured into the earth
an: Serpent, lizard, underground creatures
co: Black, deep earth brown, mineral grey
dp: Saturday / Pluto
cr: Obsidian, coal, dark garnet
bp: Bones, the deepest root structures, the mineral body
af: I honour the underground world. The earth's depths are sacred. I claim the wealth that lies beneath.
sh: The colonial demonisation of indigenous underground gods — the suppression of Andean spirituality
ar: The Andean Underworld / The Inner Earth
cb: Inti (the sun above) counterbalances Supay (the earth below). The Andean vertical axis: Hanan Pacha (upper) / Kay Pacha (middle) / Ukhu Pacha (inner).

do: The earth itself, the world below heaven, the primordial ground
at: Ki is the earth goddess of Sumerian cosmology — the earth that was separated from An (Heaven) at the beginning of creation. In the oldest Sumerian texts, An-Ki (Heaven-Earth) was the primordial couple from whom all creation emerged. Ki is one of the most ancient named deities in human history. She was increasingly erased as Ninhursag and then male earth deities were promoted.
of: Soil ceremonies, the honouring of the earth itself, the first planting of each season
an: All earth-dwelling creatures — she is the earth they inhabit
co: Deep brown, dark green, rich earth ochre
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Dark brown jasper, obsidian, pyrite, petrified wood
bp: The entire body as earth — bones as mountains, blood as rivers, flesh as soil
af: I am as old as the earth itself. My body and the earth body are one. I am ancient ground.
sh: The earth that has been entirely forgotten — nature as merely backdrop rather than sacred living being
ar: The Primordial Earth / The Ground of All
cb: An (Heaven) — Ki (Earth) and An (Heaven) are the primordial division from which all creation emerges. The great Sumerian cosmological polarity.

do: The earth, the foundation of the home, the ground upon which life is built
at: Onile means 'Owner of the Earth' — the earth goddess of the Yoruba tradition, particularly associated with the Ogboni secret society. She is called upon to witness oaths and contracts. The land is her body. Her law is invoked at the deepest moments of commitment.
of: Soil, cowrie shells, the Edan (Ogboni brass figure), libations to the earth, oaths sworn upon the earth
an: All creatures of the earth, the tortoise
co: Earth brown, dark red
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Dark jasper, brown tourmaline, iron ore
bp: Feet, legs, bones, the physical foundation of the body
af: I stand upon the earth that owns me. I make my vows with the earth as my witness. My foundation is sacred.
sh: The earth that has been violated — the consequence of broken oaths to the earth goddess
ar: The Earth Witness / The Foundation of Vows
cb: Olodumare (the supreme above) counterbalances Onile (the earth below). Also the sky Orishas counterbalance the earth-dwelling Onile.

do: The green face of resurrection — the deity of vegetation who dies each dry season and is reborn with the flood
at: Osiris in his most ancient, root aspect — the grain god whose green skin is the colour of vegetation resurrected from the earth. His death and resurrection is the myth of the agricultural cycle: the grain planted in the earth (buried/dead), the grain sprouting (resurrected). He IS the earth's regenerative power.
of: Green candles, grain buried in the earth, Nile water poured on the ground, germinating grain placed on the altar
an: Bull (Apis bull), scarab beetle (rolling its ball of dung/new life like the sun is rolled)
co: Green (the resurrection colour), black (the rich Nile soil)
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the cycle of death and growth
cr: Green jasper, malachite, emerald
el: Earth / Water (the Nile flood that resurrects the land)
bp: The spine (the Djed pillar — the backbone of Osiris = the axis of resurrection), the bones
af: From the earth I was buried in, I am rising. The grain within me is ready to sprout. I am the resurrection of the earth.
sh: The buried seed that refuses to germinate — the self that was put in the ground but will not allow itself to be reborn
ar: The Grain God / The Green Resurrection
cb: Set (the desert, the dry season, the force that kills vegetation) counterbalances Osiris (the Nile flood, the growing season, the resurrection of vegetation). Drought counterbalanced by the green return.

do: Creation, death, the divine feminine creative principle, the first underworld queen
at: Izanami ('She who invites') and her consort Izanagi created the Japanese islands and the Shinto gods. When she died giving birth to the fire god, Izanagi descended to the underworld and fled in horror at her rotting body. Their story is Japan's telling of the descent myth.
of: Offerings to both the living and dead, honouring of grief as sacred, salt (purification), water
an: Wagtail, crane, turtle
co: White and black, the colours of the living and the dead
dp: Monday and Saturday
cr: Pearl, clear quartz, black obsidian
el: All elements — they created all elements
bp: The entire body — she and Izanagi created human bodies
af: I honour the great polarity within me. I contain both the will to create and the acceptance of death.
sh: The divorce of the divine couple — Izanagi's rejection of Izanami in her death-form created the eternal enmity between life and death
ar: The Divine Mother of Creation / The First Underworld Queen
cb: Izanagi (her consort — life, sky, the living) — they ARE the supreme polarity. Life and death in eternal tension and relationship.

do: Healing, medicine, the healer of warriors, the divine physician of Asgard
at: Eir is the Norse goddess of healing — one of the handmaidens of Frigg, she is specifically the divine physician. She is associated with the healing of battle wounds and with the sacred knowledge of medicinal herbs. She sits on Lyfjaberg (Healing Mountain) and heals all who climb to reach her.
of: Healing herbs gathered and offered, prayers for healing, water from sacred healing springs
an: Crane (the healer's bird), swan
co: White (the healer's colour), green (the herbs of healing)
dp: Friday / Venus — healing and love
cr: Green calcite, malachite, green agate, healing quartz
bp: Hands (the healer's instruments), heart (compassion), the entire body in its capacity for healing
af: The divine physician moves through my hands. Healing is the earth's gift. I am restored.
sh: The healer who cannot heal herself — the goddess of healing who bears her own wound in silence
ar: The Divine Healer / The Mountain of Medicine
cb: The Morrigan (who wounds on the battlefield) counterbalances Eir (who heals after the battle). Wounding and healing as the great battlefield polarity in Norse tradition.

do: The night, the darkness that preceded creation, the mother of sleep and death
at: Nyx (Night) is one of the most ancient of Greek deities — she emerged from Chaos at the beginning of creation and gave birth to Erebus (Darkness). Even Zeus feared to anger her. She is the mother of Sleep (Hypnos) and Death (Thanatos), of the Fates (Moirai), of Strife (Eris), of Dreams, and of many other primal forces. She drives her chariot across the sky each night and the stars follow.
of: Black candles, nighttime offerings, star-watching ceremonies, offerings made at midnight
an: Owl (the bird of night), bat
co: Black, deep purple, midnight blue studded with stars
dp: Monday (the night of the week) / Pluto / The Moon's dark phase
cr: Black moonstone, jet, black obsidian, dark labradorite
el: Ether / Earth (the darkness that is both cosmic and chthonic)
bp: The entire body in sleep (she governs sleep), the pineal gland (the body's light-receptor in darkness), the unconscious
af: I am not afraid of the dark. Night is the mother of all things. The darkness holds as much as the light.
sh: The night that swallows rather than rests — the darkness that absorbs consciousness rather than restoring it
ar: The Primordial Night / The Mother of All Dark Things
cb: Hemera (Day — her daughter and opposite) counterbalances Nyx (Night). The primordial Greek day-night polarity. Also Eos (the dawn) counterbalances Nyx — the night always answered by the dawn.

do: The deep darkness, the underworld's darkness, the primordial dark
at: Erebus emerged from Chaos at the same time as Nyx (Night) — he is the deep darkness, the primordial dark void of the underworld. He and Nyx together are the first pair of the cosmos — the darkness of the underworld and the darkness of the night sky. He is largely forgotten in favour of his more famous consort.
of: Black offerings, void meditation, the honouring of the absolute dark
an: Bat, owl
co: Absolute black
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the deepest dark
cr: Jet, black obsidian, coal
bp: The darkest structures of the body, the unconscious below the unconscious
af: I am not afraid of the void. Darkness preceded light. The deep dark holds the seeds of all creation.
sh: The darkness without the light that will follow it — the void that is not a womb but a tomb
ar: The Primordial Dark / The Void
cb: Nyx (his consort — Night, which has stars and cycles) counterbalances Erebus (the absolute dark with no stars and no cycles). The visible darkness counterbalanced by the absolute darkness.

do: Filth and purification, sexual transgression and its release, the steam bath, sin-eating
at: The Aztec goddess of filth and purification — she governs sexual transgression but also its forgiveness. Called Tlaelquani ('Eater of Filth') because she devours all sins, particularly sexual ones. The shadow of Xochiquetzal — the filth beneath the flower.
of: Broom (her sacred instrument), offerings at the temazcal (steam bath), cotton fibre
an: Owl, spider
co: Black and red
dp: Saturday / Moon (her dark phase)
cr: Obsidian, red jasper
bp: Reproductive organs, the sacral region, the skin
af: I release what I am ashamed of. I confess to the one who devours shame. I am made clean.
sh: The shame that never confesses — the sexual wound that festers because it is never spoken
ar: The Sacred Purifier / The Sin-Eater
cb: Xochiquetzal (beauty, flowers, love, the flower of sex) counterbalances Tlazolteotl (filth, shame, the devouring of sexual transgression). Beauty and shame, the flower and the steam bath.

do: The sea, the otherworld, magic, shapeshifting, the land of eternal youth (Tír na nÓg)
at: The Irish sea god and ruler of the otherworld — he rides his horse across the waves as if they were solid land. A shapeshifter and illusionist of supreme skill. He guards the entrance to Tír na nÓg (the Land of Youth).
of: Salt water, sea shells, offerings at the ocean shore on the solstices, mead, silver coins cast into the sea
an: Pig (his magical pigs resurrect each morning), seagull, seal
co: Sea-blue, silver, green
dp: Monday / Neptune and Mercury
cr: Aquamarine, ocean jasper, moonstone, sea glass
bp: The sacral (the sea within), the entire fluid body, the mind as the sea of possibilities
af: I ride the waves of life with ease. The otherworld is as real as this world. I shapeshift with the tides.
sh: The shape-shifter who loses his own form — the magical trickster who forgets which face is real
ar: The Wave-Rider / The Otherworld King
cb: The Dagda (the earth father, the practical and grounded) counterbalances Manannán (the sea lord, the magical and fluid). Earth and sea as the Irish cosmic polarity.

do: Creation of humanity, marriage, the rainbow, the restoration of the sky, music
at: One of the supreme creator deities of Chinese mythology — she created humanity by moulding figures from yellow clay. When Gonggong the water god broke the sky, she repaired it with five-coloured stones. She is also the goddess of marriage, matchmaking, and the sacred union of male and female.
of: Clay offerings, five-coloured stones, rainbow imagery, prayers for good marriages, music
an: Serpent (her body is half-serpent), phoenix
co: Five colours (the five elements), red, gold
dp: Friday / Venus — marriage and creation
cr: Five-coloured stones (jasper in various colours), moonstone
bp: The womb (she created humans), the hands (she moulded them), the sacral creative centre
af: I am the creator of my own life. I repair what has been broken. I bring souls together.
sh: The creation that is imperfect — Nü Wa's rope-people were less carefully made than her clay nobles, creating inequality within her own creation
ar: The Creator of Humanity / The Sky-Repairer
cb: Fuxi (her brother-consort, the sage king who taught humanity the arts and sciences) counterbalances Nü Wa (the goddess who created humanity and repaired the cosmos). Creation counterbalanced by civilisation.

do: Desire, love, the creative fire of attraction, the spring, sensual pleasure
at: The Hindu god of desire and love — analogous to Eros/Cupid. He carries a bow made of sugarcane strung with a line of bees, his arrows tipped with five flowers. He was burnt to ashes by Shiva's third eye when he interrupted Shiva's meditation. He was later restored.
of: Sugarcane, honey, jasmine flowers, rose offerings, ceremonies of love and marriage
an: Parrot (his vehicle), fish, cuckoo bird
co: Red, pink, gold, spring green
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Pink tourmaline, rose quartz, pink sapphire
bp: The sacral plexus, the heart, the sense organs as the seat of pleasure
af: My desire is sacred. I allow the arrows of love to find their mark. I am open to the spring.
sh: The desire that caused the divine wrath — the sacral fire that dares to disturb the highest contemplation
ar: The Arrow of Sacred Desire / The Spring God
cb: Shiva (who burnt him to ashes with his third eye) counterbalances Kamadeva (whose arrow interrupted Shiva's meditation). Desire counterbalanced by the withdrawal of desire in meditation.

do: Waters, fertility, healing, wisdom, the sacred feminine of Persian tradition
at: THE FORGOTTEN PERSIAN GODDESS. Anahita is the great goddess of the waters of Persian tradition — a deity of immense importance in ancient Iran who was later assimilated into Greek (Aphrodite) and Armenian traditions. She governs fertility, healing, wisdom and the life-giving waters. Her name means 'the immaculate one.'
of: Water offerings, flowers (especially white flowers), healing herbs, pure spring water
an: Peacock, dove, fish
co: White, gold, water-blue
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Aquamarine, white opal, moonstone, rose quartz
bp: Womb, kidneys, the heart's watery nourishment
af: The sacred waters of compassion flow through me. I am immaculate in my love. My heart is clean.
sh: The immaculate ideal — the love that cannot be reached because it is too pure for the human world
ar: The Sacred Waters of Love / The Immaculate Feminine
cb: Ahura Mazda (the supreme masculine light principle) counterbalances Anahita (the sacred feminine waters). Also Mithra (the solar covenant) counterbalances Anahita (the watery compassion).

do: The sun, war, the south, the hummingbird of the south, sustaining the solar cycle
at: The supreme solar war god of the Aztec — he was born fully armed from his mother Coatlicue to immediately defeat his siblings (the moon and stars). He must be fed with human hearts to sustain his daily journey across the sky.
of: Historically: human hearts offered to feed the sun's journey. Modern: red candles, solar ceremonies, offerings of courage and commitment
an: Eagle (the sun's warrior bird), hummingbird (his spirit is in the hummingbird)
co: Blue and yellow (his sacred colours)
dp: Sunday / Sun
cr: Gold, sunstone, red jasper, fire opal
bp: Solar plexus (the warrior's centre), the adrenal glands, the heart (literally consumed)
af: I sustain my solar journey with the offering of my highest effort. I will not let my inner sun die.
sh: The price of sustaining one's own power through the consumption of others
ar: The Solar Warrior / The Sun That Must Be Fed
cb: Tlaloc (rain, water, earth's nourishment) counterbalances Huitzilopochtli (solar fire, war). Also Quetzalcoatl (civilisation, the peaceful feathered serpent) counterbalances Huitzilopochtli (war, sacrifice, the violent sun).

do: The sun, light, the sacred covenant, truth, justice, friendship
at: The solar deity of the Persians — Mithra governs the sun, truth and the sacred covenant. He is the divine witness of all agreements. In the Roman mystery religion of Mithraism, he slays the sacred bull (Tauroctony). His birth on December 25th was one of the most important solar festivals of the ancient world.
of: Fire ceremonies at dawn, the sacred covenant offered, bull imagery (the Tauroctony), sacred meals shared among initiates
an: Bull (the cosmic bull he slays), raven (his messenger), dog, snake
co: Gold, red, solar yellow
dp: Sunday / Sun — the solar covenant keeper
cr: Sunstone, citrine, gold topaz, yellow jasper
bp: Solar plexus, the eyes (he is the god of light), the right hand (the hand of covenant)
af: I keep my covenants. I witness my own truth. The sun within me rises on the side of justice.
sh: The secret initiation — the solar power that only reveals itself to the initiated, excluding the uninitiated
ar: The Solar Covenant / The Light of Truth
cb: Ahriman (the Zoroastrian principle of darkness and evil) counterbalances Mithra (the solar principle of light and truth). In Persian cosmology the supreme cosmic polarity is Ahura Mazda (light) vs Ahriman (darkness).

do: Compassion, swift action for liberation, the mother who protects all beings
at: Green Tara — the Bodhisattva who vowed to achieve enlightenment in female form and to remain in the world until all beings are liberated. She acts in the time it takes to blink. Her mantra Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha is one of the most widely used in Tibetan Buddhism.
of: Green candles, green foods, Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha 108 times, white lotus, turquoise
an: Lotus (she sits on it), the animals she protects
co: Green, gold
dp: Friday / Venus and Monday / Moon
cr: Emerald, green tourmaline, jade, malachite, turquoise
bp: Heart, thymus gland, the immune system of love, the right foot (extended in readiness to act)
af: Om Tare Tuttare Ture Svaha. I act swiftly from compassion. I am the green light of the heart.
sh: Compassion fatigue — the bodhisattva who burns out; the heart that cares for all while neglecting itself
ar: The Compassionate Action / The Swift Heart
cb: White Tara (longevity, peaceful compassion) counterbalances Green Tara (swift action). Also Mahakala (fierce protector, wrathful wisdom) counterbalances Green Tara's compassion.

do: Love, beauty, flowers, art, sexuality, weaving, the heart's creative pleasures
at: The Aztec Venus — goddess of love and beauty, associated with the planet Venus and all the heart-opening pleasures of existence. She governs romantic love, artistic creation, the sensory beauty that the heart delights in.
of: Flowers (marigolds, roses), butterflies, chocolate, gold, beautiful fabric, art-making as prayer
an: Hummingbird, butterfly, quetzal bird
co: Red, orange, gold, vivid tropical flower colours
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Jade, rose quartz, carnelian, turquoise
bp: Heart, womb, hands (for creating beauty), the senses
af: I create beauty as a form of prayer. My heart opens to what is beautiful. Love is sacred.
sh: Tlazolteotl — the dark goddess of filth who swallows sin, Xochiquetzal's shadow self
ar: The Flower Heart / The Sacred Artist
cb: Tlazolteotl (filth, purging, shadow of beauty) — the flower goddess counterbalanced by the purger. Beauty counterbalanced by the necessity of purification.

do: The love that celebrates existence, joy as devotion, the music that opens the heart
at: Hathor at the heart chakra — the principle that joy is a form of spiritual practice. Her worship was explicitly celebratory: music, dance, wine, sexual expression were all forms of Hathor devotion. She teaches that opening to pleasure and beauty is a form of prayer.
of: Music and dance as sacred offerings, flowers (especially lotus), wine shared in celebration, sistrum rattles
an: Cow (she wears the cow horns of the loving mother), falcon
co: Gold, turquoise, the warm red of loving fire
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Turquoise, rose quartz, carnelian, amber
bp: Heart, breasts, the sense organs in their pleasure-receiving mode
af: My heart opens through joy. Celebrating beauty is my prayer. Love is everywhere I look for it.
sh: The grief when joy is refused — Hathor becomes Sekhmet when her love is rejected
ar: The Open Heart / The Sacred Celebrant
cb: Sekhmet (the wrath when the loving heart is wounded) counterbalances Hathor (the joy when love is given freely). They are the same goddess — love and wrath, the two faces of the heart.

do: Eloquence, language, writing, the Ogham script, strength through speech
at: The Celtic god of eloquence and writing — he invented the Ogham script (the sacred Celtic alphabet). He is depicted leading willing followers by thin chains attached to his tongue — meaning people are bound willingly by the power of his eloquent speech.
of: Written offerings in Ogham, speech given as prayer, poetry offered aloud
an: Bee (the honey of eloquence), chain (his chain of eloquence)
co: Gold, deep green, the green of holly (an Ogham tree)
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the eloquent word
cr: Green jasper, amber (words preserved like insects in amber)
bp: Tongue, lips, vocal cords, the chain connecting throat to listener
af: My words have more power than my muscles. Eloquence is strength. I chain hearts with truth beautifully spoken.
sh: The eloquence that manipulates — the words so beautiful they lead people away from their own judgment
ar: The Eloquent Champion / The Chain of Beautiful Speech
cb: The Morrigan (war, the wordless battle cry) counterbalances Ogma (eloquence, the word). Also counterbalanced by Cernunnos (the wild that has no human language).

do: Writing, mathematics, architecture, the recording of time, the library of the gods
at: The forgotten twin of Thoth — the goddess of writing, measurement and record-keeping. She wore a leopard skin and a star-pointed crown. She stretched the cord for the foundation of every temple, measured the years of each pharaoh's reign, and kept the library of the gods. Almost entirely eclipsed by Thoth.
of: Pens and writing implements, mathematical instruments, records of what is true (written as offering), leopard imagery
an: Leopard (her skin), the seven-pointed star (her crown)
co: Black, white, the starry colours of night
dp: Wednesday / Saturn — she measures and records the passage of time
cr: Black tourmaline (the grounding of precision), clear quartz (the crystal of measurement)
bp: Hands (for writing and measurement), third eye (for precision), the tongue (for recording)
af: I record what is true. I measure what matters. My records are precise and my foundations are true.
sh: The forgotten recorder — Seshat's shadow is the divine feminine knowledge that was attributed to Thoth and forgotten
ar: The Divine Architect / The Forgotten Measurer
cb: Thoth (who speaks divine truth) counterbalances Seshat (who records and measures it). Speech and record. The oral tradition counterbalanced by the written tradition.

do: Divine discernment, the vision that distinguishes what must be preserved from what must change
at: Vishnu at the third eye — the principle of divine discernment (viveka). His Sudarshana Chakra (the spinning discus) is the weapon of perfect discrimination — it cuts away what is false with absolute precision.
of: Tulsi (holy basil), Vishnu Sahasranama, blue and gold offerings, the spinning discus meditated upon
an: Garuda the eagle (the vehicle of pure discriminating vision), the Shesha serpent
co: Blue (the infinite sky and ocean), gold
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — divine discernment and the dharmic vision
cr: Blue sapphire, lapis lazuli, indigo kyanite
bp: Third eye, pineal gland, the two physical eyes as instruments of divine discernment
af: I see what must be preserved and what must change. My discernment is perfect. I know the difference between what is eternal and what is passing.
sh: The preserver who cannot discern when it is time for Shiva's destruction to come
ar: The Discriminating Eye / The Avatar-Discerner
cb: Shiva (who destroys without discrimination) counterbalances Vishnu (who preserves through precise discernment). The eye that distinguishes counterbalanced by the fire that does not.

do: The highest of all angels, the scribe of God, the guardian of the crown, Metatron's Cube
at: The supreme archangel of the Kabbalistic tradition — said to have been the prophet Enoch, so beloved by God that he was taken into heaven and transformed into an angel. He sits at the throne of God and records all human actions. Metatron's Cube is the sacred geometric pattern containing all five Platonic solids.
of: Sacred geometry drawn in his honour, white and violet candles, the pursuit of divine wisdom, meditation on Metatron's Cube
an: None — he is beyond animal correspondence
co: White, violet, gold
dp: Sunday / Uranus — the crown, the highest plane
cr: Clear quartz (especially Metatron's Cube carved in crystal), selenite, apophyllite
bp: Crown, the entire light body, the merkaba
af: I am recorded in the divine record exactly as I am. My entire journey has been witnessed. I am known to the highest.
sh: The impossible standard — Metatron represents such cosmic perfection that his shadow is the human sense of never being enough
ar: The Divine Scribe / The Crown Guardian
cb: Sandalphon (Metatron's twin, the earthly twin who collects human prayers) counterbalances Metatron (the supreme heavenly principle). The highest heaven counterbalanced by the earthly bridge.

do: The Great Spirit, the Great Mystery, the all-encompassing divine, the totality of the sacred
at: The supreme divine principle of the Lakota people — 'Wakan Tanka' means 'the Great Mystery' or 'the Great Spirit.' It is not a personal god but an all-encompassing mystery that permeates everything. Every creature, stone, river, and being is wakan (sacred) because Wakan Tanka permeates all.
of: The pipe ceremony (Chanunpa), sage, sweetgrass, cedar, prayer ties, ceremony performed with full heart
an: All animals — all are sacred expressions of Wakan Tanka. Particularly the eagle (the messenger), the buffalo (the sustainer), the bear
co: All colours of the four directions — white (north), red (east), yellow (south), black (west) — together as the whole
dp: Sunday / All celestial bodies
cr: All sacred stones — Wakan Tanka permeates all minerals. Specifically: quartz, turquoise, obsidian
el: All elements — the Great Mystery permeates all
bp: Crown, the entire body as a sacred ceremony, every breath as prayer
af: All is sacred. All is connected. I walk in a sacred manner. The Great Mystery breathes through me.
sh: The absence of the sacred — the world from which Wakan Tanka has been drained; the desacralised universe of pure materialism
ar: The Great Mystery / The Sacred Permeating All
cb: Iktomi (the spider trickster of Lakota mythology — who weaves the web of illusion) counterbalances Wakan Tanka (the Great Mystery, the sacred reality beneath all illusion). The sacred counterbalanced by the illusory.

do: The primordial serpent, creation, the cosmic axis, the ancient wisdom beyond words
at: Damballa Wedo is the great serpent Lwa — one of the most ancient and revered in the Haitian Vodou pantheon. He represents the cosmic serpent: the primordial creative force connecting earth (root) to heaven (crown). He rarely speaks in words — he communicates in hisses and the movement of his serpent body.
of: White foods (eggs, white rice, white cornmeal), milk, pure water, no alcohol (he does not drink), white candles, white cloth laid on the floor
an: Serpent (he IS the great white serpent), dove
co: White (pure white — the most ancient and most pure)
dp: Thursday / Mercury — the ancient wisdom
cr: White quartz, selenite, white calcite
el: Ether / Water / Earth — the cosmic axis connects all elements
bp: The entire spine (the serpent = the Kundalini axis), crown, root
af: I am connected from the deepest earth to the highest heaven. The ancient wisdom does not need words. I rest in the primordial.
sh: The silence that cannot communicate — the Lwa so ancient and so pure that he cannot relate to ordinary human consciousness
ar: The Cosmic Serpent / The Primordial Connection
cb: Ogou (fire, iron, the warrior — everything hot, hard, and modern) counterbalances Damballa (water, the serpent, the ancient — everything cool, fluid, and primordial). The oldest and the newest.

do: The supreme truth, light, the uncreated divine, the source of all good
at: The supreme deity of Zoroastrianism — 'Wise Lord.' He is the uncreated, the eternal, the source of all light and truth. He created the world through his divine thought (Vohu Mana) and his word (Asha — the principle of truth). Zoroastrianism was the world's first monotheistic religion and influenced Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
of: The sacred fire (the fire temple is his domain — the sacred fire that must never go out), offerings of truth and righteous action, frankincense
an: Fire (the living symbol of his presence)
co: White (pure light), gold (divine wisdom), the colours of the sacred fire
dp: Sunday / Sun — the supreme divine light
cr: Clear quartz, topaz, sunstone, gold
bp: Crown, the entire being in its righteous form, the mind (Vohu Mana — good thought)
af: I think good thoughts, speak good words, perform good deeds. The Wise Lord illuminates my path. I am the fire of truth.
sh: Angra Mainyu (the destructive principle within) — the shadow of Ahura Mazda is the force that opposes him in every human soul
ar: The Wise Lord / The Source of All Light
cb: Angra Mainyu / Ahriman (the destructive spirit, the principle of darkness and evil) counterbalances Ahura Mazda (the supreme light). The supreme cosmic polarity of Zoroastrianism — good versus evil, light versus darkness, truth versus falsehood.

do: The supreme mystery, the father of all Orishas, the cosmic source of Yoruba creation
at: Oduduwa descended from the heavens on a chain, carrying a calabash of sand and a five-toed hen, and created the earth at Ile-Ife. He is the ancestor of all Yoruba kings. He represents the crown principle within the Yoruba system: the divine whose mystery cannot be fully penetrated.
of: White offerings, the veneration of Yoruba kingship and ancestry, the tracing of royal lineage, ceremonies at Ile-Ife
an: The five-toed hen (his creation tools), pigeon
co: White (the colour of the divine in Yoruba cosmology)
dp: Sunday — the supreme day of the crown
cr: White quartz, white onyx, ivory
bp: Crown, the entire being
af: I stand on the ground created by the divine. My royal ancestry reaches back to the source. I carry the first light within me.
sh: The remoteness of the supreme — the creator who retreated to the crown and left the earth to be governed by his children
ar: The Cosmic Ancestor / The Crown of Yoruba Creation
cb: Eshu/Elegua (the immediate, the trickster, the earthly crossroads) counterbalances Oduduwa (the supreme crown). The vertical axis: the most supreme counterbalanced by the most immediate.

do: The supreme creator, the source of all life, that which cannot be named or depicted
at: The supreme deity across many Bantu-speaking peoples of central and southern Africa — including the Bakongo, the Mongo, and many others. 'Nzambi' is the name for God across an enormous swathe of central and southern Africa. So supreme that prayer is directed to the Bakulu (ancestors) as intermediaries.
of: Righteous living, ancestral veneration (the Bakulu as intermediaries), offerings of food and respect to the ancestors who carry the prayers upward
an: None — Nzambi is beyond all animal representation
co: White (the colour of the divine and the ancestors in Bakongo tradition)
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: White chalk (mpemba — the most sacred substance in Bakongo cosmology), clear quartz
bp: The crown, the entire being, the breath as the gift of Nzambi
af: I am created by the unknowable. The ancestors carry my prayers to the supreme. I am held by all that came before me.
sh: The supreme so remote that the everyday human connection to the divine is lost
ar: The Unknowable Supreme / The Bantu Divine Source
cb: The Bakulu (the ancestors — the immediate mediators between Nzambi and the living) counterbalance Nzambi (the remote supreme). The infinite source counterbalanced by the intimate dead.

do: The supreme god of Haitian Vodou, too great for direct approach, the source behind the Lwa
at: 'Bondye' is the Haitian Creole word for God (from French 'Bon Dieu' — Good God). In Vodou theology, Bondye is the supreme, unknowable divine being — too great and too distant to be approached directly by humans. The Lwa serve as the intermediaries.
of: The living of a righteous life, serving the Lwa faithfully (which honours Bondye), prayer, community service
an: None — Bondye is beyond all representation
co: White (the colour of the supreme in Vodou theology)
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: Clear quartz, white calcite
bp: Crown, the entire being
af: The supreme source knows me. I am held by what is beyond my understanding. I serve the spirits who serve the supreme.
sh: The supreme so remote that the everyday divine connection is maintained only through the Lwa
ar: The Supreme Behind the Lwa / The Distant Divine
cb: The Lwa themselves (Erzulie, Ogou, Baron Samedi, Damballa, etc.) counterbalance Bondye — the supreme counterbalanced by the immediate, the distant divine counterbalanced by the intimate spiritual forces.

do: The supreme God of the Igbo, the great Chi, the source of all individual divine flames
at: The supreme deity of the Igbo people — 'Chukwu' means 'Great Chi' (the Great God). He is the source from which every individual's Chi (personal divine flame) derives. He is the sun behind the Chi — the infinite source from which each person's unique divine spark is drawn.
of: Prayer, righteous living, the honouring of one's Chi (personal divine flame), morning acknowledgment of Chukwu
an: None — he is beyond animal representation
co: White (the colour of divine purity in Igbo tradition)
dp: Sunday — the supreme day
cr: Clear quartz, white calcite
bp: Crown, the entire being, the ori (the head as the seat of personal divine connection to Chukwu)
af: I am a flame of the Great God. Chukwu lives in me as my Chi. The source of all fires burns within me.
sh: The gap between the supreme and the personal — the moment when the individual Chi loses its sense of connection to Chukwu
ar: The Source of All Personal Divine Flames / The Great Chi
cb: Ala (the earth goddess, the immediate, the physical law) counterbalances Chukwu (the supreme sky god, the distant, the cosmic law). Sky and earth in their Igbo expression.

do: The primordial giant from whose body the world was made
at: The first being in Norse cosmology — from his body Odin and his brothers created the world: his flesh became the earth, his blood the sea, his bones the mountains, his skull the sky.
of: Offerings made by standing on bare earth acknowledging the body beneath your feet
an: Aurochs (primordial cattle)
co: Ice-white, flesh-pink, earth brown
dp: Saturday / Saturn
cr: Granite, flint, bone
bp: The entire physical body — every stone and mountain is his bone
af: My body is the world. The earth beneath me is sacred flesh. I am made of cosmic matter.
sh: The unconscious giant — the one who does not know his own body will become the world
ar: The World Body / The Primordial Flesh
cb: Odin (who shaped Ymir's body into the world) counterbalances Ymir (the raw material). The creator counterbalanced by the created-from.

do: The earth, fertility, the Roman sacred land, the productive ground
at: The Roman earth goddess — also called Terra Mater. She is the divine personification of the productive earth. She was invoked in oaths, treaties and in ploughing ceremonies.
of: First fruits of the harvest, ploughing ceremonies, incense burned at the earth altar
an: Serpent (the earth's wisdom), cow, pig
co: Green, earth brown, ochre
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Green jasper, terracotta, brown tourmaline
bp: The womb, the digestive system, the bones
af: The sacred earth of this land holds me. I give and I receive in equal measure.
sh: The earth depleted by over-extraction — the mother who has given more than she can restore
ar: The Productive Earth / The Sacred Roman Land
cb: Jupiter (the sky father, the rain-giver) counterbalances Tellus Mater (the earth mother). Rain and earth — the fundamental Roman agricultural polarity.

do: The harvest, nursing, fate given at birth, the granary serpent goddess
at: The cobra goddess of the harvest — she is the serpent who inhabits the granary and protects the grain. She is also the goddess who nurses the pharaoh and speaks the name that determines a child's fate at birth.
of: Grain offerings, snake imagery in the granary, first fruits left at the threshold
an: Cobra, lioness (her alternate form)
co: Golden yellow, grain-gold, green
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Yellow jasper, citrine, harvest-gold stones
bp: The womb (nursing), the mouth (fate-giving), the stomach (the harvest-fed body)
af: I am fed by the sacred harvest. My name carries my fate. The serpent in the granary is my ally.
sh: The blight — Renenutet's shadow is the cobra whose venom destroys the harvest
ar: The Harvest Serpent / The Name-Giver
cb: Apophis (the chaos serpent) counterbalances Renenutet (the order-keeping serpent of the harvest).

do: Abundance, wealth, the earth's resources, the harvest's riches
at: The Roman goddess of abundance and the earth's resources — her name gives us 'opulent.' She was the consort of Saturn. She was worshipped with hands touching the earth, since her gifts came from the ground.
of: Hands placed on the bare earth in worship, grain and fruit offerings, the first harvest's wealth dedicated to her
an: None — she is the earth's abundance itself
co: Gold, earth tones, the colours of autumn harvest
dp: Saturday / Saturn
cr: Citrine, amber, yellow jasper, gold pyrite
bp: The hands (she is touched through hands on earth), the belly (abundance)
af: The earth's abundance is my birthright. I receive its gifts with open hands. I am opulent with the earth's wealth.
sh: The over-abundance that wastes — the granary so full it rots rather than being shared
ar: The Abundant Earth / The Wealth of the Ground
cb: Saturn (her consort — time, the harvest's boundary) counterbalances Ops (the abundance). Plenty counterbalanced by the limit of the season.

do: The earth rising, the first mound of creation, the fertile earth emerging from the primordial waters
at: Tatenen ('the exalted earth') is the god of the first mound of earth that rose from the primordial waters at the beginning of creation — the Benben mound. He is the earth in its most creative aspect: the first solid ground rising from the flood. He later merged with Ptah to become Ptah-Tatenen.
of: Grain sprouting in water, seeds planted in the first earth of the growing season
an: Serpent (the earth's depth), ram
co: Green (the first vegetation), dark brown (the fertile earth)
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Dark green malachite, fertile soil, brown jasper
bp: The base of the spine (the first rising mound), the legs (the rising earth column)
af: I am the first earth rising from the waters. New ground forms beneath my feet. I am the emergence of solid foundation.
sh: The earth that sinks back into the flood — the ground that was gained and then lost
ar: The First Mound / The Rising Earth
cb: Nun (the primordial waters from which Tatenen rises) counterbalances Tatenen (the earth that rises). Water and earth in their creative polarity.

do: Corn, agriculture, self-sacrifice for the community, the sacred sustenance
at: Selu is the Corn Mother of the Cherokee — she fed her family by rubbing her stomach to produce corn. When her sons discovered her secret and planned to kill her, she told them how to perform the ceremony to grow corn from her blood and body. She sacrificed herself to feed her people for all generations.
of: Corn pollen offered on the wind, the corn planting ceremony, first corn of the season returned to the earth, the Green Corn Ceremony
an: Deer (her husband Kanati was the deer hunter), crow (who brought the first corn seed)
co: Gold (ripe corn), green (growing corn), red (corn blood/kernels)
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Yellow jasper, corn-gold citrine, red jasper
bp: The womb (from which corn grows), the hands (planting), the stomach (the nourishment of corn)
af: I plant in sacred trust. The earth will feed what I tend with love. I give so that all may eat.
sh: The secret discovered — the sacred source destroyed once it is seen without reverence
ar: The Sacred Sustenance / The Self-Sacrificing Mother
cb: Kanati (her husband, the great hunter — the masculine hunting principle) counterbalances Selu (the feminine planting principle). The hunt and the cultivation, the forest and the field.

do: Agriculture, renewal through shedding, the earth renewed through death, the seasons
at: 'Our Lord the Flayed One' — one of the most striking Aztec deities. He wears the flayed skin of a sacrificed person, representing the earth's husk that must be shed for new crops to grow. He is the principle of the earth renewing itself by shedding its old skin.
of: The shedding of old skins (literal and symbolic), seeds planted in ceremony, red offerings
an: Snake (the skin-shedder), eagle, jaguar
co: Gold and red — the colours of the flayed skin and the new flesh beneath
dp: Tuesday / Mars — renewal through sacrifice
cr: Red jasper, obsidian, carnelian
bp: The skin (the shed skin = the old self), the root chakra (renewal from the ground up)
af: I shed what is no longer my skin. Beneath the old layer I am renewed and vivid. I emerge from my own transformation.
sh: The shedding that goes on indefinitely because the old skin never stops growing back
ar: The Renewer / The Sacred Shed Skin
cb: Quetzalcoatl (the spiritual ascent) counterbalances Xipe Totec (the physical renewal through shedding). The ascending principle counterbalanced by the renewing-through-stripping principle.

do: The hunt, justice, precision, the forest, the tracker
at: The Orisha of the hunt — the divine tracker, the one whose arrow never misses. He lives in the forest, governs justice, and represents the root chakra's principle of precise, focused will. His arrow flies straight from the root to its target without deviation.
of: Game meat, honey, wild fruits, indigo blue cloth, arrows, offerings at the forest's edge
an: Hawk (his sacred bird), deer (his primary quarry), hound
co: Blue and gold (his sacred colours)
dp: Monday / Mercury — the swift and precise tracker
cr: Blue sapphire, sodalite, gold pyrite, arrow flint
bp: The eyes (the tracker's precision), the hands and arms (bow and arrow), the feet (silent in the forest)
af: My aim is true. I track what I seek with the patience of the forest. My will flies straight to its mark.
sh: The hunter who hunts the wrong prey — the precision of will directed toward an unjust target
ar: The Divine Tracker / The Arrow That Never Misses
cb: Ogun (iron, the forge, the warrior of the open road) counterbalances Oshosi (wood, the hunt, the tracker of the hidden forest). Iron and wood. The road and the forest.

do: Plants, herbs, the forest medicine, nature's healing power
at: The Orisha of plants and herbal medicine — depicted as a one-eyed, one-armed, one-legged figure who nevertheless controls all the plants of the earth. Without his permission, no herb has power. He lives in the deepest forest and holds the knowledge of the earth's healing plants.
of: Herbs and plants offered with reverence, forest ceremonies, the planting of medicinal herbs as prayer, offerings to trees
an: Bird (he hangs a bird's skull from a stick to speak through)
co: Green (the colour of all plants), white (the pure medicine)
dp: Saturday / Earth
cr: Green aventurine, moss agate, malachite, all green healing stones
bp: The entire body as plant medicine governs it — each plant corresponds to specific organs and systems
af: The forest medicine is in my blood. Every plant on this earth is my teacher. The earth heals what the earth created.
sh: The hidden healer who will not share — Ossain's shadow is the keeper of knowledge who withholds it
ar: The Forest Healer / The Root Medicine
cb: Ogun (iron, cutting through, the forge) counterbalances Ossain (plant, growing, the gentle medicine). The cut and the cure.

do: Passion, physical love, erotic pleasure, desire, the wife of Kamadeva
at: Rati is the goddess of passion and physical love — the consort of Kamadeva. Her name means 'delight' or 'pleasure.' She embodies the sacral chakra's erotic principle in its purest, most joyful form: physical pleasure as divine.
of: Rose and jasmine offerings, red flowers, honey, physical intimacy honoured as sacred practice
an: Dove, the parrot (her husband's vehicle), the cuckoo
co: Red, rose, deep pink
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Pink tourmaline, rose quartz, red garnet, ruby
bp: The sacral plexus, the reproductive organs, the skin as the organ of pleasure
af: My pleasure is divine. I honour my body's capacity for joy. Love and physical delight are sacred.
sh: The grief of Rati — the passion that destroys itself when its object is removed
ar: The Pleasure Goddess / The Sacred Erotic
cb: Parvati (devoted love, the spiritual love that transforms) counterbalances Rati (erotic love, the physical pleasure that delights). Devotion and desire as the two faces of the divine feminine love principle.

do: Art, games, beauty, dance, flowers, singing, poetry, the sacral masculine
at: 'Flower Prince' — the Aztec god of art, games, beauty, dance, flowers, singing, poetry and the sacred intoxicants. He is the masculine face of the sacral chakra — the male principle of creative joy, sensory pleasure, and the sacred beauty of the living world.
of: Flowers, music and dance offered as prayer, creative work dedicated to him, the sacred use of entheogens in ceremony
an: Butterfly (the living flower), hummingbird, quetzal bird
co: Orange, gold, vivid flower colours
dp: Friday / Venus
cr: Jade, turquoise, citrine, all bright stones
bp: The sacral plexus, the hands (art), the feet (dance), the voice (singing)
af: I create beauty as a form of prayer. My joy is sacred. The living world is adorned with my delight.
sh: The prince who becomes the addict — the sacred intoxicant that becomes the addiction
ar: The Flower Prince / The Sacred Joy
cb: Tlazolteotl (filth, purification, the shadow of pleasure) counterbalances Xochipilli (beauty, joy, the light of pleasure). The flower prince counterbalanced by the devourer of filth.

do: The sea, ships, the navy, fish, marine life, everything beneath the ocean surface
at: The Lwa of the sea — the naval captain of the Vodou pantheon. He is the master of all that lives beneath the ocean's surface. He is invoked by sailors, fishermen and all who depend on the sea. He governs the sea with military precision and a naval captain's authority.
of: Ship models floated out to sea with offerings, rum and champagne, blue and white candles, fish, lobster, underwater offerings in a waterproof container
an: Whale, all sea fish, the sea turtle
co: Blue and white (the sea's colours), silver (the sea's light)
dp: Monday / Neptune — the sea ruler
cr: Aquamarine, blue topaz, sea glass, pearl
bp: The sacral plexus (the body's ocean), the lungs (holding breath underwater)
af: I navigate the deep waters with the confidence of a sea captain. The ocean serves those who respect it.
sh: The sea captain who becomes the tyrant — authority over the sea that becomes control over those who depend on it
ar: The Sea Captain / The Naval Commander of the Deep
cb: La Sirène (his consort — the mysterious, enchanting face of the sea) counterbalances Agwé (the authority, the organised navigation of the sea). The siren's mystery counterbalanced by the captain's order.

do: The supreme Babylonian deity, the city god of Babylon, the victor over Tiamat
at: The chief deity of Babylon — the son of Enki who defeated the primordial monster Tiamat and from her body created the world. He is the solar plexus principle of the patriarchal city-state: the conquering, ordering, city-building masculine power that defeated the older feminine chaos.
of: Golden offerings, cedar and cypress incense, the Marduk star (Jupiter), lion-dragon imagery
an: Dragon (Mušḫuššu — the lion-dragon), bull, lion
co: Gold, sky blue, the royal colours of Babylon
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the king of the gods
cr: Gold, yellow sapphire, lapis lazuli, jasper
bp: Solar plexus (the conqueror's power), the eyes (his symbol was the all-seeing eye of judgement)
af: I order what was chaos. I build from what was formless. My power serves creation.
sh: The victor's shadow — Marduk's triumph is also the erasure of Tiamat and the divine feminine. Power that mistakes domination for creation.
ar: The Conquering King / The Builder of Civilisation
cb: Tiamat (the primordial feminine chaos he defeated) — her restoration is the healing of this cosmic wound. Order counterbalanced by the chaos that preceded and surrounds it.

do: The sun of midday and midsummer, plague, war, the underworld, the lion
at: The god of the destructive midday sun and the lord of the Babylonian underworld. He is the solar plexus in its most scorching, merciless form — the sun that kills crops rather than grows them, the plague that spreads in summer heat.
of: Red and black candles, lion imagery, warrior offerings, the burning midday sun ceremony
an: Lion, cock, fish
co: Red, the burning yellow of midday, black
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Saturday / Pluto
cr: Red jasper, carnelian, obsidian, iron
bp: The adrenal glands (the noon sun's fire), the solar plexus at its most fierce, the liver
af: I face the full heat of my own power without flinching. I am sovereign even in the underworld of my challenges.
sh: The plague that spreads without mercy — the solar power that destroys indiscriminately
ar: The Destructive Sun / The Underworld King
cb: Ereshkigal (his consort by conquest — the queen he forced to accept him) counterbalances Nergal. Also Marduk (the orderly, city-building sun) counterbalances Nergal (the chaotic, destructive sun).

do: The sun itself, the all-seeing eye of the sky, the solar chariot, oaths witnessed by the sun
at: The original Greek sun god — before Apollo absorbed his solar functions, Helios was the sun itself. He drove his fiery chariot across the sky each day. His son Phaethon nearly set the world on fire when he borrowed the chariot. He is the witness to all oaths because the sun sees everything.
of: Golden offerings at sunrise, chariot imagery, honey (the sun's gift), frankincense, sunflowers
an: Eagle (the sky bird), white horses (pulling his chariot), rooster (announces him)
co: Gold, brilliant orange, the red of the rising and setting sun
dp: Sunday / Sun
cr: Sunstone, amber, citrine, gold topaz, clear quartz
bp: The solar plexus, the eyes (the sun as the eye of the sky), the skin exposed to sunlight
af: The sun sees all. My deeds are witnessed by the all-seeing light. I shine without apology or hiding.
sh: The son who drives the chariot too close to earth — the solar plexus that overreaches, that burns what it means to warm
ar: The All-Seeing Sun / The Solar Charioteer
cb: Selene (the moon goddess — his sister) counterbalances Helios (the sun). The day and night sky divided between brother and sister. Solar and lunar in their fundamental sibling polarity.

do: The sun goddess of Arinna, the supreme deity of the Hittite pantheon
at: THE FORGOTTEN HITTITE SUN GODDESS. The sun goddess of Arinna was the most important deity in the entire Hittite pantheon — above even the storm god. Also identified with the Hurrian Hebat ('Queen of Heaven'). She was the divine protector of the Hittite state. Almost entirely forgotten outside of academic archaeology.
of: Solar ceremonies, golden offerings, prayers for the protection of the land and state
an: Lion (the solar animal across all Near Eastern traditions), eagle
co: Gold, the brilliant yellow of the Anatolian sun
dp: Sunday / Sun
cr: Gold, sunstone, yellow jasper
bp: The solar plexus, the crown (she was the supreme deity), the eyes
af: I am the sun goddess whose name was forgotten. I rise again. The supreme feminine solar power is restored.
sh: The solar goddess who was replaced by the solar god — the systematic erasure of the feminine sun in Near Eastern religion
ar: The Supreme Feminine Sun / The Forgotten Hittite Queen
cb: Teshub (the Hittite/Hurrian storm god, her consort) counterbalances Arinna/Hebat (the sun goddess). Storm and sun — the fundamental Anatolian cosmic polarity.

do: The sun subdued, the islands fished from the sea, fire stolen for humanity
at: The great trickster hero of Polynesian mythology — he lassoed the sun and beat it to make the days longer. He fished the islands of Polynesia up from the sea floor. He stole fire from the underworld for humanity. He is the solar plexus principle at its most heroically creative: the impossible made possible through wit, courage and determination.
of: Fish hooks (his sacred symbol), fire ceremonies, the ocean's offering
an: Turtle, dolphin, rooster (the sun's bird)
co: Blue (the ocean), gold (the sun), red (the fire he stole)
dp: Sunday / Sun — the hero who wrestled the sun itself
cr: Black tourmaline (his great hook), ocean jasper, carnelian (the fire he stole)
bp: The solar plexus (the heroic will), the arms (his great strength), the hands (the fishhook and the jawbone)
af: If the sun needs to be slowed, I will lasso it. My courage makes the impossible possible.
sh: Maui attempted to win immortality for humanity by entering the sleeping body of Hine-nui-te-po and was killed. The solar plexus that reaches too far dies in the attempt.
ar: The Impossible Hero / The World-Transformer
cb: Hine-nui-te-po (the great goddess of death who killed him) counterbalances Maui (the hero who overcame everything except death). The transformer counterbalanced by the one thing that cannot be transformed.

do: Divine love, sacred play (Lila), devotion, the Bhagavad Gita, the eighth avatar of Vishnu
at: The beloved blue god — the eighth avatar of Vishnu, the divine teacher of the Bhagavad Gita, the sacred flute player whose music draws all souls toward the divine. He is the heart chakra in its most elevated, playful and cosmically vast expression: the god whose love play (Lila) IS the universe.
of: Butter and milk, yellow flowers (Radha's colour), blue candles, the Bhagavad Gita read as prayer, flute music
an: Peacock (he wears peacock feathers), cow, parrot
co: Blue (the colour of the infinite sky — his skin colour), yellow, gold
dp: Thursday / Moon
cr: Blue sapphire, lapis lazuli, moonstone, peacock-blue labradorite
bp: Heart (the flute's music opens it), throat (the sacred song), crown (the Bhagavad Gita's cosmic teaching)
af: I am the divine play. The universe is God's love play. My heart opens to the flute's call. All of this is Lila.
sh: The trickster god's shadow — Krishna's divine play sometimes manifests as divine deception. The cosmic game that doesn't care about ordinary morality.
ar: The Divine Playmate / The Cosmic Love
cb: Arjuna (the human warrior who must be convinced to fight) counterbalances Krishna (the divine charioteer who reveals the cosmic order). The reluctant human counterbalanced by the willing divine. The Bhagavad Gita IS this counterbalance made into sacred text.

do: The future Buddha, loving-kindness, the next great age of the dharma
at: Maitreya ('the Loving One') is the future Buddha — he sits in a heavenly realm waiting for the right moment to come and restore the dharma. His name means loving-kindness (metta). He is the heart chakra at its most patient and most universal: the love that waits through aeons for the right moment.
of: The cultivation of metta (loving-kindness meditation), offerings made while setting an intention for the future world, white lotus
an: Dragon (the vehicle of the future), golden lion
co: Gold, bright yellow, the clear gold of loving-kindness
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the future abundance
cr: Gold topaz, citrine, yellow sapphire, golden tiger's eye
bp: Heart (loving-kindness), crown (the future enlightenment)
af: I am loving toward all beings. I wait with patience for the right time. The future holds what the present cannot yet contain.
sh: The future that never comes — the waiting Maitreya's shadow is the messianic expectation that postpones present awakening
ar: The Future Love / The Patient Bodhisattva
cb: Shakyamuni (the present Buddha — the historical Buddha of this age) counterbalances Maitreya (the future Buddha). The present teaching counterbalanced by the future renewal.

do: The rainbow, messages between gods and humans, the link between heaven and earth
at: The goddess of the rainbow — the golden-winged messenger who carries messages between the gods and humanity. She travels the rainbow as her road between the worlds. She is the throat chakra's most beautiful expression: the divine communication made visible as colour.
of: A glass of water left in sunlight to create rainbow light, offerings of all seven colours, the honouring of rainbows as divine messages
an: Dove, eagle, peacock (the rainbow of feathers)
co: All seven colours of the rainbow — she contains them all
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — divine messages
cr: Rainbow moonstone, labradorite, iris-coloured iolite
el: Air / Water (the rainbow is water and light)
bp: Vocal cords (the message), eyes (the rainbow seen)
af: My message is a rainbow bridge between worlds. I carry what needs to be communicated with beauty and precision. The divine speaks through me in colour.
sh: The message that is misunderstood — the rainbow that ends before it reaches the one it was meant for
ar: The Rainbow Bridge / The Colourful Messenger
cb: Hermes (the trickster-messenger who works in grey areas) counterbalances Iris (the pure luminous messenger of divine communication). The straight rainbow counterbalanced by the winding messenger path.

do: Dreams, sleep, the shapes taken in dreams, the visions of the night
at: The god of dreams — son of Hypnos (Sleep) and grandson of Nyx (Night). He takes human form in the dreams of kings and heroes. He is the third eye's nocturnal face: the vision that comes when the conscious mind is silent.
of: Poppy flowers and seeds (his sacred plant — induces sleep), offerings made at the threshold of sleep, the honouring of significant dreams
an: Bat (the creature of the night vision), owl, heron
co: Deep purple, midnight black, the silver of moonlit sleep
dp: Monday / Moon — the night visions
cr: Amethyst (the dream stone), black moonstone, labradorite, grey obsidian
bp: Third eye (the dream vision), the pineal gland (which produces DMT in sleep), the entire mind in its dreaming state
af: My dreams speak truth. The visions of the night are as real as the visions of the day. I honour what comes in sleep.
sh: The nightmare — Morpheus's shadow is the terrible dream that is also true. The prophetic vision that is also a horror.
ar: The Dream Shaper / The Vision of the Night
cb: Hypnos (his father, Sleep itself) counterbalances Morpheus (the shaping of specific dream forms within that dissolution). The void sleep counterbalanced by the meaningful dream.

do: Writing, wisdom, scribes, destiny, the record of human fates
at: The Babylonian god of writing and wisdom — patron of scribes, keeper of the Tablets of Destiny. Son of Marduk. He holds the stylus that writes fate. One of the most important deities of Mesopotamia, largely forgotten outside academia.
of: Reed pens, clay tablets, writing implements offered as prayer, stylus imagery
an: Winged dragon (Mušḫuššu), goat-fish
co: Deep blue, lapis, gold
dp: Wednesday / Mercury
cr: Lapis lazuli, blue agate, sodalite
bp: Hands (the scribe's hands), throat, brain
af: I write my destiny with the stylus of my choices. The record of my life is being kept in the divine ledger. I write what is true.
sh: The destiny that is fixed on the tablet and cannot be changed — the despair of the predetermined
ar: The Divine Scribe of Destiny / The Keeper of Fates
cb: Marduk (his father, the supreme active force) counterbalances Nabu (the recorder, the contemplative). Action counterbalanced by the record of action.

do: The eternal blue sky, the supreme deity of the steppes, the sky father of the nomads
at: Tengri ('Eternal Blue Sky') is the supreme deity of the Mongol, Turkic and many Central Asian peoples — the sky itself as the supreme divine. Genghis Khan attributed his conquests to the mandate of Tengri. He is the crown principle of the nomadic steppe worldview: the infinite blue sky above the grasslands.
of: Blue offerings (the colour of the sky), offerings made in the open air on high ground, no temples (the sky is the temple), the sacred blue scarf (khadag)
an: Eagle (the sky's bird), wolf (his sacred animal), horse (the steed beneath the sky)
co: Blue (the eternal sky blue is his colour)
dp: Sunday — the supreme sky's day
cr: Blue lapis, blue sapphire, sky blue turquoise
el: Air / Cosmic Consciousness — the infinite sky is both air and cosmic
bp: Crown, the entire upward gaze, the body looking upward toward the infinite sky
af: The eternal blue sky is my home. The divine has no walls and no ceiling but the infinite. I am a nomad beneath the endless heaven.
sh: The sky so vast it dwarfs the individual into nothing — the crown chakra that becomes existential terror rather than cosmic awe
ar: The Eternal Sky / The God of the Infinite Horizon
cb: Etugen / Etügen Eej (the earth mother — the green steppe beneath the blue sky) counterbalances Tengri (the blue sky above). The fundamental nomadic polarity: the green earth below and the blue sky above.

do: The supreme sky god, the one who created all, the divine on Mount Kenya
at: Ngai is the supreme deity of the Kikuyu and Maasai peoples of Kenya — the creator of all things who dwells on Mount Kenya (Kirinyaga) and Mount Kilimanjaro. He created the first man (Gikuyu) and gave him the land of Kenya. He communicates through thunder and lightning.
of: Prayers offered while facing Mount Kenya, sacrifices made on high ground, prayer before and after meals, the fig tree as sacred
an: Lamb (sacrificial), cattle (the Maasai's sacred animals given by Ngai), eagle
co: White (the snow on the mountain peaks), blue (the sky), green (the sacred land)
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: White quartz (the snow peaks), blue topaz (the sky), green jasper (the sacred land)
el: Air / Cosmic Consciousness / Earth (the sacred mountain)
bp: Crown, the entire being, the eyes (looking toward the mountain)
af: My eyes rest on the sacred mountain. The supreme watches from the heights. I lift my prayers to what is higher than me.
