sh: The forty-year war — the sovereignty that must fight for so long it can never fully rest
ar: The Divine Sovereign Warrior / The Sacred Queen
cb: The Portuguese colonial power (the external force that tried to extinguish her sovereignty) counterbalances Njinga (the sovereign who refused to be extinguished). The oppression counterbalanced by the inextinguishable divine authority.

do: The supreme sky god, the one who created all, the divine on Mount Kenya
at: Ngai is the supreme deity of the Kikuyu and Maasai peoples of Kenya — the creator of all things who dwells on Mount Kenya (Kirinyaga) and Mount Kilimanjaro. He created the first man (Gikuyu) and gave him the land of Kenya. He communicates through thunder and lightning.
of: Prayers offered while facing Mount Kenya, sacrifices made on high ground, prayer before and after meals, the fig tree as sacred
an: Lamb (sacrificial), cattle (the Maasai's sacred animals given by Ngai), eagle
co: White (the snow on the mountain peaks), blue (the sky), green (the sacred land)
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: White quartz (the snow peaks), blue topaz (the sky), green jasper (the sacred land)
el: Air / Cosmic Consciousness / Earth (the sacred mountain)
bp: Crown, the entire being, the eyes (looking toward the mountain)
af: My eyes rest on the sacred mountain. The supreme watches from the heights. I lift my prayers to what is higher than me.
sh: The divine on the mountain that cannot be reached — the crown that is forever 'up there' and never 'in here'
ar: The Mountain God / The Supreme Creator of East Africa
cb: Waweru (the wind-being, the spirit that Ngai sends to communicate with humans) counterbalances Ngai (the supreme who is too high to approach directly). The accessible divine counterbalanced by the inaccessible supreme.

do: Invisibility, cunning wisdom, commanding respect, the power to pass unseen
at: The first and most powerful of the 72 Goetic spirits — King Bael commands 66 legions of spirits. He appears as a cat, a toad, a man, or all three simultaneously. He grants the power of invisibility and cunning. In the chakra system he governs the root's primal fear-conquered-as-power and the third eye's ability to see without being seen.
of: Red candles, sigil drawn in ink on parchment, offerings of incense and commanding intent, Solomon's brass vessel imagery
an: Cat, toad (his three forms)
co: Red, deep black
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the authority of command
cr: Black tourmaline, obsidian, black onyx
bp: Root chakra base, the eyes (invisibility — seeing without being seen)
af: I move through the world with cunning and ease. I am seen only when I choose to be. My authority is quiet and absolute.
sh: The invisibility that becomes total isolation — the one who cannot be seen even when they want to be found
ar: The Hidden Command / The First King
cb: Agares (2nd spirit — the bringer-down of the great) counterbalances Bael (the raiser into hidden authority). The concealed and the revealed.

do: Languages, destruction of enemies' dignities, causing earthquake, teaching all languages
at: The second Goetic spirit — Agares appears as an old man riding a crocodile, carrying a goshawk. He teaches all languages instantly, causes deserters to return, destroys dignities (both of enemies and of the self-inflated), and causes earthquakes. He governs the throat's gift of language as power and the root's capacity to shake what has become too rigid.
of: Crocodile imagery, language offerings (write in an unknown language), yellow candles
an: Crocodile (his mount), goshawk (he carries it)
co: Yellow, earthy brown
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — language mastery
cr: Blue lace agate, yellow jasper, citrine
bp: Throat, tongue, the vocal cords as instruments of cosmic language
af: Every language is available to me. I shatter the pride that no longer serves. I bring back what has fled.
sh: The one who speaks all languages but communicates nothing — the linguistic mastery that conceals emptiness
ar: The Language Master / The Pride Destroyer
cb: Bael (the hidden commander) counterbalances Agares (the vocal revealer of all languages). Silence and speech.

do: Discovering what is hidden, declaring past and future events, finding lost things
at: The third Goetic spirit — Vassago is considered 'good natured' among the spirits. He declares things past, present and future; discovers what has been hidden or lost. He is the seer-spirit, the third eye's cleanest Goetic expression — the one who does not demand but reveals.
of: Indigo candles, mirror gazing, an offering of a question asked sincerely, lapis lazuli placed on the altar
an: None specific — he is the all-seeing vision itself
co: Indigo, deep purple, silver
dp: Monday / Moon — the reflective sight
cr: Lapis lazuli, purple amethyst, black mirror glass, moonstone
bp: Third eye, pineal gland, the visual centres of the brain
af: What is hidden is being revealed to me. Past and future are both visible to clear sight. Nothing is truly lost.
sh: The seer who cannot unsee — the burden of knowing what is hidden
ar: The Benevolent Seer / The Finder
cb: Bael (who conceals) counterbalances Vassago (who reveals). The hidden and the found in eternal polarity.

do: The souls of the dead, knowledge of occult sciences, the liberal arts
at: The fourth spirit — Samigina appears as a small horse or ass, then transforms into human form at the magician's command. He teaches the liberal arts and sciences, and — most distinctively — he brings knowledge from the souls of those who have died in sin, speaking of what they know. He is the voice of those not heard.
of: White candles, academic texts offered as symbol, invocation at dusk when worlds thin
an: Small horse or ass (his appearing form)
co: White, grey, pale yellow
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — learning and the liberal arts
cr: Celestite, blue calcite, sodalite
bp: Throat (the voice of the silent dead), crown (their released knowledge)
af: The wisdom of those who went before me is available. I learn from all sources, including those society would silence.
sh: The knowledge gained from those who suffered — the shadow of learning built on others' pain
ar: The Speaker for the Silenced / The Scholar of Death
cb: Gaap (who causes ignorance in others) counterbalances Samigina (who bestows knowledge from the dead). Knowing and not-knowing.

do: Diseases and their causes, cures, mechanical arts, shape-changing, wisdom
at: The fifth spirit — Marbas appears as a great lion that changes into human form. He reveals the causes of hidden diseases and provides cures for them; he gives great wisdom; he teaches mechanical arts; he can change men into other shapes. The solar plexus's physician-spirit — the one who knows what is wrong because he can cause it.
of: Lion imagery, medical herbs offered in ceremony, green candles, a question about healing asked at the altar
an: Great lion (his primary form)
co: Green, gold, deep amber
dp: Sunday / Sun — the healing fire
cr: Malachite, green tourmaline, bloodstone, emerald
bp: Solar plexus (the seat of healing fire), heart (the compassion of cure), the immune system
af: I know what causes my illness and I know its cure. The physician within me understands every system. Healing intelligence lives in me.
sh: The one who knows the disease and chooses to cause it rather than cure — the healer who can become the destroyer
ar: The Hidden Physician / The Disease-Knower
cb: Botis (who tells of past and future) counterbalances Marbas (who tells of the body's present condition). Time and the body.

do: Thievery, familiarity, temptation, the sweet corruption of the body's desires
at: The sixth spirit — Valefor appears as a lion with the head of an ass, braying. He is a good familiar but seduces people into theft, leading them eventually to the gallows. He governs the sacral chakra's shadow principle: the seduction of desire that leads step by step into the destruction of one's foundation. He teaches the price of taking what is not freely given.
of: The acknowledgment of temptation honestly faced, black and red candles, the offering of what you have been compelled to want
an: Lion with ass's head (his hybrid form)
co: Dark red, earthy yellow
dp: Friday / Venus — desire as seduction
cr: Red jasper, obsidian, dark garnet
bp: Sacral plexus (the hunger of desire), root (the foundation undermined by compulsion)
af: I examine what I am compelled to take. The desire that would destroy me teaches me what I truly need.
sh: The gallows at the end of the seduction — Valefor leads wherever desire leads, and he knows where that is
ar: The Sweet Corruptor / The Desire That Destroys
cb: Oshun (the sacred desire that nurtures) counterbalances Valefor (the profane desire that destroys). Love and addiction.

do: Past and future, reconciling enmities, procuring love, the wolf-fire of seeing
at: The seventh spirit — Amon appears as a wolf with a serpent's tail who vomits flames; at command he transforms into a man with a raven's head or dog's teeth. He declares past and future events with perfect accuracy, procures love, and reconciles feuds. He is the seer who appears in the most alarming form — the third eye that opens through confrontation with what frightens.
of: Wolf imagery, flame candles, the honest naming of an enmity one wishes to resolve, black ink sigil
an: Wolf (his primary form), raven (his human-form head)
co: Red, deep orange, black
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the confrontational fire
cr: Fire opal, carnelian, dark obsidian
bp: Solar plexus (the confronted fear becoming power), third eye (the prophetic sight that sees through fear)
af: I reconcile what has been at war within me. The sight that opens through confrontation sees clearly. I speak truth to what I fear.
sh: The reconciliation forced before it is ready — the peace imposed by fire rather than earned through understanding
ar: The Wolf-Fire Seer / The Enmity-Resolver
cb: Barbatos (who communicates with animals and the natural world in harmony) counterbalances Amon (who confronts through the alarming). The harmonious and the confrontational.

do: Understanding the language of animals, knowledge of past and future, reconciling friendships, treasure hidden by enchantment
at: The eighth spirit — Barbatos appears as a great archer with four noble kings and their companies of great troops. He understands the singing of birds, the barking of dogs, the lowing of bullocks and the voice of every living creature. He reconciles friends and reveals treasure hidden by magic. He is the heart chakra's wild-world connection: the one who hears what animals say.
of: Offerings left outdoors for animals, natural objects (feathers, stones, shells) brought in with gratitude, birdcall ceremony
an: All animals — he speaks every animal language
co: Forest green, deep earth brown, archer's gold
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the language of nature
cr: Moss agate, green aventurine, all earth stones
bp: Heart (compassion for all creatures), ears (the hearing of all languages), third eye (the translated vision of animal consciousness)
af: I understand what the animals are saying. The natural world speaks to me and I am listening. Hidden treasure reveals itself to those with open hearts.
sh: The one who hears the animals' suffering and cannot act — the heart that is too open to the world's pain
ar: The Animal Listener / The Nature Translator
cb: Marbas (who transforms and reveals the body's condition) counterbalances Barbatos (who communicates with the natural world). Inner nature and outer nature.

do: All arts and sciences, knowledge of the mind, binding spirits, commanding obedience, the arts
at: The ninth spirit and one of the four Goetic kings — Paimon appears with a great noise, riding a dromedary, surrounded by music and his court. He teaches all arts and sciences, reveals secrets of the earth, wind and water. He must be faced with the respect due a king; he requires proper ceremony. Among practitioners he is among the most widely worked with, governing the crown chakra's sovereignty over knowledge.
of: Incense (frankincense), gold candles, the sigil drawn on gold paper, music offered, the request made as petition to a king (not command)
an: Dromedary (camel — his mount), his court of spirits
co: Gold, royal blue, the colours of sovereignty
dp: Sunday / Sun — the king's domain
cr: Gold topaz, lapis lazuli, sunstone, clear quartz
bp: Crown (the king of all knowledge), throat (the command voice), third eye (the revelation of deep secrets)
af: I approach the throne of knowledge with proper respect. All arts and sciences are available to me. I command my own mind with the authority of a king.
sh: The king who demands servitude — Paimon's shadow is the demand for obedience that becomes tyranny; the knowledge-seeker who mistakes information for wisdom
ar: The Knowledge King / The Royal Science
cb: Beleth (who compels love through terror) counterbalances Paimon (who bestows knowledge through sovereignty). Love-terror and knowledge-sovereignty.

do: Natural philosophy, logic, ethics, the healing herbs, healing of all diseases
at: The tenth spirit — Buer appears in Sagittarius as a great star. He teaches philosophy, logic and ethics; he heals all infirmities; he gives good familiars. He is one of the most benevolent of the 72, the healer-philosopher. He bridges heart (the ethics of care) with root (the physical healing of the body through earth's plants).
of: Medicinal herbs offered on the altar, green and earth candles, questions of ethics asked honestly, healing ceremony in his name
an: None specific — he is the philosopher
co: Green, earth brown, the colours of healing plants
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the wisdom of philosophy and medicine
cr: Green jasper, malachite, herbal stones, green tourmaline
bp: Heart (ethical philosophy), root (healing plants that grow from the earth), the entire body as healed system
af: I think clearly about what is right. The earth's plants heal what the body needs healed. Logic and love are not opposites.
sh: The philosophy that has no embodiment — the ethicist who cannot heal because they live only in the mind
ar: The Healing Philosopher / The Ethics Doctor
cb: Valefor (the seducer, the corruptor) counterbalances Buer (the healer, the philosopher). Corruption and healing — the same knowledge turned in opposite directions.

do: Honour, dignity, understanding all questions past and future, reconciling enemies, bestowing favour
at: The eleventh spirit — Gusion appears as a xenopilus (a dog-faced baboon). He answers all questions about past, present and future; he gives honour and dignity; he reconciles enmities and grants the love and favour of friends and foes alike. He is the spirit of earned dignity — the third eye that sees clearly enough that one gains natural authority.
of: Baboon imagery (his form — sacred to the Egyptians), silver candles, questions asked with genuine humility
an: Dog-faced baboon (his form — resonant with Thoth)
co: Silver, grey-blue, the colours of twilight clarity
dp: Monday / Moon — the reflective dignity
cr: Selenite, grey moonstone, blue calcite
bp: Third eye (the clear seeing that earns dignity), heart (the reconciliation of enemies)
af: I earn honour through clear seeing. My dignity comes from understanding, not from demanding respect. I reconcile even the harshest enmity.
sh: The dignity that is brittle — the honour that depends on others' recognition and shatters when not given
ar: The Earned Dignity / The Reconciler
cb: Amon (who procures love through fire and force) counterbalances Gusion (who earns favour through clear sight and earned dignity). Force and merit.

do: Love, lust, the revelation of secrets with mirth and delight, passionate desire
at: The twelfth spirit — Sitri appears as a leopard with angel's wings; at command he transforms into a beautiful man. He enflames people with love and lust, causes women to appear naked before the conjurer's eye, and reveals secrets with great mirth and delight. He is the sacral at its most charged — the spirit of erotic revelation.
of: Red and pink candles, roses, perfume, honey, the offering of one's own honest desire named without shame
an: Leopard (his animal form), the winged leopard
co: Red, rose, the deep crimson of desire
dp: Friday / Venus — erotic love
cr: Red garnet, rose quartz, ruby, pink tourmaline
bp: Sacral plexus (the centre of sexual desire), solar plexus (the confidence of erotic power)
af: My desire is honest and sacred. I am at ease with the full spectrum of attraction. I reveal myself with delight.
sh: The desire revealed without discernment — the nakedness that is violation rather than liberation
ar: The Erotic Revealer / The Mirth of Desire
cb: Marbas (the physician who knows disease) counterbalances Sitri (the spirit who enflames desire). The cool physician and the burning desire.

do: Causing love between men and women, terrible in appearance, commanding fear as the door to love
at: The thirteenth spirit and one of the four Goetic kings — Beleth appears riding a pale horse, with terrifying noise and great ceremony. He compels love between people. The conjurer must hold a hazel wand toward the west and south to protect himself. He governs the love that begins in fear — the root chakra's survival-terror transmuted through the sacral into desire.
of: A hazel wand, pale candles, the acknowledgment of what is terrifying about love, courage offered as the price of calling love forward
an: Pale horse (his mount)
co: Pale, grey-white, the grey of fear before love
dp: Tuesday / Mars — love won through courage
cr: Black tourmaline (protection), rose quartz (the love revealed), grey moonstone
bp: Root (the terror of love's vulnerability), sacral (the desire that survives the terror)
af: I am willing to be terrified by love. True love requires courage. I hold my wand toward the west and I call what I need.
sh: The love compelled by terror — the relationship that began in fear and never escaped it
ar: The Terror-Love / The Love That Demands Courage
cb: Sitri (who enflames love with delight) counterbalances Beleth (who compels love with terror). Joy and fear as the two paths to love.

do: Battles, putrefaction of wounds, the archer's art, causing great battles
at: The fourteenth spirit — Leraje (Leraie) appears as a gallant archer clad in green, carrying a bow and quiver. He causes all great battles and contests and makes wounds to putrefy that are made with arrows. He is the solar plexus's warrior-archer: the one who hits the mark, who knows that some battles must be fought before healing can begin.
of: Green candles, an arrow (real or symbolic) placed on the altar, warrior ceremony, the honest naming of what battle is truly necessary
an: None — he IS the archer
co: Green (his archer's garb), the green of the forest warrior
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the warrior's art
cr: Green jasper, carnelian, bloodstone, iron
bp: Solar plexus (the warrior's will), root (the soldier's grounded stance), right arm and hand (the archer's draw)
af: I aim with precision. I fight only battles that matter. My arrow finds its mark.
sh: The wound that putrefies — the battle that leaves poison rather than resolution
ar: The Precise Warrior / The Green Archer
cb: Buer (the healer) counterbalances Leraje (the one who causes wounds to putrefy). Wounding and healing as the polar dynamic.

do: Hidden things concerning war, discovering secrets, knowing the future of battles, winning favour of lords
at: The fifteenth spirit — Eligos appears as a goodly knight carrying a lance, an ensign, and a serpent. He discovers hidden things and knows future events, particularly of wars and battles. He wins the favour of great lords, knights and men of power. He is the military strategist's spirit: the third eye focused on power structures and how to navigate them.
of: Knight imagery, lance symbol, strategic plans offered at the altar, silver candles
an: None — the noble knight
co: Silver, dark blue, the colours of military intelligence
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Saturday / Saturn — strategy and discipline
cr: Labradorite (strategic sight), silver, dark sapphire
bp: Third eye (military intelligence, seeing hidden structures of power), solar plexus (the confidence to approach lords)
af: I see the hidden dynamics of power clearly. I know what is coming and I prepare. I earn the respect of those in authority through my clear sight.
sh: The strategist who serves power rather than truth — the military intelligence that is brilliant but ethicless
ar: The Strategic Knight / The Power Navigator
cb: Barbatos (who works with natural harmony) counterbalances Eligos (who navigates human power structures). Nature and politics.

do: Love between women and men, causing women to love men, causing barrenness
at: The sixteenth spirit — Zepar appears as a soldier in red armour, carrying arms. He causes women to love men and bring them together in love. He can also cause barrenness. He governs the sacral chakra's magnetic principle: the force of attraction that draws two people together, and its darker face — the love that forecloses other life.
of: Red candles, red roses, offerings of honest longing
an: None — the red soldier of desire
co: Red, the deep red of passion
dp: Friday / Venus — attraction and its price
cr: Red garnet, ruby, rose quartz, carnelian
bp: Womb (attraction and its foreclosure), sacral plexus (the magnetic pull of desire)
af: I call what I truly desire toward me. My magnetic force is honest. I attract through authenticity, not compulsion.
sh: The barrenness — the desire so focused on a single object that all other fertility closes
ar: The Magnetic Desire / The Red Soldier of Love
cb: Sitri (who enflames with delight) counterbalances Zepar (who compels with longing-unto-barrenness). Joy and obsession.

do: Past and future events, reconciling friends and foes, the viper's sight
at: The seventeenth spirit — Botis appears as a viper, then transforms into a man with large teeth and two horns, carrying a bright sword. He tells all things past and present and future, and he reconciles friends and foes. He is the third eye's most honest seer — the viper that strikes only when truth must be delivered.
of: Viper imagery (non-harmful), silver candles, the asking of a question that is hard to hear answered
an: Viper (his first form)
co: Silver, viper-green, white
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the truth that arrives with a sword
cr: Serpentine, green tourmaline, clear quartz
bp: Third eye (the viper's vision, sensing what others cannot), heart (reconciling what the truth has revealed)
af: The truth I most need to hear is available to me. I reconcile even the most difficult enmity by seeing clearly.
sh: The sword of truth that cuts without healing — the honest seer whose clarity destroys rather than clarifies
ar: The Viper-Seer / The Reconciling Truth
cb: Gusion (who earns dignity through understanding) counterbalances Botis (who delivers truth through the viper's strike). The earned truth and the sudden truth.

do: Properties of stones and herbs, swift transportation between worlds, knowledge of the virtues of stones
at: The eighteenth spirit — Bathin appears as a strong man with a serpent's tail, riding a pale horse. He knows the virtues of herbs and precious stones, and can transport men swiftly from country to country. He is the root chakra's alchemical earth-spirit: the one who knows what stones and herbs can do.
of: Stones and herbs placed on the altar, earth ceremony, pale candle, the intention of swift transformation
an: Pale horse (his mount), serpent (his tail)
co: Pale earth tones, silver, the grey-white of stone
dp: Saturday / Saturn and Earth — the knowledge of stone and herb
cr: All stones are sacred to him — especially those with healing virtue
bp: Root (the earth knowledge of stone and herb), crown (the swift transport — the journey between worlds)
af: I know what the earth's gifts can do. I move swiftly from where I am to where I need to be. Stone and herb are my teachers.
sh: The knowledge without application — the one who knows every herb and stone and never uses that knowledge
ar: The Herb-Stone Alchemist / The Swift Transporter
cb: Eligos (who knows the power structures of the human world) counterbalances Bathin (who knows the power structures of the natural world). Politics and alchemy.

do: Love between men and women, gentle loving, the peaceful face of desire
at: The nineteenth spirit — Sallos (Saleos) appears as a gallant soldier riding a crocodile, wearing a duke's coronet. He causes love between men and women and is gentle and pacific in his ways. He is the Goetia's most tender love-spirit: the heart chakra's Goetic expression, the desire that is both safe and warm.
of: Pink and gold candles, gentle offerings (flowers, honey), the naming of who you love or wish to love honestly
an: Crocodile (his mount — the ancient reptile carrying the gentle love)
co: Gold, gentle pink, warm amber
dp: Friday / Venus — gentle love
cr: Rose quartz, pink tourmaline, amber, gentle citrine
bp: Heart (the gentle love), sacral (the warm desire that accompanies it)
af: I am gentle in my loving. I allow love to be easy and warm. Tender desire is as powerful as passionate fire.
sh: The gentleness mistaken for weakness — the pacific love that allows itself to be exploited
ar: The Gentle Lover / The Crocodile-Carried Tenderness
cb: Beleth (love through terror) counterbalances Sallos (love through gentleness). The two faces of desire: the terrifying and the tender.

do: Hidden things, creation of the universe, divine and earthly secrets, manifestation of spirits
at: The twentieth spirit and one of the great Goetic kings — Purson appears as a man with a lion's face, carrying a viper and riding a bear, with a voice like a trumpet. He reveals hidden things, knows past, present and future, can discover treasure and familiars. He discloses divine secrets and the creation of the world — one of the most mystically oriented of the 72.
of: Lion and bear imagery, trumpet (or any brass instrument sound), gold candles, the offering of a genuine desire to understand creation
an: Bear (his mount), viper (he carries it), lion (his face)
co: Gold, deep amber, the colours of ancient authority
dp: Sunday / Sun — the king of revelation
cr: Gold, amber, clear quartz, sunstone
bp: Crown (the divine secrets of creation), third eye (the hidden things revealed), root (the treasure of the earth)
af: The secrets of creation are available to me. I stand before the king of revelation with honest inquiry. What is hidden shows itself.
sh: The divine secret that overwhelms — the crown too full of revealed creation to function in ordinary life
ar: The Creation-Knower / The Lion-Faced King
cb: Vassago (the gentle seer) counterbalances Purson (the grand royal revealer). Quiet revelation and thunderous revelation.

do: Astronomy, the liberal sciences, herbs and precious stones, providing familiars
at: The twenty-first spirit — Marax appears as a great bull with a man's face. He teaches astronomy and all the liberal sciences; he gives good familiars who know many things; he understands herbs and precious stones. He bridges the intellectual (third eye) with the grounded (heart + earth knowledge).
of: Bull imagery, star charts, astronomical instruments offered symbolically, earth offerings of herbs
an: Great bull (with man's face)
co: Deep blue (the night sky of astronomy), earth brown (the bull)
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the expansive knowledge of the cosmos
cr: Lapis lazuli, star sapphire, meteorite, moss agate
bp: Third eye (astronomical vision), heart (the care for the earth's plants and stones), brain (the liberal sciences)
af: The cosmos is an open book to me. I learn the sciences with a bull's steady determination. Herbs and stones speak their wisdom.
sh: The astronomer who has mapped the stars but never looked at them with wonder — the science without the sacred
ar: The Bull-Astronomer / The Cosmic Science
cb: Bathin (who knows herbs and stones) counterbalances Marax (who knows astronomy and the liberal arts). Earth knowledge and cosmic knowledge.

do: Wit, courage, the knowledge of things past and future, boldness of speech
at: The twenty-second spirit — Ipos appears as an angel with a lion's head, a goose's foot, and a hare's tail. He knows and declares things past, present and future. He gives wit and courage, makes men bold and valiant. He is the throat chakra's courageous spirit: the one who gives the voice that has been afraid to speak.
of: Lion imagery, offerings of words that have been held back (written and burned), gold and red candles
an: Lion (head), goose (foot), hare (tail — the strange compound form)
co: Gold, fire red, the colours of bold speech
dp: Tuesday / Mars — courage to speak
cr: Carnelian (the stone of bold speech), golden tiger's eye, fire opal
bp: Throat (the bold speech he grants), solar plexus (the courage to speak), third eye (the knowing that makes speech true)
af: I speak with wit and courage. I have the boldness to say what must be said. My voice is an instrument of my truth.
sh: The boldness without wisdom — the courageous speech that has no discernment about when to speak and when to be silent
ar: The Bold Voice / The Wit-Giver
cb: Marax (who gives the vast knowledge of the cosmos) counterbalances Ipos (who gives the courage to speak that knowledge). Knowing and speaking.

do: Eloquence, the arts and sciences, restoring lost honours and dignities, the black crane's voice
at: The twenty-fourth spirit — Naberius appears as a black crane, fluttering about the circle, speaking with a hoarse voice. He makes men cunning in all arts and sciences; he restores lost honours and dignities; he is a strong marquis with eloquence as his gift. The throat chakra's art-spirit: the hoarse but powerful voice that teaches all creative knowledge.
of: Black candle, crane or bird feather placed at the altar, the dedication of creative work to him
an: Black crane (his form — also appearing as a three-headed dog)
co: Black, dark grey, ink-dark blue
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — eloquence and the arts
cr: Blue kyanite, black tourmaline, labradorite
bp: Throat (the hoarse voice of mastery), crown (the arts and sciences at their height)
af: My hoarse voice is the voice of mastery. I learn every art with patience. Lost honour can be restored.
sh: The hoarse voice that can no longer speak — the creative mastery that has exhausted itself
ar: The Black Crane Eloquence / The Arts Restorer
cb: Ipos (who grants courage of speech) counterbalances Naberius (who grants eloquence of speech). Raw courage and refined eloquence.

do: Logic, ethics, longevity, strength, invisibility, knowledge of plants and stones
at: The thirty-first spirit — Foras appears as a strong man in human form. He teaches the arts of logic and ethics; he can make men invisible; he grants wisdom, eloquence, longevity and strength. He knows the virtues of all herbs and stones. A benevolent, stabilising spirit whose root chakra gifts are durability, longevity and the medicine of the earth.
of: Earth offerings, stones, long-lived plants (oak, yew) as symbol, green candles
an: None — the strong man himself
co: Deep green, the brown of old oak, the grey of stone
dp: Saturday / Saturn — longevity and endurance
cr: Petrified wood (longevity), malachite, green jasper, black tourmaline
bp: Root (longevity, strength, the body's endurance), heart (the ethical clarity that accompanies long life)
af: I live a long and strong life. The earth's plants and stones protect and sustain me. Logic and ethics guide my days.
sh: The long life without meaning — the longevity that has outlasted its own purpose
ar: The Longevity Spirit / The Ethical Strongman
cb: Leraje (who causes wounds) counterbalances Foras (who heals through longevity and the earth's gifts). Wounding and enduring.

do: The gaming arts, mathematics, the invisible, astronomy, geomancy, the power of invisibility, the lust-compulsion
at: The thirty-second spirit — Asmodeus (Asmoday) is one of the greatest and most well-known Goetic kings. He appears with three heads (bull, ram, man), a serpent's tail, goose feet, riding a lion and carrying a lance and flag. He teaches mathematics, astronomy, geomancy and the crafts. He gives a ring of invisibility. He governs the primal sacral-solar intersection: desire, craft, calculation, and the invisible power that underlies all.
of: Three-coloured candles, lance symbol, ring imagery, offerings of the craft one practices most deeply
an: Bull (first head), ram (second head), man (third head), lion (mount), serpent (tail)
co: Red, gold, black — the king's three colours
dp: Wednesday / Tuesday — craft and warrior-desire combined
cr: Fire opal, red garnet, iron, gold
bp: Sacral (the primal desire), solar plexus (the craft of calculation), root (the earthed compulsion)
af: I master calculation and craft with a king's authority. My desire is directed toward excellence. The invisible ring of power is already mine.
sh: The lust-compulsion that overrides all other intelligence — Asmodeus's most famous shadow is the desire that destroys every marriage it enters
ar: The Craft King / The Triple-Headed Desire
cb: Buer (the healing philosopher) counterbalances Asmodeus (the calculating desire-king). Healing wisdom and driven calculation.

do: Transporting between realms, causing ignorance or knowledge, philosophy, the liberal sciences, love and hatred
at: The thirty-third spirit — Gaap appears as a man who goes before four great and mighty kings as their guide. He can teach philosophy and all the liberal sciences; he can cause love or hatred; he can make men ignorant or knowing; he can transport men from kingdom to kingdom. He is one of the most philosophically oriented spirits and governs the crown's transit between states of consciousness.
of: Four cardinal direction candles, philosophical question offered, globe or map as symbol of transit
an: None — he is a man before kings
co: Royal purple, deep blue, the colours of philosophy
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the philosophical expansiveness
cr: Amethyst, indigo kyanite, purple fluorite, clear quartz
bp: Crown (the transit between realms of consciousness), third eye (the choice between knowing and not-knowing)
af: I move freely between states of consciousness. I choose knowledge. Philosophy is my guide through the kingdoms of the mind.
sh: The transit that disorients — the crown chakra that travels between realms without grounding
ar: The Kingdom-Transporter / The Philosopher-Guide
cb: Samigina (who speaks for the dead) counterbalances Gaap (who transports the living between realms). The dead's wisdom and the living's travel.

do: Past, present and future events, occult sciences, the fallen angels' story, knowledge of creation
at: The twenty-ninth spirit — Astaroth is a mighty and strong duke who appears as a hurtful angel riding a dragon, carrying a viper. He must be approached carefully (he has a strong offensive breath). He declares things past, present and future; he teaches the liberal and secret sciences; he speaks willingly of the creation of the world and the fall of the spirits. He is the Goetia's keeper of the cosmic narrative — the one who tells the story of the fall from the highest perspective.
of: Silver candles, the sigil drawn on silver, the sincere asking of the question 'why did this happen?'
an: Dragon (his mount), viper (he carries one)
co: Silver, dark grey, the colour of fallen light
dp: Sunday / Saturn — the crown's fall and its possibility of return
cr: Dark amethyst, grey labradorite, silver sheen obsidian
bp: Crown (the story of the fall from the highest), third eye (the occult sciences and cosmic narrative), throat (the speaking of the creation story)
af: I understand the cosmic narrative of why things fell. The story of the fall is also the story of the possible return. I hold both without despair.
sh: The narrative of fall that becomes identity — the one who tells the story of the fall so many times they forget they can rise
ar: The Fall's Keeper / The Cosmic Narrative
cb: Paimon (the sovereign of knowledge and the crown's authority) counterbalances Astaroth (the narrator of the crown's fall). Sovereignty and its shadow.

do: Discovery of witches, hidden things, storms and sea, building towers and destroying walls
at: The forty-fifth spirit and one of the Goetic kings — Vine appears as a lion riding a black horse, carrying a viper. He reveals witches and hidden things; he builds towers and tears down walls; he creates storms and stirs the sea. He is the root chakra's elemental power over the physical world: he builds and destroys structures, natural and human.
of: Lion imagery, a wall (literal or symbolic) both built and destroyed in ceremony
an: Lion (his form), black horse (his mount), viper (he carries it)
co: Dark storm grey, black, lightning blue
dp: Tuesday / Mars — destruction and construction
cr: Black tourmaline, storm meteorite, labradorite, iron
el: Earth / Water / Air — the storm that crosses elements
bp: Root (the foundation built and destroyed), solar plexus (the authority that commands storms), third eye (the discovery of what is hidden)
af: I build what needs building and I destroy what needs destroying. I can see what has been concealed. The storm I call also clears the air.
sh: The builder who cannot stop destroying — the power over structures turned to pure demolition
ar: The Storm-Builder / The Wall-Breaker King
cb: Foras (who endures) counterbalances Vine (who builds and destroys). Permanence and impermanence.

do: Friendship, covenant, the morning sun, contracts between beings, the social order of light
at: Mitra is one of the oldest of the Vedic Adityas — the god of friendship and covenant, closely paired with Varuna. Where Varuna governs the cosmic law from above, Mitra governs the human social covenant: agreements between people, the morning light that begins each day as a new bond. He is among the most ancient Aryan deities, surviving into Persian Mithra and Roman Mithras.
of: Sunrise ceremonies, contracts honoured as sacred acts, friendship sealed with light, saffron offered at dawn
an: Horse (the solar horse of the morning), eagle
co: Gold, the warm amber of dawn, the pale gold of friendship
dp: Sunday / Sun — the morning covenant
cr: Citrine, amber, yellow topaz, gold
bp: Solar plexus (the social contract as personal power), heart (the warmth of genuine friendship)
af: I honour my covenants. Friendship is a sacred force. The morning light renews every bond I have made.
sh: The contract used as a weapon — the covenant invoked not to sustain relationship but to destroy it
ar: The Sacred Covenant / The Morning Friend
cb: Varuna (the cosmic law from above) counterbalances Mitra (the human covenant from within). The universal law and the personal agreement.

do: The cosmic law (Rta), the ocean, the night sky, the all-seeing sovereign, sin and its forgiveness
at: Varuna is the supreme Vedic cosmic deity — the guardian of Rta (the cosmic order), the all-seeing king who governs with his noose (pasham), the ocean and the night sky. He was once the highest Vedic deity before his displacement by Indra. He forgives sins when sincerely confessed. He is the crown chakra's supreme Vedic expression: the divine that watches all and governs the law of the entire cosmos.
of: Water (especially ocean water), night sky ceremonies, confession spoken honestly, midnight offerings, blue lotus
an: Makara (the sea-monster, his vehicle), horse, eagle
co: Deep ocean blue, midnight, the colour of the night sky filled with stars
dp: Monday / Neptune — the cosmic ocean and the all-seeing night
cr: Lapis lazuli, deep blue sapphire, aquamarine, dark tourmaline
bp: Crown (the cosmic law), third eye (the all-seeing Varuna-eye), solar plexus (the sovereign will that upholds Rta)
af: I am seen by the divine that misses nothing. I confess my transgressions and receive forgiveness. The cosmic order holds me.
sh: Varuna's noose — the cosmic law applied without mercy, the all-seeing that becomes surveillance rather than wisdom
ar: The All-Seeing Sovereign / The Cosmic Law
cb: Mitra (the human covenant) counterbalances Varuna (the cosmic law). The agreement between people counterbalanced by the law above all agreements.

do: Friendship, patronage, the Milky Way, the hospitality of the Aryan people, marriage
at: Aryaman is the Aditya of friendship, patronage and the Milky Way (considered his path). He governs hospitality, the social bonds of the Aryan people, and the sacred institution of marriage. He is one of the most invoked Adityas in wedding ceremonies. The heart chakra's Vedic expression of the social-sacred: friendship as a cosmic force.
of: Wedding ceremonies, hospitality offered genuinely (food shared as prayer), Milky Way viewed with reverence
an: Horse (the Vedic solar animal), cow (the sacred social animal)
co: Milky white, silver, warm gold — the colours of the Milky Way
dp: Sunday / Jupiter — the expansive social harmony
cr: Moonstone, pearl, white quartz, silver-sheen labradorite
bp: Heart (the warmth of genuine friendship and hospitality), solar plexus (the confidence of social belonging)
af: I offer genuine hospitality. My friendships are sacred covenants. The Milky Way above me is the path of those who have walked before.
sh: The patronage that becomes dependency — the friendship that requires gratitude-as-payment
ar: The Sacred Hospitality / The Milky Way Friend
cb: Varuna (cosmic impersonal law) counterbalances Aryaman (the warm personal social bond). The impersonal and the personal as the two modes of cosmic order.

do: Fortune, prosperity, the morning sun's wealth, the portion each being receives of the divine
at: Bhaga is the Aditya of fortune and prosperity — his name means 'the portion' and also gives the word 'bhaga' (good fortune, happiness). He was blinded in a confrontation with Shiva. He governs the sacred portion of divine abundance that is each being's birthright — the solar plexus's understanding that prosperity is not earned but received as a divine portion.
of: Morning offerings of gratitude for one's portion, golden flowers, incense, the recounting of one's blessings
an: Horse (the solar animal), the cow (the animal of prosperity)
co: Gold, the amber of morning wealth, warm saffron
dp: Friday / Jupiter — the fortune and prosperity of the morning
cr: Citrine, yellow topaz, gold pyrite, amber
bp: Solar plexus (the personal authority to receive one's portion), sacral (the pleasure of abundance received)
af: I receive my divine portion with gratitude. Fortune is a sacred gift, not a competition. I celebrate what is mine.
sh: The blindness of Bhaga — the fortune that cannot see itself; the abundance that is invisible to the one who holds it
ar: The Divine Portion / The Blind Fortune
cb: Indra (the warrior who takes by force) counterbalances Bhaga (the recipient of the divine portion). Taking and receiving.

do: Ritual skill, cosmic dexterity, the capacity to act with divine precision, skill and competence
at: Daksha as one of the Adityas represents cosmic skill and ritual dexterity — his name means 'the able one' or 'the skilled.' He is also the famous Prajapati (progenitor) who organised the cosmic sacrifice, refused to invite Shiva, and was destroyed by Shiva's wrath before being restored. He is the solar plexus's principle of skilled action and its shadow: the competence that excludes.
of: Ritual tools offered with care, the dedication of skilled work to the divine, saffron and ghee
an: Goat (the animal of the sacrificed ritual), horse
co: Saffron, gold, the ceremonial red of ritual
dp: Sunday / Mars — skilled solar action
cr: Carnelian (skilled action), citrine (competence), gold topaz
bp: Solar plexus (the skilful will), hands (the dexterous craftsman's instrument), crown (the cosmic organiser)
af: I act with skill and precision. My competence is a form of devotion. I perform my work as ritual.
sh: Daksha's fatal flaw — the skill that refuses to honour what it cannot control. The competence that becomes arrogance, provoking its own destruction.
ar: The Skilled Performer / The Competent Creator
cb: Shiva (who destroyed Daksha's sacrifice) counterbalances Daksha (the organiser of the cosmic ritual). The wild and the ordered in their necessary confrontation.

do: The divine portion of light, the share of the gods, generosity, the share of cosmic grace
at: Amsha (Amsa) is the Aditya of the divine share or portion — similar to Bhaga but specifically the divine portion that flows as generosity. He embodies the principle that divine grace distributes itself equally: every being receives their portion of the sun's light. He is the heart chakra's cosmic justice principle: the sun that shines on all.
of: Offerings distributed equally to all who attend ceremony, the sharing of one's portion with others, sunlight honoured as equal distribution
an: None specific — he is the universal portion
co: Pale gold, the white-gold of even light
dp: Sunday / Sun — the equal distribution of light
cr: Clear quartz (the equal light), golden topaz, citrine
bp: Heart (the generous equal sharing), crown (the cosmic distribution of grace)
af: The sun's light reaches me equally with all others. I share my portion generously. Divine grace distributes itself without prejudice.
sh: The portion withheld — the hoarding of light, the refusal to let grace flow through
ar: The Equal Portion / The Generous Share
cb: Bhaga (the personal fortune) counterbalances Amsha (the cosmic distribution). Personal abundance and universal sharing.

do: The divine craftsman, the maker of divine weapons and tools, the progenitor of forms, Vishvakarman's predecessor
at: Tvashtr is the divine craftsman Aditya — he fashioned Indra's thunderbolt (vajra), the divine cup of soma, and many of the gods' weapons. He is the artisan of the divine world, the one who gives form to what was previously formless. His name gives the Sanskrit word for 'carpenter.' He is the throat chakra's artisanal expression: the voice that names and shapes things into being.
of: The tools of one's craft placed on the altar, offerings of handmade objects, the dedication of craftsmanship to the divine
an: Bull (the form-giver's animal), none specific
co: The shimmering gold of hammered metal, deep earth brown, forge-fire orange
dp: Wednesday / Vulcan — the divine craftsman's fire
cr: Carnelian (the craftsman's stone), iron, gold, bronze
bp: Throat (naming forms into being), hands (the craftsman's instruments), solar plexus (the confidence of the skilled maker)
af: I give form to what was formless. My craft is a sacred act. The things I make carry divine intention.
sh: The craftsman who creates weapons that destroy their creator — Tvashtr's son Vritra was made to destroy Indra, who had killed Tvashtr's son Vishvarupa
ar: The Divine Craftsman / The Form-Giver
cb: Indra (who uses the weapons Tvashtr makes, and conflicts with him) counterbalances Tvashtr (who creates the tools of Indra's power). The creator of power and the wielder of power.

do: Roads, journeys, cattle, the shepherd, abundance through movement, the protector of travellers
at: Pushan is the Aditya of roads, safe journeys and cattle — he is the shepherd and guardian of travellers, the one who knows every path and road. He eats porridge (his teeth were knocked out in the same event that blinded Bhaga). He is the root chakra's Aditya of safe passage: the one who ensures that movement through the world is protected.
of: Offerings made at the beginning of a journey, porridge or simple grain food, prayers for safe travel, cattle/animals blessed
an: Goat (his cart is pulled by goats), all cattle and domestic animals
co: The earthy gold of grain fields, warm brown, travel dust
dp: Friday / Mercury — the safe journey
cr: Tiger's eye (the traveller's stone), yellow jasper, citrine, gold
el: Earth (the roads), Fire (the solar warmth that lights the way)
bp: Feet (the traveller's instrument), root (the safety of the journey), solar plexus (the confidence of movement)
af: I travel safely on every road. The path I take is protected. I find abundance through my willingness to move.
sh: The lost traveller — Pushan's shadow is the guide who cannot guide himself, the shepherd whose own flock strays
ar: The Sacred Traveller / The Road-Protector
cb: Varuna (who governs the cosmic order from above) counterbalances Pushan (who protects the earthly path below). The heavenly law and the earthly road.

do: The ancestral sun, the first mortal, the father of Manu (humanity), the dawn of human time
at: Vivasvat (the Shining One) is the ancestral Aditya — the sun at the beginning of human time. He is the father of Yama (the first to die) and Manu (the first human, the Noah-figure of Hinduism who survived the flood). He is the crown chakra's ancestral solar principle: the light from which all human consciousness descended.
of: Ancestral ceremonies, dawn prayers specifically acknowledging the ancestors, the Shraddha (ancestor ceremony) performed at solar moments
an: Horse (the solar horse), eagle
co: The brilliant gold of the original sun, ancestral white
dp: Sunday / Sun — the ancestral solar origin
cr: Sunstone, amber, clear quartz, ancestral bone or ivory
bp: Crown (the ancestral origin of human consciousness), solar plexus (the vitality of the ancestral solar gift)
af: My consciousness descends from the first sun. The ancestors who came before me are held in the solar light. I honour the beginning.
sh: The ancestral sun that becomes the weight of lineage — the crown that is crushed by the obligation to the past
ar: The Ancestral Sun / The First Light of Humanity
cb: Yama (his son, the first to die — the king of the dead) counterbalances Vivasvat (the first living sun). Life and death as the two children of the ancestral light.

do: The impeller, the solar force that sets all in motion, the Gayatri Mantra's deity, the golden-handed sun
at: Savitr (Savitri) is the Aditya of solar impulsion — the golden-handed one who sets all in motion. He is the deity of the Gayatri Mantra (Om Bhur Bhuva Swaha, Tat Savitur Varenyam...) — the most powerful mantra in the Vedic tradition, recited daily by hundreds of millions. His golden hands set the sun in motion each morning. He is the crown chakra's supreme Vedic activator.
of: Gayatri Mantra recited at sunrise (the supreme offering — no object is more sacred), golden objects, saffron at dawn
an: Horse (pulling the solar chariot), the eagle
co: Gold, brilliant white, the light of dawn before the sun clears the horizon
dp: Sunday / Sun — the impeller of all solar motion
cr: Sunstone, clear quartz, golden topaz, citrine
bp: Crown (the Gayatri's divine destination), throat (the mantra's recitation), solar plexus (the impelling will of the sun's daily journey)
af: Om Bhur Bhuva Swaha, Tat Savitur Varenyam. I am set in motion by the divine sun. My day begins with the greatest light.
sh: The motion without direction — the impelling force that moves without purpose, like the sun without the dawn ceremony to orient it
ar: The Divine Impeller / The Gayatri's Sun
cb: Varuna (the stable cosmic law) counterbalances Savitr (the constant motion of impulsion). The unmoved mover and the mover of all things.

do: The divine portion shared in love, the gift given freely from abundance
at: Ansa (or Ansha, distinct from Amsha) is among the lesser-known Adityas — an embodiment of the freely given divine share, the gift that flows from abundance rather than obligation. He represents the heart chakra's solar principle: the sun that gives its warmth not from duty but from the fullness of its nature.
of: Spontaneous gifts given to others (not asked for), the free offering without expectation
an: None — the freely given itself
co: Warm gold, the colour of sunshine on skin
dp: Sunday / Venus — the warmth given freely
cr: Rose quartz (the loving gift), citrine, amber
bp: Heart (the generous solar warmth), hands (the giving gesture)
af: I give freely from my fullness. My warmth is not earned — it is simply my nature. I am the sun in my relationships.
sh: The gift that creates obligation — the generosity that secretly calculates its return
ar: The Freely Given Warmth / The Open-Handed Sun
cb: Bhaga (the portion received) counterbalances Ansa (the portion freely given). Receiving and giving as the solar circuit of abundance.

do: The creator, the supporter of the cosmic order, the ordainer, the foundation of all things
at: Dhata (Dhatri) is the Aditya of cosmic support and ordaining — he is the creator who supports and sustains the structures of the cosmos. His name means 'the supporter' or 'the ordainer.' He is the root chakra's cosmic principle: the divine support that holds everything in place so that life can proceed.
of: Offerings of stability and gratitude for what is held in place (home, body, relationships), earth ceremonies, supporting the foundations of one's life
an: Tortoise (the cosmic support), elephant
co: Earth brown, the deep gold of sustained foundation, stable grey
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the cosmic support and ordaining
cr: Obsidian (the deep earth support), black tourmaline, citrine, brown jasper
bp: Root (the divine support of the physical foundation), crown (the ordaining principle above)
af: I am supported by the cosmic ordainer. The structure of my life is held in place by divine intention. Foundation is a sacred gift.
sh: The cosmic support withdrawn — the ordaining power that withholds its sustaining force and everything falls
ar: The Cosmic Supporter / The Divine Foundation
cb: Savitr (the impeller of all motion) counterbalances Dhata (the supporter of all structure). Movement and stability.

do: The wrathful feminine wisdom, transmutation of desire and ego, the red dakini of supreme bliss
at: Vajrayogini is the supreme dakini of the Vajrayana tradition — the red, naked, wrathful female deity who embodies the highest tantric wisdom. She stands on a corpse (the ego) and holds a skull cup of blood (the transmuted life force). She is the Kundalini principle at its most explicit: the root's primal fire risen through the body to the crown's supreme bliss. She is the teacher of direct path practitioners and the most powerful female deity in Tibetan Buddhism.
of: Red offerings, the Vajrayogini sadhana (a complete meditation practice), red candles, her mantra Om Vajrayogini Hum Hum Phat
an: Tiger (she stands on or wears the tiger skin), vulture, crow
co: Red (her body is red — the colour of supreme bliss)
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the wrathful wisdom
cr: Ruby, red garnet, carnelian, red coral
bp: Root (the Kundalini fire she embodies), sacral (the transmuted bliss), crown (the dakini's flight to liberation)
af: I transmute every desire into wisdom. The fire at the base of my spine rises to liberation. I stand on the corpse of what was false.
sh: The tantric path used for spiritual materialism — the wrathful wisdom used to feed the ego rather than dissolve it
ar: The Red Wisdom / The Transmuter of Desire
cb: Avalokiteshvara (the compassion that saves) counterbalances Vajrayogini (the wisdom that destroys illusion). Compassion and sharp wisdom.

do: Protection, the destruction of obstacles, wrathful compassion, the lord of time
at: Mahakala ('Great Black Time') is the supreme protector deity of Tibetan Buddhism — a wrathful emanation of Avalokiteshvara who destroys all obstacles to enlightenment with terrifying speed. He has multiple arms, carries skull cups, wears a crown of skulls, and stands surrounded by flames. He is not destructive for its own sake but removes with absolute precision what obstructs liberation. One of the most widely propitiated dharmapalas.
of: Black candles, the Mahakala puja, skull cup imagery, black sesame seeds, the recitation of his mantra Om Shri Mahakala Hum Phat
an: Tiger (his power), vulture (his crown's skulls), elephant (he stands on demons)
co: Black (his body), deep blue, the charcoal of wrathful fire
dp: Tuesday / Saturn — the destructive protection of sacred time
cr: Black tourmaline (the protector stone), obsidian, dark garnet, jet
bp: Root (the protection of the physical basis of practice), solar plexus (the fierce will to destroy obstacles), crown (the compassionate intent behind the wrath)
af: Every obstacle to my highest development is being destroyed. The protector clears the way. Wrath in service of love is the most powerful force.
sh: The protection that becomes aggression — Mahakala's shadow is the use of fierce energy to defend the ego rather than dissolve it
ar: The Obstacle Destroyer / The Wrathful Protector
cb: Green Tara (the swift compassionate action) counterbalances Mahakala (the wrathful obstacle removal). The lotus and the sword.

do: Wisdom, the sword of discernment, transcendent knowledge, the bodhisattva who cuts through ignorance
at: Manjushri ('Gentle Glory') is the Bodhisattva of Wisdom — he holds the flaming sword of transcendent knowledge that cuts through ignorance and delusion, and the Prajnaparamita Sutra (the Perfection of Wisdom). He is considered the guru of all Buddhas. The third eye's supreme Buddhist expression: the sword that cuts through the veil of illusion with absolute precision.
of: The study of wisdom texts, his mantra Om Ah Ra Pa Cha Na Dhi, blue lotus, golden offerings, the dedication of intellectual work to him
an: Blue lion (his mount in some traditions), the lion of wisdom
co: Deep gold, brilliant yellow, the gold of the sword of wisdom
dp: Wednesday / Jupiter — transcendent wisdom
cr: Blue sapphire (the wisdom mind), lapis lazuli, golden topaz, clear quartz
bp: Third eye (the sword of discernment), throat (the word of wisdom), crown (the transcendent knowledge)
af: Om Ah Ra Pa Cha Na Dhi. The sword of wisdom cuts through what is false. My understanding is becoming as sharp as light.
sh: Wisdom without compassion — the sword of discernment that cuts without care for what bleeds
ar: The Sword of Wisdom / The Discernment Bodhisattva
cb: Avalokiteshvara (compassion without limit) counterbalances Manjushri (wisdom without attachment). The supreme Buddhist polarity of wisdom and compassion.

do: Universal goodness, the offering of all worlds, the ten great vows, the primordial Buddha of Dzogchen
at: Samantabhadra ('Universal Goodness') is two distinct but related figures: in Mahayana he is the bodhisattva of universal goodness who made ten great vows to serve all beings; in Vajrayana Dzogchen he is the primordial blue Buddha — the dharmakaya (truth body), the naked deep blue figure in union with his consort Samantabhadri, representing the primordial ground of awareness itself.
of: The Samantabhadra vows (the ten great bodhisattva vows), infinite offering contemplation (visualising offering all worlds to the Buddhas), deep blue candles
an: Elephant (his traditional mount in Mahayana), the lotus
co: Deep blue (the primordial sky — the Dzogchen Buddha is deep blue), white lotus
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — universal goodness and the infinite offering
cr: Sapphire (primordial wisdom blue), lapis lazuli, clear quartz (the space-like nature of mind)
bp: Crown (the primordial ground of awareness), heart (the ten great vows of universal service)
af: I offer everything to the benefit of all beings. My awareness is primordially pure. The ground of being is already perfect.
sh: The vow without the action — the universal goodness that remains an aspiration but never manifests in the world
ar: The Universal Goodness / The Primordial Ground
cb: Manjushri (the sharp discernment) counterbalances Samantabhadra (the expansive universal goodness). Precision and vastness.

do: Beings in hell realms, the dead, children who died young, the darkest places, the earth's compassion
at: Ksitigarbha ('Earth Womb') is the Bodhisattva who vowed not to achieve Buddhahood until every single being in all the hell realms has been liberated. He descends into the darkest places. In Japan as Jizo he is the guardian of children, of travellers, and of those who died young. The root chakra's supreme Buddhist expression: the compassion that goes into the earth's deepest darkness.
of: Small stone Jizo statues dressed with red bibs (Japanese tradition), offerings for deceased children, the recitation of his mantra Om Ha Ha Ha Vismaye Svaha, earth ceremonies
an: None — he is purely the compassionate presence in darkness
co: Grey (the earth), deep red (the bib — the children's protection), gold (the monk's staff)
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the compassion in the deepest darkness
cr: Grey moonstone, earthy jasper, black tourmaline, dark garnet
bp: Root (he descends into the hell realms — the root chakra's darkest spaces), heart (the compassion that persists in total darkness)
af: There is no darkness too deep for compassion. I bring the light of care into the worst places. No being is abandoned.
sh: The compassion that loses itself in the darkness it enters — the one who descended into hell to save others and forgot the way back
ar: The Hell-Descender / The Children's Guardian
cb: Amitabha (the Buddha of pure bliss in the highest realm) counterbalances Ksitigarbha (the bodhisattva in the deepest hell realm). The highest light and the deepest descent.

do: Unshakeable equanimity, the mirror-like wisdom, the east and the dawn, the transmutation of aggression
at: Akshobhya ('The Immovable') is one of the Five Dhyani Buddhas — the deep blue Buddha of the east, whose wisdom is the mirror-like wisdom (the mind that reflects all without distortion). He transmutes aggression into wisdom. His gesture touches the earth, calling it to witness. He is the root chakra's highest expression: the absolute, unshakeable equanimity that nothing can disturb.
of: Deep blue candles, his mantra Om Akshobhya Hum, offerings of mirror-like clarity (a clear mirror placed at the altar), blue lotus
an: Elephant (his mount — the animal of unshakeable solidity)
co: Deep blue (his body colour)
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the unshakeable foundation
cr: Sapphire, blue tourmaline, lapis lazuli, dark indigo kyanite
bp: Root (the unshakeable earth-witness gesture), third eye (the mirror-like wisdom that reflects without distortion)
af: Om Akshobhya Hum. Nothing can disturb the mirror of my mind. I am unshakeable. My equanimity is complete.
sh: The immovable that becomes rigidity — the unshakeable equanimity that refuses to respond to genuine situations requiring action
ar: The Mirror Wisdom / The Unshakeable
cb: Amitabha (the red Buddha of the west, whose wisdom is discriminating awareness) counterbalances Akshobhya (the blue Buddha of the east, whose wisdom is mirror-like). East and west, reflection and discrimination.

do: Boundless light, the Pure Land (Sukhavati), compassionate rebirth, infinite life
at: Amitabha ('Boundless Light') or Amitayus ('Boundless Life') is the most widely worshipped Buddha in the world — the centre of Pure Land Buddhism across China, Japan, Vietnam, Korea, and Tibet. His Pure Land (Sukhavati) is accessible to any being who sincerely calls his name. The heart chakra's supreme Buddhist expression of boundless compassion-light. Namo Amituofo (the Chinese Pure Land invocation) is perhaps the most recited phrase in human history.
of: Namo Amituofo / Namu Amida Butsu recitation (the Pure Land practice), red lotus, red candles, prayer beads (mala) turned while reciting, offerings of sincere heart
an: Peacock (whose tail feathers are the colours of his paradise)
co: Red (his body colour — the red of infinite compassionate light), amber, gold
dp: Sunday / Sun — the boundless light
cr: Red coral, ruby, rose quartz, amber
bp: Heart (boundless compassion-light), crown (the Pure Land as the highest aspiration)
af: Namo Amituofo. The boundless light is calling me home. I am already in the Pure Land of an open heart.
sh: The Pure Land as escape — the boundless compassion used to avoid the difficulties of this present world
ar: The Boundless Light / The Pure Land Buddha
cb: Akshobhya (the mirror-like equanimity, the imperturbable blue) counterbalances Amitabha (the boundless compassionate warmth, the infinite red). East and west, equanimity and compassion.

do: The jewel-born, equanimity toward wealth, the south and the harvest, the transmutation of pride
at: Ratnasambhava ('Jewel-Born') is the golden Buddha of the south — his wisdom is the wisdom of equality (all beings are equally precious). He transmutes pride into this equalising wisdom. His gesture is the giving gesture (varada mudra — the hand offering gifts). He is the solar plexus's highest expression in Buddhism: the generous authority that gives freely because it knows all things have equal value.
of: Gold candles, his mantra Om Ratnasambhava Tram, golden offerings, the giving gesture made consciously
an: Horse (his mount — the noble steed of the south)
co: Deep gold, yellow (his body colour)
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the jewel of equal value
cr: Citrine, yellow topaz, gold, amber
bp: Solar plexus (the generous authority of equalising wisdom), root (the grounded gift)
af: Om Ratnasambhava Tram. All beings are equally precious. My generosity comes from recognising that there is no hierarchy of worth.
sh: The equality wisdom turned into relativism — the recognition that all things are equal used to avoid discernment
ar: The Jewel of Equal Value / The Golden Giver
cb: Amoghasiddhi (the northern Buddha of accomplished action) counterbalances Ratnasambhava (the southern Buddha of generous equanimity). Giving and accomplishing.

do: All-accomplishing wisdom, fearless action, the north, the transmutation of envy into unfailing action
at: Amoghasiddhi ('Unfailing Accomplishment') is the green Buddha of the north — his wisdom is the all-accomplishing wisdom, the knowledge of how to act in perfect accordance with what is needed. He transmutes envy and jealousy into the fearless, unfailing action that accomplishes all good purposes. His gesture is the fearlessness gesture (abhaya mudra — the palm raised as protection).
of: Green candles, his mantra Om Amoghasiddhi Ah, offerings of actions completed fully and fearlessly
an: Garuda (the great eagle-being, vehicle of swift action)
co: Green (his body colour)
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the accomplishing action
cr: Emerald, green tourmaline, malachite, jade
bp: Solar plexus (the accomplishing will), crown (the all-accomplishing wisdom)
af: Om Amoghasiddhi Ah. My action is fearless and unfailing. I act in perfect accordance with what is needed. Nothing is left incomplete.
sh: The unfailing action that becomes compulsion — the accomplishing wisdom that cannot rest because it is always achieving
ar: The Unfailing Action / The Fear-Dispelling Accomplisher
cb: Ratnasambhava (the generous giver) counterbalances Amoghasiddhi (the fearless accomplisher). Giving and accomplishing, generosity and action.

do: The cosmic illuminator, the dharmadhatu wisdom, the centre, all phenomena as Buddhahood
at: Vairocana ('The Illuminating') is the white central Buddha of the Vajrayana mandala — the supreme Buddha who represents the dharmadhatu wisdom (the wisdom of the total expanse of reality). He embodies the insight that all phenomena, without exception, are already the display of the Buddha's awakened mind. The crown chakra's absolute apex in Buddhist cosmology.
of: White candles, his mantra Om Vairocana Om, offerings of pure presence, the contemplation of all phenomena as Buddha-display
an: Lion (his mount — the lion of dharma)
co: White (the colour of the total expanse of reality)
dp: Sunday / Sun and Uranus — the cosmic illumination
cr: Clear quartz, white selenite, diamond, white calcite
bp: Crown (the total expanse of reality), third eye (all phenomena seen as the display of awareness)
af: Om Vairocana Om. Every phenomenon is the display of the awakened mind. I see everything as it truly is.
sh: The cosmic illumination that bypasses the ordinary — the crown so filled with totality that it cannot function in the particular
ar: The Total Illuminator / The Cosmic Centre
cb: The individual self (the unawakened sense of a separate person) counterbalances Vairocana (the recognition that all phenomena are the display of non-dual awareness). The path and the destination.

do: The founder of Tibetan Buddhism, the Guru Rinpoche, the lotus-born, the master of the wrathful and peaceful
at: Padmasambhava ('Lotus-Born') is the tantric master who brought Buddhism to Tibet in the 8th century and subdued the local spirits, binding them to the dharma. He is simultaneously a historical master and a cosmic Buddha-principle: the 'second Buddha,' the embodiment of all Buddhas in a single form. He has eight primary manifestations. His mantra — Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum — is considered the most complete mantra in Tibetan Buddhism.
of: Guru Rinpoche puja, Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum mantra (the supreme practice), offerings of incense, the study of Nyingma teachings
an: Tiger (his fierce mount in certain forms), snow lion, garuda
co: Red, gold, white — the three colours of his primary form
dp: Wednesday / Jupiter — the master's wisdom
cr: Turquoise (sacred to Tibet), coral, gold, dark ruby
bp: Third eye (the visionary master's sight), crown (the cosmic Buddha he embodies), root (the binding of the earth's spirits to the dharma)
af: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum. The lotus-born master lights my path. I am held in the lineage of awakening.
sh: The guru principle misused — the great teacher's shadow is the use of his vast authority to control rather than liberate
ar: The Lotus-Born Master / The Second Buddha
cb: Shantarakshita (the philosopher-scholar who was the intellectual complement to Padmasambhava's tantric power) counterbalances Padmasambhava. The wrathful tantrika and the peaceful scholar.

do: The Green One, the immortal guide, the hidden master, the initiator of those who cannot find a human teacher
at: Al-Khidr (the Green One) is the most mysterious figure in Islamic mysticism — the immortal guide who drank from the Water of Life and serves as the direct initiator for those who cannot find a human teacher. He appears in the Quran in the story of Moses, performing apparently inexplicable acts (killing a boy, sinking a ship) that later reveal their deeper wisdom. He is the patron saint of Sufi wanderers and the hidden master of the outer edge of the spiritual path.
of: Green candles, prayers of opening at the water's edge, the sincere request for direct guidance, green clothing worn in his honour
an: None fixed — he is the Green One, associated with all green living things
co: Green (the colour of life, of the Water of Life)
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the hidden guide
cr: Emerald, green tourmaline, green jade, malachite
el: Water / Earth (the Water of Life, the green earth)
bp: Crown (the direct initiation beyond lineage), third eye (the sight that sees the deeper reason), root (the Water of Life in the body)
af: The hidden master sees me and guides me. I am open to direct initiation. Even the inexplicable serves a deeper wisdom.
sh: The wisdom that cannot be explained in time — Khidr's acts seem monstrous until their reason is revealed. His shadow is the teacher who acts without the student's understanding, creating harm.
ar: The Green Immortal / The Hidden Guide
cb: Moses (the great law-giver who could not understand Khidr's wisdom) counterbalances Al-Khidr (the mystic wisdom that transcends the law). The law and the beyond-the-law.

do: Pure divine love (mahabba), the love of God for its own sake, the heart beyond fear and hope
at: Rabia al-Adawiyya (717-801 CE) is the mother of Sufi mysticism — the first to articulate the principle of pure love for God beyond fear of hell or hope of paradise. She carried a torch in one hand and a bucket of water in the other: to set fire to paradise and quench the fires of hell, so that people would love God for God's own sake alone. The most radical heart chakra teaching in the mystical tradition.
of: The dedication of one's prayer to pure love without hope of reward, a candle carried as Rabia carried her torch, love letters written to the divine
an: None — she was an ascetic who transcended animal symbolism
co: Pure white (her asceticism), deep rose (the pure love)
dp: Friday / Venus — the pure love that transcends even divine reward
cr: Rose quartz, clear white quartz, pink tourmaline, rhodochrosite
bp: Heart (the pure love for its own sake), crown (the union with the divine beyond all transaction)
af: I love the divine for its own sake, not for reward. My love does not need paradise to justify itself. I carry the torch of pure love.
sh: The love so pure it becomes indifferent to the world — the mystic heart that transcends everything, including the need to return love to other human beings
ar: The Pure Love / The Torch of the Heart
cb: Al-Khidr (the hidden guide — the indirect divine) counterbalances Rabia (the direct heart-love). The guide and the one who needs no guide because she loves directly.

do: The Unity of Being (Wahdat al-Wujud), the cosmic imagination, the Perfect Human (al-Insan al-Kamil)
at: Muhyiddin Ibn Arabi (1165-1240) — 'the Greatest Sheikh' (al-Shaykh al-Akbar) — is the supreme metaphysician of Islamic mysticism. His Fusus al-Hikam (Bezels of Wisdom) and Futuhat al-Makkiyya (Meccan Illuminations) are among the most profound works in all of world mysticism. His central teaching: Wahdat al-Wujud (the Unity of Being) — that there is only God, appearing as all things. The Perfect Human (al-Insan al-Kamil) is the one who has realised this unity.
of: The study of his texts, sitting meditation contemplating 'there is only the One Being appearing as all things,' Arabic calligraphy as offering
an: None — he is pure metaphysical intelligence
co: Black and gold (the colours of ink and illumination), deep indigo
dp: Sunday / Jupiter — the supreme metaphysical intelligence
cr: Lapis lazuli (the wisdom stone), amethyst (the crown of metaphysics), clear quartz
bp: Crown (the Unity of Being), third eye (the cosmic imagination through which God sees the world), throat (the articulation of the deepest mystical truth)
af: There is only the One Being appearing as all things, including me. I am the mirror in which the divine knows itself. The unity is not an idea — it is what I am.
sh: The metaphysics that replaces living experience — the Ibn Arabi scholar who knows Wahdat al-Wujud intellectually but has never tasted it
ar: The Unity of Being / The Perfect Human
cb: Rabia (the pure love) counterbalances Ibn Arabi (the pure knowledge). The heart and the mind at the highest level — the love that transcends understanding and the understanding that transcends love.

do: The divine love poetry, the whirling dervish, the Beloved, the reed's longing for the reed bed
at: Jalal al-Din Rumi (1207-1273) — the most widely read poet in the world, and perhaps the greatest mystic poet in history. His Masnavi (the Quran in Persian, according to Jami) opens with the reed flute crying for its origin. Every verse is a teaching in divine love. The whirling ceremony (Sema) he inspired represents the heart chakra's highest kinetic expression: the body as prayer, turning as the planets turn.
of: The reading of the Masnavi aloud, whirling (the Sema) as prayer, the reed flute played or listened to, offerings of love poetry to the divine
an: Reed (the reed flute — the central symbol of his teaching)
co: White (the whirling dervish's white robe), gold, the deep blue of night sky longing
dp: Friday / Venus — divine love expressed through poetry
cr: Rose quartz, lapis lazuli, moonstone, turquoise (the Islamic mystical stone)
bp: Heart (the divine love that burns through all of Rumi's poetry), crown (the union with the divine Beloved), throat (the divine poetry)
af: I hear the reed crying for its origin in the reed bed. That crying is my prayer. The longing itself is the arrival.
sh: The longing that substitutes for the love — the Rumi lover who is addicted to the longing but afraid of the union
ar: The Reed's Longing / The Whirling Heart
cb: Shams-i-Tabrizi (the wild, homeless mystic who shattered Rumi's world and opened his heart) counterbalances Rumi (the divine poet who could not have existed without Shams). The teacher and the one transformed.

do: Ana'l-Haqq (I am the Truth/God), the mystical union proclaimed aloud, the martyr of divine love
at: Husayn ibn Mansur al-Hallaj (858-922 CE) — the most controversial figure in Islamic mysticism. He proclaimed 'Ana'l-Haqq' (I am the Truth — a divine name) in the streets of Baghdad and was executed for it: crucified and dismembered. He went to his execution joyfully, seeing it as the final merging with the Beloved. He represents the throat and crown chakras' most extreme expression: the divine truth that cannot remain unspoken, no matter the cost.
of: The contemplation of dying for one's deepest truth, prayers that acknowledge the divine within, the courage to speak what the institution forbids
an: None — he is pure proclamation
co: Blood red (the martyr's colour), white (the mystic's robe)
dp: Tuesday / Sun — the truth that costs everything
cr: Ruby (the blood stone), red garnet, dark carnelian
bp: Throat (the proclaimed truth that could not be silenced), crown (the union with God that he declared), heart (the love that made him willing to die for the truth)
af: I am the Truth. I speak what I know even at great cost. The mystical union cannot be contained.
sh: The proclamation before its time — al-Hallaj's shadow is the spiritual truth spoken before the listener can receive it, which destroys instead of liberates
ar: The Proclaimed Union / The Martyr of Truth
cb: Ibn Arabi (the metaphysician who taught the same truth in careful, systematic language) counterbalances al-Hallaj (who shouted the same truth from the streets). The systematic and the ecstatic.

do: The All-Compassionate, the womb-like mercy that encompasses all things
at: Al-Rahman ('The All-Compassionate') is the first and most invoked of the 99 Beautiful Names of God in Islam — it appears in the basmala (Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim) that opens every surah of the Quran. Its root is raham — related to rahm (womb). The divine mercy is womb-like, encompassing, unconditional. In Sufi practice, the Beautiful Names are not merely attributes but portals into the divine nature through meditation.
of: Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim recited at the beginning of every significant action, the Ya Rahman invocation, the contemplation of mercy
an: None — the name encompasses all
co: Deep green (the colour of mercy in Islamic tradition), white, rose
dp: Friday / Moon — the encompassing mercy
cr: Rose quartz, green jade, moonstone, white quartz
el: Water / Ether (the mercy as all-encompassing water)
bp: Heart (the womb-like mercy), crown (the divine quality that encompasses all)
af: Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim. I begin in the name of the all-encompassing mercy. I am held in the womb of divine compassion.
sh: The mercy without justice — the all-encompassing compassion without Al-Adl (the Just), becomes enabling rather than healing
ar: The Womb-Mercy / The All-Compassionate
cb: Al-Jabbar (the Compeller — one of the Names of Power) counterbalances Al-Rahman (the All-Compassionate). The Names of Majesty (jalal) and Names of Beauty (jamal) in their eternal polarity.

do: The Light (of the heavens and earth), the divine illumination, the Light Verse (Ayat al-Nur)
at: Al-Nur ('The Light') is perhaps the most mystically potent of the 99 Names — the Light of the heavens and the earth. The Quran's Light Verse (24:35) is one of the most commented-upon verses in the entire Islamic tradition: 'God is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His light is like a niche in which there is a lamp...' In Sufi practice, the meditation on Al-Nur opens the inner light.
