Ibn Arabi wrote extensively about the Divine Light as the very substance of reality.
of: Ya Nur invocation, candle meditation contemplating the divine light within all things, the Light Verse recited before sleep
an: None — pure light transcends animal form
co: Brilliant white, luminous gold, the shimmer of the inner light
dp: Sunday / Sun — the primordial divine light
cr: Clear quartz, selenite, moonstone, white selenite wand
bp: Third eye (the divine light seen inwardly), crown (the light of the heavens and earth)
af: Ya Nur. I am a lamp through which the divine light shines. The niche, the glass, the oil and the flame are all the same light.
sh: The blinding light — the divine illumination that overwhelms ordinary perception, the mystic vision too intense for daily functioning
ar: The Inner Lamp / The Light of All Lights
cb: Al-Batin (the Inner/Hidden — another of the divine names) counterbalances Al-Nur (the Manifest Light). The hidden and the revealed.

do: The naked mystic, divine love expressed through Kashmiri poetry, the union of Sufi and Shaiva traditions
at: Lalla (Lal Ded, c.1320-1392) — the naked mystic poet of Kashmir who walked the streets singing her vakhs (mystical utterances). She combined Kashmiri Shaivism with Sufi devotion, was said to have attained liberation in this life, and is revered equally by Hindus and Muslims in Kashmir. Her vakhs are among the most direct mystical expressions in any language. She stripped off her clothes as a symbol of stripping the ego. Largely unknown outside Kashmir.
of: Her vakhs recited as prayer, the willingness to strip away what is false, dancing as a form of her ecstatic worship
an: None — she was bare of all coverings
co: None — she wore nothing. The colour of bare skin.
dp: Monday / Moon — the bare feminine divine
cr: Raw, unpolished quartz, earth stones, the unformed
bp: Root (the nakedness of the body claimed as sacred), heart (the divine love that needed no garment), crown (the liberation attained in this life)
af: I strip away everything that is not my true nature. The divine needs no ornament from me. My nakedness is my truth.
sh: The nakedness that frightens the world — Lalla's ecstasy was considered madness. Her shadow is the liberation that cannot communicate with ordinary life.
ar: The Naked Mystic / The Kashmir Ecstatic
cb: Rabia (the Sufi saint of pure love, who was also a woman in a male tradition) counterbalances Lalla (the Kashmir naked mystic who unified traditions). Two women who cracked the containers.

do: The All-Loving, the devoted love of God for creation, love as the divine's most essential quality
at: Al-Wadud ('The All-Loving') — in Sufi theology, love (mahabba, ishq) is not merely an attribute of God but the very substance of creation. The Ibn Arabi school teaches that God created the cosmos out of love — to be known. Al-Wadud is the name of this divine quality of love: not the commanding love (Al-Rahman's mercy) but the devoted, attached love — the love that needs the beloved, that created the cosmos for the sake of that need.
of: Ya Wadud invocation (especially for healing of relationships), prayers of devoted love, rose water offered
an: None — love transcends animal form
co: Rose, deep rose, the colour of devoted love
dp: Friday / Venus — the devoted divine love
cr: Rose quartz, rhodochrosite, pink kunzite, ruby
bp: Heart (the devoted love), sacral (the creative love that needed to be known)
af: Ya Wadud. The divine loves me with a devoted love. I am beloved. Love is the substance of all creation.
sh: The love that needs the beloved — the Al-Wadud shadow is the divine need that created the cosmos and thereby created suffering
ar: The Devoted Love / The Love That Created the World
cb: Al-Ghani (the Self-Sufficient, needing nothing) counterbalances Al-Wadud (the loving one who needs the beloved). Need and self-sufficiency in their divine polarity.

do: The supreme sky god of the Altai, the ruler of the upper world, the giver of shamanic power
at: Ülgen is the supreme deity of the Altaic Turkic peoples of Siberia — the ruler of the upper world (Upper Tengri), dwelling in the highest heaven. He is the giver of all life and the grantor of shamanic power (kur) to the kam (shaman). His messenger is the eagle. He is opposed in the lower world by Erlik. He represents the crown chakra's Siberian expression: the infinite sky that empowers those who serve its will.
of: White horse sacrifice (traditional), offerings of vodka and food burned in fire, the eagle feather, shamanic drum ceremony, prayers facing the sky
an: Eagle (his messenger), white horse (his sacred animal)
co: White (the colour of the pure upper world), sky blue, gold
dp: Sunday / Sun — the supreme sky ruler
cr: Clear quartz, white calcite, meteorite, eagle bone
bp: Crown (the sky ruler's domain), solar plexus (the strength he grants to the shaman)
af: The sky above me is the body of the divine. I am empowered by what is highest. The eagle carries my prayers upward.
sh: The sky god too remote to reach — the crown that requires the shaman's sacrifice to approach
ar: The Sky Sovereign / The Shaman-Empowerer
cb: Erlik (the lord of the lower world) counterbalances Ülgen (the lord of the upper world). The supreme Altaic cosmic polarity of upper and lower.

do: The lord of the underworld, death, disease, the lower world, the testing of souls
at: Erlik Khan is the supreme lord of the lower world (Erlik's world) in Altaic Siberian cosmology — the most important underworld deity of northern Asia. He governs death, disease, and the fate of souls after death. Shamans must negotiate with him to retrieve lost souls. He is depicted as a great black man with a beard, wearing dark robes. He and Ülgen represent the supreme Siberian cosmic polarity.
of: Black offerings, vodka, the shamanic drum journey to the lower world, negotiations conducted with respect rather than servility
an: Black bull (his sacred animal), black snake, the creatures of the underworld
co: Black, deep brown, the colours of the underworld earth
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the lord of the lower world
cr: Black tourmaline, obsidian, dark garnet, jet
bp: Root (the underworld as the root's shadow domain), solar plexus (the authority that must be negotiated with rather than commanded)
af: I negotiate with the lord of the lower world with respect. What has been lost can be retrieved. Death is a domain that can be entered and left.
sh: The soul stolen by Erlik that is never retrieved — the depression and illness that comes from the permanent loss of vital essence
ar: The Underworld Khan / The Soul's Tester
cb: Ülgen (the supreme upper world) counterbalances Erlik (the supreme lower world). The Siberian axis mundi.

do: The ancestor spirits of the Buryat people, the helpers and guides of shamans, the whitened shamans
at: Ongons are the ancestral spirits central to Buryat shamanism — the souls of deceased shamans and powerful ancestors who can be invoked for help, healing and guidance. They are housed in small figures (ongon figures) kept at home altars. Buryat shamanism is among the most complete and ancient shamanic traditions still practiced, with its black shamans (serving Erlik/lower world) and white shamans (serving Ülgen/upper world) representing the full cosmic spectrum.
of: Ongon figures kept at home shrines, milk vodka (arhi) offered, the Buryat shamanic ceremony (tailagan), tobacco and food offerings
an: The animal spirits of each individual ongon — eagle, wolf, bear, specific to lineage
co: White (white ongons/shamans) and black (black ongons/shamans)
dp: Saturday / Moon — the ancestors' time
cr: Any stones given by the ongon in the shaman's vision, bear bone, eagle feather
bp: Root (the ancestral roots that the ongon guards), third eye (the vision the ongon opens in the shaman)
af: My ancestors are present and helpful. The ongon spirits guide my healing. I am connected to a living lineage.
sh: The ongon that possesses rather than guides — the ancestor spirit that takes over rather than serves
ar: The Living Ancestors / The Shamanic Lineage
cb: Ülgen (the impersonal sky supreme) counterbalances the Ongons (the personal ancestral helpers). The universal and the personal.

do: The master spirits of places — mountains, rivers, lakes, trees, the earth's local consciousness
at: Eezi (or Ezhi, Ezi) are the master spirits or 'owners' of specific places in Tuvan and broader Siberian shamanism — every significant mountain, river, lake, rock formation, and tree has its own Eezi. These are not abstract forces but specific conscious presences that must be acknowledged, respected and offered to before any activity on their land. Tuva, in the geographical centre of Asia, is considered the heart of Siberian shamanism.
of: Offerings tied to trees or left at spring sources (aas) before crossing into new territory, milk, vodka, food placed at the base of sacred mountains, the first sip of any drink offered to the local Eezi
an: The animal most characteristic of each place (mountain Eezi may appear as a bear, river Eezi as a fish or otter)
co: The colours of the specific place — no universal colour
dp: Saturday / Earth — the spirit of the specific place
cr: Local stones from the specific place (not imported — the Eezi speaks through the stone of its own location)
el: Earth / Water / Air (each place has its own element)
bp: Root (the connection to specific ground), heart (the respect for what lives in the place)
af: I acknowledge the spirit of this place before I enter it. The land has a consciousness and I am a guest. I offer before I take.
sh: The Eezi offended — when the master spirit of a place is disrespected, illness and misfortune follow those who violated its territory
ar: The Spirit of Place / The Land's Master
cb: Ülgen (the sky deity — the impersonal universal above) counterbalances the Eezi (the personal specific spirit of each earthly place). The vast and the intimate.

do: The supreme deity of the Nenets reindeer people, the sky god of the Arctic tundra
at: The Nenets are one of the largest indigenous peoples of the Russian Arctic — reindeer herders of the Yamal peninsula and beyond. Their supreme deity is called Num (the sky) or Ngan'typoi — the great sky force. Their shamans (tadibei) mediate between this sky force and the world of spirits. The Nenets shamanic tradition is among the least-studied indigenous traditions in the world.
of: Reindeer offerings (traditional), prayers facing the sky, the shamanic drum ceremony, offerings at sacred poles
an: Reindeer (the supreme sacred animal of the Nenets), eagle, the white bear
co: White (the Arctic sky), deep blue (the polar night)
dp: Sunday — the sky's day
cr: Polar quartz, white calcite, reindeer antler, meteorite (from the sky)
bp: Crown (the Arctic sky deity), root (the reindeer herder's connection to the tundra)
af: I am under the vast Arctic sky. The reindeer know the paths I have forgotten. The tundra is a teacher.
sh: The Arctic sky's silence — the crown that is so vast and so cold it cannot respond to human need
ar: The Arctic Sky / The Reindeer People's Supreme
cb: The earth spirits of the tundra counterbalance Ngan'typoi (the sky deity). The frozen earth below counterbalancing the vast sky above.

do: The white god of the upper world, the giver of all creation, the sender of rain
at: Bai-Ulgan (also written Bay-Ülgen — 'the Exceedingly Great') is in some traditions the same as Ülgen, in others a distinct aspect — the specifically white creative deity of the upper world who created all living things and sends rain. In the great Altaic shamanic ceremony (Kам algysh), the shaman ascends through seventeen heavens to reach Bai-Ulgan and make offerings on behalf of the community.
of: White mare's milk, the great shamanic drum journey through seventeen heavens, fire ceremony, offerings of the community's needs
an: White horse (the ultimate offering), eagle, white crane
co: Brilliant white, the colour of the upper world's seventeenth heaven
dp: Sunday / Jupiter — the creative white above
cr: White quartz, clear quartz, white selenite, moonstone
bp: Crown (the seventeenth heaven of the shaman's ascent), third eye (the vision opened by reaching the highest)
af: I ascend through all the levels to the highest. The white creator knows my community's needs. I carry the prayers of my people upward.
sh: The ascent that does not return — the shaman who reaches Bai-Ulgan and cannot descend back to the human world
ar: The Seventeen Heavens / The White Creator
cb: Erlik Khan (the dark lord of the lower world) counterbalances Bai-Ulgan (the white lord of the upper world). The shamanic axis.

do: Disease-causing spirits, the shadow beings of the lower world, the tests of the Yakut shaman
at: Abasy are the disease spirits of Yakut (Sakha) shamanism — malevolent beings that cause illness by stealing or disturbing the soul. Yakut shamans (oyun — male, udagan — female) battle the abasy to retrieve stolen souls. The Yakut of eastern Siberia have one of the most elaborate shamanic cosmologies, with detailed accounts of the spirit world, the tree of life (Aal Luuk Mas), and the battles of healing.
of: Not invoked — known and navigated. The shaman's battle against abasy is the model: illness confronted with clear sight and sovereign will
an: The dark versions of all animals — shadow eagle, shadow bear
co: Dark, shadow colours — the colours of illness and obstruction
dp: Tuesday / Pluto — the spirit illness
cr: Black tourmaline (protection against abasy), obsidian, onyx
bp: Root (they steal root chakra vitality), solar plexus (the will needed to battle them)
af: I confront the spirits of my illness with clear sight. My shaman self knows how to retrieve what has been taken. I am whole.
sh: The abasy as the truth about illness — sometimes what we call a disease spirit is the suppressed truth of what has caused the illness
ar: The Shadow Forces / The Illness Navigated
cb: The great shaman (the oyun/udagan who battles the abasy) counterbalances the abasy (the forces of illness). Healing sovereignty and its shadow.

do: The World Tree, the axis of the three worlds, the cosmic centre of Yakut cosmology
at: Aal Luuk Mas ('The Great Sacred Tree') is the World Tree of Yakut cosmology — the cosmic birch tree whose roots reach into the underworld and whose crown touches the highest heaven. The creator deity Ürüng Ayıı Toyon resides at its crown. The shaman climbs the World Tree in their journeys between realms. The tree's sap is the ambrosia of the gods. This is the Siberian expression of the universal World Tree (Yggdrasil in Norse, Ashvattha in Hindu, etc.).
of: The birch tree as sacred (offerings tied to birch trees), the shamanic drum as the World Tree traversed, prayers at the base of ancient trees
an: All animals that inhabit the World Tree's three worlds: eagle at the crown, bear in the middle, snake at the root
co: White birch bark, the green of leaves, the brown of roots
dp: Wednesday / Earth — the cosmic axis
cr: Petrified wood, birch bark, tree resin, amber (fossilised tree sap)
el: Earth / Air / Ether — the tree spans all elements
bp: Root (the tree's roots in the underworld), the entire spine as the axis mundi, crown (the tree's canopy in the highest heaven)
af: My spine is the World Tree. My roots go deep and my crown reaches high. I am the axis between the worlds.
sh: The World Tree cut — the cosmic centre severed. The Yakut know that the health of the World Tree mirrors the health of the community.
ar: The Cosmic Axis / The Sacred Birch
cb: Ülgen (dwelling at the crown) and Erlik (dwelling at the root) counterbalance through the World Tree — the cosmic axis holds the polarity of the supreme opposites.

do: Smallpox, rain, the earth's heat, disease and its cure, the fierce village mother
at: Mariamman is the most widely worshipped goddess of Tamil Nadu and South India — the village mother whose name connects to both Mari (rain/disease) and Amman (mother). She governs smallpox and all fever-diseases; she both sends and heals them. Her energy is immense, fierce and immediate. The fire-walking ceremony (Thimithi) performed in her honour is among the most dramatic acts of devotion on Earth. She is the root and solar plexus chakra's South Indian supreme expression.
of: Turmeric, lemon, neem leaves (her sacred plant — used to brush away disease), fire-walking ceremony, the chariot festival (ther), votive offerings of clay figures, red sarees
an: None — she manifests through fever heat and rain
co: Red (her primary colour), yellow turmeric, the dark green of neem
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the fierce mother's fire
cr: Red jasper, carnelian, bloodstone, turmeric-yellow stone
el: Fire / Water (she governs the fever-heat and the rain that relieves it)
bp: Root (the village's ground of being), solar plexus (the fierce maternal fire of protection and disease)
af: The fierce mother's fire burns away what makes me sick. I walk through the fire and I am not burned. Her rain relieves my fever.
sh: The village goddess who becomes the plague — Mariamman's shadow is the disease she governs, when she is disrespected or her order is violated
ar: The Fever Mother / The Fire-Walking Goddess
cb: Murugan (her often-paired divine son or companion — the young, beautiful warrior god) counterbalances Mariamman (the fierce, earthy mother). The graceful and the fierce.

do: Dharma, celibacy, the forest path, devotion through austerity, the synthesis of Shiva and Vishnu
at: Ayyappan (Dharma Sasta) is one of the most widely worshipped deities in Kerala and Tamil Nadu — he is considered the son of Shiva and Mohini (Vishnu in female form), representing the synthesis of the two supreme male deities. His great temple at Sabarimala is one of the world's most visited pilgrimage sites. Pilgrims undergo 41 days of celibacy and austerity to visit him. He governs the solar plexus's ascetic discipline as the path to liberation.
of: The Sabarimala pilgrimage (41 days of austerity), the irumudi (the sacred two-compartment bag), coconut oil lamp, black clothing, the mantra 'Swamiye Saranam Ayyappa'
an: Tiger (he rode a tigress — symbol of dharma taming the wild), elephant
co: Black (the pilgrim's colour of austerity), saffron, deep forest green
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the austerity and dharma
cr: Black tourmaline (the pilgrim's protection), tiger's eye, dark garnet
bp: Root (the forest path), solar plexus (the 41-day disciplined will), crown (the liberation through the ascetic path)
af: Swamiye Saranam Ayyappa. I discipline my desires in service of my highest purpose. The forest path is the dharma path.
sh: The celibacy that becomes suppression — the austerity practised to dominate the body rather than transform it
ar: The Ascetic Path / The Forest Dharma
cb: Murugan (the graceful, beautiful divine warrior) counterbalances Ayyappan (the austere, celibate forest devotee). Embodied grace and ascetic renunciation.

do: War, beauty, youth, Tamil language, the sacred vel (spear), the six-faced god
at: Murugan (Tamil) or Karthikeya (Sanskrit) — the six-faced son of Shiva, born to destroy the demon Surapadman. He is the supreme deity of Tamil culture and the patron of the Tamil language. He rides a peacock, carries the vel (sacred spear), and represents youth, beauty, martial power, and the precision of wisdom. The Thaipusam festival in his honour (where devotees pierce their bodies with vels) is one of the most dramatic devotional events on Earth.
of: Vel (sacred spear) at the altar, peacock feather, Thaipusam ceremony, the Kandhar Anubhuti and Thiruppugazh recited in Tamil, six-faced imagery
an: Peacock (his sacred vehicle — the beauty that destroys the cobra of ego), cock (his banner)
co: Red, gold, the iridescent blue-green of the peacock
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the sacred warrior's beauty
cr: Ruby (the warrior stone), peacock tourmaline, carnelian, green peacock jade
bp: Solar plexus (the warrior's power and precision), sacral (the divine beauty), third eye (the six faces see in all six directions simultaneously)
af: The vel of wisdom pierces all illusion. I am the young warrior who sees in every direction. My beauty is a form of power.
sh: The divine youth who never matures — the beautiful warrior who remains forever the son, never the full embodiment of his own sovereign wisdom
ar: The Six-Faced Warrior / The Tamil God
cb: Ganesha (his elder brother — the remover of obstacles, the grounded) counterbalances Murugan (the swift warrior, the beautiful, the piercing precision). The elephant and the peacock.

do: Divine bridal mysticism, the soul's longing for union with Vishnu, the sacred poetry of Thiruppavai
at: Andal (c.9th century CE) is the only female poet among the Twelve Alvars (Tamil Vaishnava saint-poets) and the only woman considered to have achieved direct union with Vishnu. She refused to marry any human, wearing garlands she made for Vishnu herself as her own adornment first (a breach of devotional protocol that became the ultimate act of love). She is said to have literally entered the image of Vishnu at the Ranganathaswamy temple and disappeared. Her Thiruppavai is recited daily across South India.
of: Garlands worn first by the worshipper before offering to the deity (her radical devotional gesture), Thiruppavai recited in the mornings of the Tamil month Margazhi
an: Parrot (her name means 'she who rules'), the peacock
co: Gold, the colour of saffron, deep green (Vishnu's colour)
dp: Friday / Venus — the divine bridal longing
cr: Yellow sapphire (devotion to Vishnu), rose quartz (the bridal love), gold
bp: Heart (the bridal mysticism — the soul as bride of the divine), crown (the union achieved), sacral (the erotic dimension of divine love)
af: I am already wearing the garland meant for the divine. My love for the eternal is so complete I have dissolved into it.
sh: The love so focused on the divine that all human love becomes impossible — Andal married no human, literally dissolving into the divine
ar: The Bridal Mystic / The Dissolved Lover
cb: Rabia al-Adawiyya (the Sufi who also refused human love for divine love) counterbalances in a cosmic sense with Andal. Two women from different traditions who both chose divine love exclusively.

tr: Dravidian / Tamil and Sri Lankan (South India)
do: Fidelity, the power of a chaste wife, righteous anger, the goddess born from a broken ankle-ring
at: Pattini (Tamil: Kannagi) is one of the most dramatic sacred figures in South Asian tradition — the epic heroine of the Silappatikaram (The Tale of the Ankle Bracelet) who burned the city of Madurai with the power of her rage at her husband's unjust execution. She tore off her breast and threw it at Madurai, setting it aflame. She became a goddess of fidelity, justice, and the cosmic power of a truly faithful woman's wrath. Still widely worshipped in Sri Lanka.
of: Ankle bracelets offered, votive lamps, camphor burning ceremonies, prayers for justice, rice and flowers
an: Lion (her wrath), the peacock
co: Red (the fire of her wrath), gold (the ankle bracelet's gold), white (her chastity)
dp: Tuesday / Mars and Friday / Venus — righteous wrath and faithful love
cr: Red garnet (her wrath), gold (her ankle ring), moonstone (her fidelity)
bp: Root (the ground she set ablaze), solar plexus (the righteous wrath), heart (the fidelity that generated the power)
af: My fidelity is a cosmic force. The righteousness of my love generates power. I burn away injustice with the fire of my faithfulness.
sh: The wrath that cannot be recalled — once Kannagi's city-burning begins, even she cannot stop it. The righteous anger that becomes the consuming fire.
ar: The Faithful Fury / The Ankle-Ring Goddess
cb: Kovalan (her unfaithful husband whose death she avenged) counterbalances Pattini (the faithful wife whose devotion became cosmic fire). Faithfulness and its testing.

do: The primordial power, the mother before all mothers, the Dravidian ground of sacred feminine
at: Aadi Shakti in the Dravidian tradition refers to the primordial sacred feminine that underlies all the South Indian mother goddesses — the ur-mother from whom Mariamman, Durga, Kali, Parvati, and all the amman-goddesses emerge. The South Indian tradition has an unbroken matrilineal sacred tradition that is older than the Sanskrit traditions and forms the bedrock of the living goddess worship of Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Karnataka.
of: The unified ceremony that acknowledges all the amman-goddesses as one, the offerings made at the sacred grove (kavu), the village goddess ceremony
an: Tiger (the Dravidian lion-tiger that carries the primal power), serpent (the earth serpent of the Dravidian goddess), peacock
co: Red, the deep earth colours of South India
dp: Tuesday / Earth — the primal Dravidian feminine
cr: Red coral, carnelian, turmeric-yellow stone, black tourmaline
bp: Root (the Dravidian sacred feminine as the foundation of South Indian civilisation), solar plexus (the fierce mother's fire), crown (the primordial power above all forms)
af: The oldest mother in the world holds me. The sacred feminine of the South is my ground. I am rooted in a tradition older than history.
sh: The mother-power that no longer has its sacred space — the Dravidian sacred feminine displaced by later traditions
ar: The Dravidian Mother / The Ground of All Ammans
cb: Murugan (the Tamil male divine) counterbalances Aadi Shakti (the Dravidian female divine). The son and the mother.

do: The fish-eyed goddess, divine sovereignty, Madurai's queen, the warrior goddess who became the perfect wife
at: Meenakshi is the tutelary goddess of Madurai — one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world. Her fish-shaped eyes (meena = fish, akshi = eye) symbolise her all-seeing divine perception. She was born as a warrior princess who conquered all three worlds before she met Shiva (as Sundareswarar) and her third breast disappeared — signifying she had found her divine match. The Meenakshi Amman temple is one of the greatest sacred sites in India.
of: Fish offerings, jasmine flowers, the Meenakshi Amman puja at the great temple, parrot (her vehicle)
an: Parrot (her primary vehicle — the bird of Kamadeva and divine speech)
co: Green (the colour of her form — the green of her fish-eyes and skin in some depictions), gold
dp: Friday / Venus — the divine feminine sovereignty
cr: Emerald (the fish-eye colour), green tourmaline, gold
bp: Third eye (her fish-eyes that see everything), solar plexus (the warrior queen's sovereign power), sacral (the divine marriage energy)
af: My eyes see in all directions with the clarity of the fish's all-around vision. My warrior power becomes the sovereign power of love. I am the queen of my own sacred city.
sh: The sovereign who conquered all three worlds but needed her third breast to disappear before she could truly meet her divine match — the sovereignty that must yield to love
ar: The Fish-Eyed Queen / The Three-World Conqueror
cb: Sundareswarar (her divine consort — the beautiful form of Shiva) counterbalances Meenakshi (the warrior queen). The fierce and the gentle; the conqueror and the beloved.

do: The ocean, all sea creatures, fishing, the primal waters of creation, the father of fish
at: Tangaroa (Kanaloa in Hawaiian, Tangaroa in Maori/Samoan/Tongan) is the supreme ocean deity of the Polynesian world — one of the great original Polynesian gods, the father of all sea creatures and the guardian of the deep ocean. In the Maori tradition he was a great sea atua; in the Society Islands he is the supreme creator. He represents the vast Polynesian understanding of the ocean as the creative source of all life.
of: Fish offerings (the first catch returned to the ocean), ocean ceremonies, deep blue candles, water poured to the ocean, prayers before and after fishing
an: Octopus (his sacred animal — he often appears as or is accompanied by an octopus), all fish, the whale
co: Deep ocean blue, the green of deep water, black (the deep ocean's darkness)
dp: Monday / Neptune — the supreme ocean
cr: Aquamarine, dark blue sapphire, sea glass, abalone shell
bp: Sacral (the creative primal waters), crown (the creator god of the ocean world)
af: The ocean is alive with consciousness. I navigate its depths with respect. The primal waters carry the memory of all creation.
sh: The ocean's anger — the storm and the flood that Tangaroa sends when the sea is disrespected
ar: The Ocean Creator / The Father of Fish
cb: Tāne (the god of forests and birds — the land) counterbalances Tangaroa (the god of the ocean). Sea and land as the Polynesian supreme polarity.

do: Forests, birds, the creation of the first human, light, the separation of sky and earth
at: Tāne Mahuta ('Tane the Great Forest') is the Maori god of forests and birds — the deity who separated his parents Ranginui (Sky Father) and Papatūānuku (Earth Mother) to create the world of light. He created the first human (Hineahuone) from red clay and breathed life into her through her nostrils. He sought the sacred knowledge (kete wānanga) in the heavens. The largest kauri tree in New Zealand bears his name.
of: Offerings made in forests (especially to ancient trees), bird feathers placed at the altar, the acknowledgment of trees as sacred beings, conservation as ceremony
an: All birds (he is their father), the huia (the sacred extinct bird of Maori tradition)
co: Green (the deep green of the New Zealand forest), the brown of ancient kauri wood
dp: Thursday / Venus — the loving creative force
cr: Greenstone (pounamu — the sacred jade of New Zealand), tree resin, emerald
bp: Heart (the creator of humans — breathing life through the nose), root (the separation of earth and sky that created the world), crown (the three baskets of knowledge he retrieved)
af: I separate what needs to be separated so that light can enter. I breathe life into what was clay. The forest is my cathedral.
sh: The separation of sky and earth — Tāne's act of creation was also an act of grief for his parents, who forever reach toward each other across the world he made
ar: The Forest Creator / The Sky-Earth Separator
cb: Tangaroa (the ocean) counterbalances Tāne (the forest and land). The great Maori elemental polarity: sea and forest.

do: The moon, tapa cloth, the ocean depths, creative art, the ancestral feminine
at: Hina is one of the most widespread figures in all of Polynesia — she appears in Hawaiian, Maori, Samoan, Tongan, and Tahitian traditions. In some versions she is Maui's mother or sister; in others she is the moon goddess who escaped to the moon to avoid an unbearable marriage. In Samoa she is the goddess who beats tapa cloth by the light of the moon. She is the Polynesian sacred feminine's most universal expression.
of: Tapa cloth offered (or the making of tapa as ceremony), moonwater, the pounding of bark cloth at the full moon, offerings at the ocean's edge at night
an: Shark (in some traditions, her guardian), the turtle
co: White (the moon's colour), the silver of moonlight on water
dp: Monday / Moon — the Polynesian moon goddess
cr: Moonstone, white coral, pearl, selenite
bp: Sacral (the creative feminine arts — tapa making as sacred work), crown (the moon), third eye (the woman who chose to live on the moon rather than suffer below)
af: I choose the moon over an unbearable earth. My creative work is as regular and luminous as the moon's rhythm. I am the Polynesian feminine.
sh: The escape to the moon — Hina's shadow is the woman who chose to leave the world rather than transform it
ar: The Moon Weaver / The Tapa Maker
cb: Maui (the sun-tamer, the active hero) counterbalances Hina (the moon, the creative artist). Sun and moon, hero and goddess.

do: Agriculture, fertility, rain, peace, the harvest festivals (Makahiki), healing
at: Lono is one of the four great Hawaiian akua (gods) — the deity of agriculture, fertility, rain and the Makahiki festival (a four-month annual period of peace, sports, and religious observance in old Hawaii). He is the peaceful face of divine masculine: not the warrior but the farmer, the healer, the one who brings the rains that make the land fertile. Captain Cook was mistakenly identified as Lono when he arrived at Hawaii during the Makahiki festival — with devastating consequences.
of: The Makahiki ceremony (the agricultural new year festival), rain water caught and offered, healing plants, the dedication of farming to him
an: Pig (his sacred animal — offered in the Makahiki ceremony), fish
co: White (his colour — he wears white tapa), the deep green of fertile growth
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the agricultural abundance
cr: Green jade, green aventurine, white coral, rain crystal
bp: Root (the agricultural earth), heart (the peace of the Makahiki, the cessation of war for the harvest)
af: I plant in peace. The rain comes when I honour the earth. The harvest is the proof of the cycle honoured.
sh: The god expected to return but whose return brings destruction — Lono's shadow is the divine expected from outside that devastates from within
ar: The Peace-Farmer / The Rain-Caller
cb: Kū (the god of war — the counter to Lono's peace during the non-Makahiki months) counterbalances Lono. Peace and war, planting season and battle season.

do: The deep ocean, sea creatures, the underworld, healing, partnership with Kāne
at: Kanaloa is one of the four great Hawaiian akua — the god of the deep ocean, squid, and the underworld. He is always paired with Kāne (the fresh water, the sky) — together they created the islands, the plants, and the first humans. He is the dark, deep complement to Kāne's bright freshness. After missionary contact, Kanaloa was falsely identified with Satan — the demonisation of the Polynesian ocean underworld.
of: Deep sea offerings, the paired ceremony with Kāne (fresh and salt water combined), squid, deep blue offerings
an: Squid and octopus (his sacred creatures), all deep sea creatures
co: Deep ocean black and blue, the darkness of the deep water
dp: Monday / Saturn — the deep ocean and the underworld
cr: Dark aquamarine, black pearl, obsidian, dark sapphire
bp: Sacral (the deep creative waters), root (the underworld aspect), crown (his pairing with Kāne creates the cosmic whole)
af: The deep ocean within me holds ancient wisdom. The underworld is not evil — it is the necessary depth. I dive and return.
sh: The colonial demonisation of Kanaloa — his shadow is the false identification with evil that erased his true nature as the ocean's depth-wisdom
ar: The Deep Ocean / The Dark Twin of Kāne
cb: Kāne (the sky, the fresh water, the light) counterbalances Kanaloa (the deep ocean, the salt water, the dark). The supreme Hawaiian polarity.

do: Creation, the sky, the trickster who helped humanity, the creator of practical things
at: Tagaro (also Tangaloa in related traditions) is the creator deity of much of Vanuatu and the surrounding Melanesian islands — a sky deity who created the world and, in many versions, is a benevolent trickster who helped humanity against the often-hostile Melanesian supernatural world. His tradition bridges Polynesian and Melanesian cosmologies and is among the least-studied sacred traditions in the Pacific.
of: Sky offerings (food and drink placed on elevated platforms or thrown upward), the naming of good things as his gifts
an: Eagle (the sky bird), all birds of the sky
co: Blue (the sky), white (the creator's purity)
dp: Sunday / Jupiter — the benevolent creator
cr: Sky blue topaz, white quartz, clear celestite
bp: Crown (the sky creator), throat (his creative speech), heart (his benevolence toward humanity)
af: The sky creator made this world with benevolent intent. I receive its gifts with gratitude. Creation intended my flourishing.
sh: The creator who cannot control what he created — Tagaro's shadow is the deity who made humanity good but cannot prevent them from choosing otherwise
ar: The Benevolent Creator / The Sky Trickster
cb: The hostile supernatural forces (the anti-creator in Melanesian tradition) counterbalance Tagaro (the benevolent creator).

do: The fire of the earth (stolen from Mahuika), the islands fished from the sea, the slowing of the sun, mortality's limit
at: Maui appears in every Polynesian tradition with extraordinary consistency — Hawaiian, Maori, Samoan, Tongan, Tahitian, and all others. His canonical feats: he fished the islands up from the sea floor with his grandmother's jawbone as a hook; he lassoed the sun and beat it to make the days longer; he stole fire from his ancestress Mahuika (the fire-goddess) for humanity; and he was killed attempting to win immortality by entering the body of the sleeping goddess of death Hine-nui-te-po. He is the most complete hero archetype in the Pacific.
of: Fish hooks (his supreme symbol), fire ceremony (in honour of the fire he stole), the sun greeted each morning as one he has tamed
an: Birds (his brothers transformed into birds), fish, the rooster (who disturbed his attempt to win immortality)
co: Red (the fire), blue (the ocean), gold (the slowed sun)
dp: Tuesday / Sun — the hero who conquered the sun itself
cr: Black tourmaline (his magic jawbone hook), ocean jasper, carnelian (the fire), dark obsidian
el: Fire / Water — the fire he stole and the ocean he fished from
bp: Solar plexus (the impossible heroic will), throat (the trickster's cunning speech), root (the islands he pulled from the earth's foundation)
af: I reach for what seems impossible and I pull it from the deep. I slow the sun when it moves too fast. The fire of transformation is mine.
sh: The rooster's crow — Maui was killed because a small bird laughed and woke the sleeping death goddess. His shadow is the hero defeated by what seems trivial.
ar: The Ultimate Polynesian Hero / The World-Transformer
cb: Hine-nui-te-po (the goddess of death — who killed him) counterbalances Maui (the trickster-hero who overcame everything but death). The death that could not be overcome.

do: Iron, war, fire, the Haitian Revolution, political resistance, the warrior who fights for justice
at: Ogou Feray is the most important of the Ogou family of warrior Lwa — the divine warrior of fire and iron who is specifically associated with the Haitian Revolution (1791-1804). He is invoked in political resistance and fierce justice. He carries a machete and wears red. His energy is raw, powerful, and direct. He is the solar plexus principle of the revolutionary: the fire that will not tolerate oppression.
of: Red rum, cigars, a machete or sword, red cloth, iron objects, the call 'Ogou, Ogou Feray, Ago Feray'
an: Horse (like his Yoruba counterpart Ogun), rooster
co: Red and blue (his specific colours — the machete and the flag of Haiti)
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the revolutionary fire
cr: Red jasper, iron, carnelian, fire opal
bp: Solar plexus (the revolutionary will), root (the machete that clears the oppressor's path)
af: The revolutionary fire burns in me. I fight for justice with Ogou's iron will. I raise the machete against what oppresses.
sh: The warrior who makes the revolution but cannot make the peace — Ogou's shadow is the revolutionary who can only fight, who cannot put down the machete
ar: The Revolutionary Warrior / The Iron Justice
cb: Erzulie Freda (love, beauty, the tender heart) counterbalances Ogou Feray (war, iron, the fierce justice). Love and war — the supreme Haitian Vodou polarity.

do: The spirit of the first young dead, those who died violently or before their time, the gentle face of death
at: Ghede Nibo is among the most poignant of the Ghede family — the spirit of the first young person to die. He mediates between the living and the young dead. Where Baron Samedi represents the universal, philosophically comic face of death, Ghede Nibo is the tender, sorrowful face: the one who accompanies those who died too soon, who speaks for the children, who is himself an eternal adolescent.
of: Black and purple candles, rum (milder than Baron Samedi's pepper rum), offerings for children who have died, messages to young relatives who died early
an: None specific — the tenderness is itself his presence
co: Black and purple (like all the Ghede family)
dp: Saturday / Moon — the tender dead
cr: Black tourmaline, amethyst, purple calcite
bp: Heart (the tenderness for those who died young), root (the earliest death — the young body returned to earth)
af: I speak for those who died before they could speak for themselves. The young dead are still with us. No death before its time is forgotten.
sh: The grief that cannot move — Ghede Nibo's shadow is the perpetual mourning for what died young, the sorrow that becomes identity
ar: The Young Dead / The Tender Ghede
cb: Baron Samedi (the universal, philosophical death) counterbalances Ghede Nibo (the particular, sorrowful young death). Universal death and the death that arrives too soon.

do: The sacred twins, duality, the divine polarity, the first children of the divine, the mystery of two-ness
at: The Marasa are the divine twins of Haitian Vodou — one of the most mysterious and powerful of all the Lwa. In Vodou theology, the twins are served first in ceremony, even before Legba, because the divine mystery of twoness (the polarity that makes the world) must be acknowledged before any other work. The Marasa represent the cosmic principle that creation requires duality: nothing can exist without its counterpart.
of: Two of everything — two cups, two plates, two candles. Children's food (corn, sweets, toys). Twinned offerings.
an: None specific — they appear as children
co: Red and blue (the two colours together), occasionally shown as the contrast of any two opposites
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the divine communication of twoness
cr: Two matched stones placed together (any stone — but matched), two crystals of the same type
el: All elements — both of them together
bp: Sacral (the creative duality), crown (the cosmic mystery of two-ness that generates all things)
af: Everything requires its counterpart. I honour the twin principle within me. Duality is not division — it is the condition of all creation.
sh: The twin separated from its other — the Marasa shadow is the profound desolation of the twin without its counterpart
ar: The Sacred Twins / The Divine Duality
cb: They are their own counterbalance — the Marasa represent the irresolvable polarity that must be held rather than resolved.

do: Fierce maternal love, Petro fire, independence of women, the sword of the Haitian Revolution
at: In Haitian Vodou cosmology there are two major 'nations' of spirits: the Rada (cool, ancient, African-origin) and the Petro (hot, fierce, born from the crucible of slavery and revolution). Ezili Dantor is the supreme Petro Lwa — the fierce mother who carries a knife, protects single mothers and children, and whose tongue was cut out but whose power needs no words. She was invoked at the Bois Caïman ceremony that initiated the Haitian Revolution.
of: Dark rum, fried pork, a knife, black candles, donations to single mothers and children's causes
an: Black pig (her sacred animal), serpent
co: Black, red, dark blue (Petro fire colours)
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the Petro fire of fierce protection
cr: Red garnet, black tourmaline, iron, obsidian
bp: Solar plexus (the fierce will of the revolutionary mother), heart (the protection of children and the vulnerable), root (the primal Petro fire)
af: I protect what I love with the knife of Ezili Dantor. I do not need words — my fierce presence speaks. No force will harm my children.
sh: The fierce love that consumes — the protective fire that destroys what it was trying to protect
ar: The Petro Fire Mother / The Revolutionary Knife
cb: Ezili Freda Rada (the cool, refined love Lwa) counterbalances Ezili Dantor Petro (the hot, fierce love Lwa). The two Ezilis — gentleness and fury.

do: Diplomacy, the political warrior, the military officer who negotiates as well as fights
at: Ogou Badagri is the most politically sophisticated of the Ogou family — less raw fire than Ogou Feray, he is the Lwa of political strategy and diplomatic warfare. He wears military dress, drinks rum, but his approach is more measured than his brother. He represents the solar plexus principle of power that knows when to speak and when to fight.
of: Red rum (not as fiery as Feray's offering), military insignia, the offering of measured political speech
an: Horse, rooster
co: Red and gold (the officer's colours)
dp: Saturday / Jupiter — the strategic political power
cr: Red garnet, iron, gold
bp: Solar plexus (the strategic will), throat (the diplomatic speech of the warrior-politician)
af: I know when to fight and when to negotiate. My power is strategic. The sword serves the word.
sh: The diplomat who has forgotten his sword — the political sophistication that loses its grounding in genuine power
ar: The Diplomatic Warrior / The Political Ogou
cb: Ogou Feray (the raw revolutionary fire) counterbalances Ogou Badagri (the measured political warrior). Revolution and strategy.

do: The cross of death, the cemetery, the passage of all souls, the Bawon who grants death with a cross
at: Bawon Lakwa ('Baron of the Cross') is one of the primary Bawon (Baron) family of Ghede Lwa — he stands at the cross in the cemetery, the mark that all graves bear. He holds the key to death: no one dies without his permission. His cross is the intersection of the living and the dead, the vertical and horizontal axes of existence. He is more solemn than Baron Samedi — the gravity of death rather than its laughter.
of: Black cross at the cemetery, black candles, rum with hot pepper, the grave's cross itself as altar
an: None — he is the cross
co: Black and purple (the grave's colours)
dp: Saturday / Pluto — the grave's cross
cr: Black tourmaline, jet, obsidian, the stone of graves
bp: Root (the grave), crown (the permission to die or to live)
af: I stand at the crossroads of life and death with equanimity. My time is not yet. The cross of death marks where I am not.
sh: The grave that cannot be left — Bawon Lakwa's shadow is the one who has permission to die but cannot be born again
ar: The Cemetery Cross / The Permission of Death
cb: Baron Samedi (the laughing death) counterbalances Bawon Lakwa (the solemn death). The gravity and levity of the same truth.

do: Cemeteries, death, healing the dying, the wife of Baron Samedi, the first dead woman
at: Maman Brigitte is the wife of Baron Samedi and the spirit of the first woman to die and be buried in a cemetery in Haiti. She is one of the few Lwa with clear European origin — she is related to the Irish Brigid, brought to Haiti through the enslaved Irish transported alongside Africans. She is fierce, foul-mouthed, drinks rum and smokes, and heals the sick. The Haitian expression of the Celtic sacred flame.
of: Black and purple candles, rum, cigars, offerings at the first grave in a cemetery, purple cloth
an: None specific
co: Black and purple, the gold of the healing flame
dp: Friday / Saturn — the healing death
cr: Obsidian, amethyst, black tourmaline
bp: Root (the first dead woman's grave), heart (the healing of the dying), throat (the foul-mouthed truth-speaking)
af: The first dead woman blesses my healing. I speak the unvarnished truth about death. The healing flame burns in the cemetery.
sh: The healer at the grave who forgets the living — the focus on the dead that disconnects from those still alive
ar: The Celtic-African Flame / The Cemetery Healer
cb: Baron Samedi (her husband — the death that laughs) counterbalances Maman Brigitte (the death that heals). The humour and the tenderness of the same inevitable truth.

do: The primordial giant from whom the world was made, the body that became the cosmos
at: Ymir is the original being of Norse cosmology — before the gods, before the world, there was only ice and fire and the gap between them (Ginnungagap), and from this void emerged Ymir, the first giant. The gods Odin, Vili, and Vé killed him and made the world from his body: his flesh became earth, his blood the sea, his bones the mountains, his skull the sky, his brains the clouds. Every stone and mountain is Ymir's sacred bone. This is the most complete expression of the 'world body' motif in any tradition.
of: Offerings made directly to the earth (acknowledging it as Ymir's sacred flesh), the touching of stone with reverence, silence at ancient geological formations
an: The Aurochs (the primordial cattle that suckled Ymir from the ice), Jörmungandr (the World Serpent who is made from his world-body)
co: Ice-white, flesh-pink, the grey of primordial stone, the red of primordial blood
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the weight and age of the first body
cr: Granite, flint, ancient bone, obsidian, any very old stone
bp: Every bone in the human body (as Ymir's body became the mountains of the world), root (the earth as sacred flesh)
af: Every stone I touch is a sacred bone. The world was made from the first body. I am walking on a being.
sh: The world that was made from a murder — the cosmos built on the first killing. The beauty of the world contains its violent origin.
ar: The World Body / The Sacred Murder of Creation
cb: The Aesir gods (who killed Ymir and made the world) counterbalance Ymir (the material of creation). The creator and the created-from.

do: The lord of Utgard (the outer realm), illusion, the test of cosmic limitation, the trickster of the giants
at: Utgard-Loki is the king of Utgard — the realm beyond the known world — who appeared to Thor as the giant Skrymir and tricked all the Aesir in a series of contests. He revealed at the end that Thor had come closer to accomplishing the impossible than any being ever had: the 'cat' he couldn't lift was the Midgard Serpent; the 'old woman' who defeated him in wrestling was Old Age. He represents the cosmic principle of the undefeatable limitation — and the dignity of giving one's all against it.
of: The acknowledgment of what cannot be overcome, offerings made to one's own limits rather than denying them
an: None — he is illusion itself
co: Vast grey, the colour of illusion, the fog of Utgard
dp: Wednesday / Uranus — the cosmic trickster beyond the known world
cr: Labradorite (the illusion stone), grey moonstone, obsidian mirror
bp: Crown (the realm beyond the known), third eye (the illusion that hides the truth of limitation)
af: The cat I cannot lift is the world serpent. My failure was the greatest performance of strength possible. I honour my cosmic limit with dignity.
sh: The cosmic trickster who reveals too late — the truth about limitation that could have been compassionately revealed but was withheld for sport
ar: The Illusion Lord / The Cosmic Limitation
cb: Thor (who was tricked, who gave his all, who was the most powerful being who ever tried) counterbalances Utgard-Loki (who devised the test). The tested and the tester.

do: The ocean, the brewer of the gods' mead, the devouring sea, the nine daughters of the wave
at: Aegir and Ran are the divine ocean pair of Norse mythology — not Aesir but Jotun (giants) who command universal respect. Aegir brews the mead for the gods' great feasts in his hall at the bottom of the sea; Ran spreads her net to catch the drowned. Their nine daughters are the nine types of waves. They govern the ocean's two faces: the generous host and the devouring huntress. Together they are the ocean's complete personality.
of: Gold thrown into the sea (sailors did this before voyages — Ran favoured gold), sea offerings, mead brewed and shared in ceremony, reverence before any sea voyage
an: All sea creatures — they are the ocean's divine parents
co: Deep sea blue, gold (Aegir's hall was lit by gold), the grey-green of Ran's waves
dp: Monday / Neptune — the ocean feast and the ocean net
cr: Gold (for Ran's ransom), aquamarine, sea glass, abalone
bp: Sacral (the pleasures of Aegir's feast and the terror of Ran's net), root (the sea bed where both hold court)
af: I offer gold to the sea before I sail. I feast in the ocean's generosity and I respect its devouring face. Ran and Aegir are both honoured.
sh: Ran's net — the drowned who did not carry gold, who were not welcomed in her hall but became lost shadows
ar: The Ocean Host and the Ocean Huntress / The Sea Marriage
cb: Odin and Frigg (the Aesir divine couple) counterbalance Aegir and Ran (the Jotun divine couple). The sky couple and the sea couple.

do: Winter, mountains, hunting, skiing, independence, the mountain woman's sovereignty
at: Skadi is the Jotun goddess of winter, mountains and the hunt — she came to Asgard to demand weregild for her father Thjazi's death. She chose her husband Njörðr by his feet (thinking they were Baldr's) and the marriage failed: she needed the mountains and he needed the sea. She is the supreme expression of the sovereign mountain woman: self-sufficient, fierce, skilled, willing to negotiate but never to be diminished. She gave her name to Scandinavia.
of: Mountain hikes as ceremony, offerings at the mountain's peak or at the forest's hunting ground, independence honoured as sacred, winter ceremonies
an: Wolf (she runs with wolves), eagle (the mountain eagle), reindeer
co: White (winter snow), grey (stone), the sharp silver of ice
dp: Saturday / Saturn — the mountain's endurance
cr: White quartz (snow crystal), grey moonstone (the winter sky), obsidian (the volcanic mountain)
bp: Root (the mountain sovereignty), solar plexus (the hunter's fierce skill), third eye (the eagle-vision of the mountain height)
af: I am the mountain woman. My independence is not loneliness — it is sovereignty. I hunt what I need and I ask no one's permission.
sh: The mountain so high it cannot accommodate anyone — Skadi's failed marriage teaches that sovereignty without the capacity for union is also a kind of imprisonment
ar: The Mountain Sovereign / The Winter Hunter
cb: Njörðr (the sea, the warmth, the southern shore) counterbalances Skadi (the mountain, the cold, the northern heights). The sea shore and the mountain summit — their failed marriage is the world's geography.

do: The theft of Thor's hammer, the power of lacking what one needs most, the giant who wanted a goddess
at: Thrym is the Jotun king who stole Thor's hammer Mjölnir and demanded the goddess Freyja as his bride as ransom. The resolution: Thor and Loki disguised Thor as the 'bride' Freyja and traveled to Thrym's hall, where Thor reclaimed his hammer and slew Thrym and his entire clan. Thrym represents the solar plexus principle of the power vacuum: the one who takes what is not his because he lacks the genuine authority he craves.
of: The acknowledgment of what has been taken and must be reclaimed, offerings of righteous demand, hammer imagery
an: None — the giant without an animal symbol, which reflects his lack of divine legitimacy
co: Dark, the colours of Jotunheim's grey winter
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the wrongful taking and the reclamation
cr: Iron (Thor's hammer), dark grey stone, black tourmaline
bp: Root (the wrongful claim on what is not his), solar plexus (the power taken without the authority to hold it)
af: I reclaim what was taken from me. The hammer of my power returns to my hand. I see through the disguise.
sh: The desire for what represents another's power — wanting the goddess and the hammer both, getting neither
ar: The Thief of Power / The Demand for the Unearnable
cb: Thor (who reclaims his hammer) counterbalances Thrym (who stole it). Legitimate power and stolen power.

do: The shape-shifting trickster, the chaos principle, the bound god, the father of Hel / Jörmungandr / Fenrir
at: Loki is the most complex figure in Norse mythology — simultaneously Aesir (he was Odin's blood brother) and Jotun (he was a giant by birth). He is the trickster whose tricks begin as helpful and end as catastrophic: the theft of Idun's apples, the death of Baldr, the cutting of Sif's hair. He is the cosmic chaos principle, the bound god (chained until Ragnarök), the parent of the three monsters that will destroy the world. He is both the funniest and the most tragic figure in Norse myth.
of: No fixed offering — Loki is not traditionally worshipped, but some modern practitioners offer him chaos (mixing things unexpectedly), laughter, or the acknowledgment of the trickster principle in one's own life
an: Salmon (he transformed into one), snake (associated with his children), the fly (he transformed into one)
co: Red and orange (fire — he is the father of Loki's fire, associated with destructive flame), black
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the ultimate trickster messenger
cr: Labradorite (the shape-shifter's stone), obsidian, fire opal
bp: Sacral (the shape-shifting, the fluid identity), throat (the trickster's cunning speech), root (the bound god — chained to the earth), crown (the cosmic chaos principle)
af: I acknowledge the trickster principle in my own life. Chaos has its own intelligence. I hold what is bound in me with awareness.
sh: Baldr's death — the trickster's final catastrophic act. Loki's shadow is the chaos that cannot be redirected before it destroys the most innocent.
ar: The Bound Trickster / The Cosmic Chaos
cb: Baldr (the innocent, the shining, the most beloved) counterbalances Loki (the chaos, the trickster, the one who killed the beloved). The most poignant polarity in Norse myth.

do: The primordial cold, the world before creation, the forces of chaos and dissolution at the cosmos's edges
at: The Hrimthursar (Frost Giants) are the primordial Jotun — they existed before the Aesir, before creation, at the very edge of Ginnungagap. They represent the chaos forces that the gods must constantly hold at bay; at Ragnarök they will return and the world will end. They are not evil in the simple sense but are the necessary chaotic dissolution that must be balanced by the cosmos's order. They represent the crown's deepest shadow: the return to the void.
of: Ice water offered at the edge of known territory, offerings made at the winter solstice acknowledging the power of the returning dark, the honest acknowledgment of what dissolves
an: Wolf (the wolves Skoll and Hati that chase the sun and moon), the World Serpent
co: White ice, the grey of Niflheim, the black of the void before creation
dp: Saturday / Saturn and Pluto — the primordial cold and dissolution
cr: Ice crystal, white quartz, ancient glacial stone, obsidian
bp: Root (the primordial cold in the earth), crown (the return to the void at the end of creation)
af: I acknowledge the primordial cold at the edge of my known world. The giants at the borders are part of the cosmos. Creation requires its counterforce.
sh: Ragnarök — the shadow is the moment when the Frost Giants succeed, when the chaos overcomes the cosmos
ar: The Primordial Cold / The Edge of Creation
cb: The Aesir (the gods who hold the cosmos in order) counterbalance the Hrimthursar (the giants who represent the chaos at creation's edge). Order and chaos in their Norse cosmic polarity.

do: The first emanation, the supreme crown, the point of unity before duality, the infinite condensed to a point
at: Kether is the first Sephirah of the Kabbalistic Tree of Life — the Crown, the supreme point of the infinite (Ein Sof) condensing into the first possible expression of being. It is beyond all attributes, beyond all quality — it is the point at which nothingness first becomes something. Associated with the divine name Ehyeh ('I Am' or 'I Will Be'), the Archangel Metatron, and the outermost heaven.
of: The deepest meditation, the offering of silence before prayer, white candles, crown imagery, the contemplation of the divine beyond all attributes
an: None — Kether is beyond all form
co: Brilliant white (the crown's light — before light divides into colour)
dp: Uranus / Neptune — the crown beyond all planets, associated with the Primum Mobile
cr: Clear diamond (the pure light), white selenite, apophyllite
bp: The crown of the head, the fontanelle — the entry point of the divine into the human body
af: Ehyeh. I Am. The infinite condenses to a point within me. The crown rests on the head that has been stilled.
sh: The crown without grounding — the tree that has only Kether and no Malkuth falls. The enlightenment that has no earth.
ar: The Point of Unity / The Supreme Crown
cb: Malkuth (the Kingdom — the earth, the tenth Sephirah) counterbalances Kether (the Crown). The highest counterbalanced by the lowest. The first and last of the Tree.

do: The first movement from unity, primordial wisdom, the Father, the Zodiac, the initial impulse of being
at: Chokmah is the second Sephirah — Wisdom, the first movement from the crown's unity. It is the initial flash of undifferentiated wisdom-energy, the Father, the masculine creative impulse before it takes form in Binah. Associated with the divine name Yah (the two-letter divine name), the Archangel Raziel ('Secret of God'), and the Zodiac. It is the third eye's deepest source — the wisdom before thought.
of: The flash of insight honoured and written down, offerings of the highest wisdom one has received, the Shema recited, silver candles
an: None — Chokmah is the undifferentiated wisdom-flash before animal form
co: Grey (the pearl-grey of wisdom before it divides), silver-white
dp: Uranus / Neptune — the zodiac's dome of the sky
cr: Star sapphire (the wisdom of the zodiac's stars), turquoise, amethyst
el: Fire (the first flash of divine energy)
bp: Crown (the wisdom's origin in the beyond), third eye (the wisdom-flash that is the deepest source of all true insight)
af: Yah. The flash of wisdom is available before I think. I receive the undifferentiated knowing and let it move through me.
sh: Wisdom without understanding — Chokmah (wisdom) without Binah (understanding) is an ungrounded flash that illuminates nothing because it cannot be processed
ar: The Flash of Wisdom / The Father
cb: Binah (Understanding — the third Sephirah) counterbalances Chokmah (Wisdom — the second). Father and Mother, the flash and the womb that receives it. The first great Kabbalistic polarity.

do: The Great Mother, the womb of creation, understanding that receives wisdom, Shabbat, sorrow and form
at: Binah is the third Sephirah — Understanding, the Great Mother, the dark womb that receives Chokmah's flash of wisdom and gives it form. She is associated with Shabbat (the Sabbath — the divine rest that gives form to the week), with sorrow (as the mother of form, she is also the mother of limitation), and with the highest divine feminine. The divine name is YHVH Elohim, the Archangel Tzaphkiel.
of: Shabbat candles lit as offering to Binah, the darkened room as the sacred womb, silence honoured, the contemplation of form as sacred limitation
an: None — she is the form before form
co: Black (the fertile darkness of the womb, the night of Binah)
dp: Saturn — the limitation that gives form, the boundary that creates structure
cr: Black sapphire, onyx, black pearl, deep garnet
el: Water (the dark waters of the womb)
bp: Crown (the source of form), third eye (the understanding that gives wisdom its shape), womb (the Great Mother principle)
af: I understand what I have received. The darkness of the womb is the darkness of creation. Form is a gift, not a prison.
sh: The Great Mother's sorrow — Binah as the mother of form is also the mother of death, limitation, and all that ends. Her shadow is the understanding that produces only grief.
ar: The Dark Womb / The Mother of Form
cb: Chokmah (the Father's wisdom-flash) counterbalances Binah (the Mother's understanding-womb). The first great polarity of the Tree.

do: Love, grace, mercy, the free flow of divine abundance, Jupiter's expansive benevolence
at: Chesed is the fourth Sephirah — Mercy or Loving-Kindness (hesed), the first Sephirah below the Supernal Triad. It is associated with Jupiter, with the divine name El, with the Archangel Tzadkiel, and with the color blue. Chesed represents the unrestricted flow of divine grace and abundance — the aspect of divinity that gives freely, loves unconditionally, and expands toward all.
of: Blue candles, acts of unrestricted generosity, prayers of gratitude for divine grace, the practice of Tzedakah (charitable giving)
an: Eagle (the noble vision of Chesed), bull (the abundance)
co: Blue (the expansive sky blue of divine mercy)
dp: Thursday / Jupiter — the expansive benevolence
cr: Sapphire, lapis lazuli, blue topaz, aquamarine
el: Water (the mercy that flows like water)
bp: Heart (the loving-kindness that flows freely), crown (the divine mercy that comes from above)
af: I receive divine grace freely and I give it freely. The loving-kindness of existence flows through me. I am an open channel.
sh: The mercy that enables — the loving-kindness without Gevurah's boundaries that allows what should be limited to continue. Grace without justice.
ar: The Open Grace / The Blue Mercy
cb: Gevurah (Strength/Severity — the fifth Sephirah) counterbalances Chesed (Mercy). The supreme Kabbalistic balance: loving-kindness and severity, grace and judgment. On the Tree, these two Sephiroth must be in perfect balance.

do: Judgment, severity, limitation, the divine power that enforces boundaries, Mars's warrior fire
at: Gevurah is the fifth Sephirah — Strength or Severity, the divine principle of judgment and limitation. Associated with Mars, the divine name Elohim Gibor, the Archangel Khamael. Gevurah provides the necessary counterbalance to Chesed's boundless mercy: without limitation, mercy becomes enabling; without strength, compassion becomes weakness. Gevurah is the divine warrior — the force that cuts away what is false and enforces the cosmic law.
of: Red candles, the honest acknowledgment of where limitation serves, the courageous act of enforcing a necessary boundary, martial arts as prayer
an: Lion (the martial strength of Gevurah)
co: Red (the martial fire)
dp: Tuesday / Mars — the divine severity
cr: Red jasper, ruby, carnelian, iron
bp: Solar plexus (the will to enforce boundaries), root (the firm ground that holds the limit)
af: I enforce the boundaries that serve the highest good. Severity is a form of love. I cut away what must be cut.
sh: The severity that forgets mercy — the judgment without Chesed's compassion that destroys rather than corrects
ar: The Divine Strength / The Enforcing Limit
cb: Chesed (mercy, grace) counterbalances Gevurah (severity, judgment). The supreme polarity of the middle pillar's wings.

do: The heart of the Tree of Life, beauty, the Self, the sun, the son, sacrifice and redemption
at: Tiphereth is the sixth Sephirah — Beauty, the heart of the Tree of Life. It is the point of balance between all the other Sephiroth, the centre through which all paths pass. Associated with the sun, the divine name YHVH, the Archangel Michael (as the solar protector), and all solar deities and saviour figures. Tiphereth governs the Higher Self, the consciousness of the solar principle, the beauty that arises when all the Tree's forces are in harmony.
of: Gold candles, heart-opening ceremonies, sunlight meditation, the cultivation of genuine beauty in one's life and character
an: Phoenix (the solar resurrection), lion (the solar animal), lamb (the sacrificial solar principle)
co: Gold (the sun's colour)
dp: Sunday / Sun — the solar centre
cr: Sunstone, amber, topaz, citrine, gold
el: Fire (the solar fire at the Tree's heart)
bp: Heart (the Tree's centre, the Self), solar plexus (the sun's radiance)
af: I am the meeting point of all forces within me. My higher self is both human and divine. The beauty of my being is the beauty of the sun.
sh: The beauty that becomes narcissism — Tiphereth's shadow is the Higher Self mistaken for the personal ego, the solar centre that shines for itself rather than for all
ar: The Heart of the Tree / The Solar Self
cb: Yesod (the foundation, the moon, the personal self) counterbalances Tiphereth (the beauty, the sun, the higher self). Moon and sun on the Tree's central pillar.

do: Nature, art, desire, the instinctual feelings, Venus, the elemental forces
at: Netzach is the seventh Sephirah — Victory or Eternity, associated with Venus, the divine name YHVH Tzvaot, and the Archangel Haniel. It governs the instinctual feelings, the raw emotional life, nature, art and desire. It is the sacral chakra's Kabbalistic counterpart: the place where the divine is felt in the body through beauty, nature, desire, and the raw force of emotional life.
of: Green candles, offerings in nature, art created as sacred act, the honest acknowledgment of desire, the veneration of beauty in all its forms
an: Lion (the passions), the wild animals of nature
co: Green (the verdant colour of Venus and nature)
dp: Friday / Venus — the nature, art and desire
cr: Emerald, rose quartz, green tourmaline, jade
el: Earth / Water (the elemental forces of nature)
bp: Sacral (the instinctual feelings), heart (the love of beauty and nature)
af: My emotions are a form of divine intelligence. Nature speaks directly to me. The beauty of desire is a sacred force.
sh: The emotions that overwhelm reason — Netzach's shadow is the raw feeling that cannot be integrated by Hod's intellect, the passion that destroys what it loves
ar: The Wild Nature / The Sacred Desire
cb: Hod (intellect, the eighth Sephirah) counterbalances Netzach (nature and instinct). The eternal polarity of Kabbalah: emotion and intellect, Venus and Mercury.

do: Intellect, communication, Mercury, magic, the written and spoken word, hermetic science
at: Hod is the eighth Sephirah — Splendour, associated with Mercury, the divine name Elohim Tzvaot, the Archangel Michael (as communicator) or Raphael. It governs the intellect, communication, the written word, and all forms of magic (which in Kabbalistic understanding is the manipulation of divine names). It is the throat and third eye chakra's Kabbalistic home: the word that creates reality.
of: Orange candles (Hod's colour), writing as sacred act, the study of language and its divine dimensions, the recitation of divine names
an: The hermaphrodite or androgyne (Hod governs the balanced blending of male and female intelligence), caduceus serpents
co: Orange (the brilliant orange of Mercurial splendour)
dp: Wednesday / Mercury — the divine communication
cr: Orange calcite, carnelian, golden labradorite, citrine
bp: Throat (the divine word), third eye (the intellect's clear vision), hands (writing)
af: Language is magic. The word I speak creates the world I inhabit. My intellect serves the divine.
sh: The intellect without feeling — Hod's shadow is the magical manipulation of language without Netzach's genuine emotional connection. Brilliant theory without living experience.
ar: The Divine Intellect / The Mercurial Word
cb: Netzach (the wild emotional force) counterbalances Hod (the ordered intellectual principle). The eternal tension of feeling and thinking on the lower part of the Tree.

do: The foundation, the astral plane, the moon, dreams, the collective unconscious, the sexual force
at: Yesod is the ninth Sephirah — Foundation, associated with the Moon, the divine name Shaddai El Chai, the Archangel Gabriel. It is the foundation of the manifest world — the astral plane, the realm of dreams, the collective unconscious, the sexual force that transmits life. It is the channel through which all the higher Sephiroth's energies are funnelled down to Malkuth. The sacral and root chakras' Kabbalistic expression: the foundation of physical reality.
of: Silver candles, moonwater, dream journal kept as sacred record, sexual energy consciously directed as spiritual practice, the colour purple or silver
an: None fixed — Yesod is the generative principle itself
co: Purple or silver (the moon's light filtered through the astral)
dp: Monday / Moon — the astral and the collective unconscious
cr: Moonstone, clear quartz, selenite, amethyst
bp: Sacral (the sexual-generative principle), root (the foundation of physical reality)
af: I am grounded in the astral foundation of reality. My dreams carry information. The moon governs the tides of my unconscious and I navigate them consciously.
sh: The astral plane without discernment — Yesod's shadow is the person lost in the collective unconscious, in dreams, in astral experience that is not grounded in Malkuth
ar: The Astral Foundation / The Moon Mirror
cb: Tiphereth (the sun, the Higher Self) counterbalances Yesod (the moon, the personal self). Sun and moon on the central pillar. The higher and lower self in their necessary relationship.

do: The earth, the physical world, the Shekhinah (the divine feminine presence), the final manifestation
at: Malkuth is the tenth and final Sephirah — the Kingdom, the Earth, the physical world, the place where all the Tree's energies finally manifest. The divine feminine Presence (Shekhinah) dwells here — exiled, according to Kabbalistic theology, until the final redemption. Malkuth is simultaneously the lowest and the most sacred Sephirah: it is the point where the divine is most hidden and most present. The Kabbalistic root chakra.
of: Earth itself (soil held as sacred offering), the Shabbat as the reunion of Malkuth with Tiphereth (Bride and Bridegroom), care for the physical world as the highest spiritual act
an: The Sphinx (the guardian of Malkuth's gate), bull, eagle, lion, man (the four fixed signs — the Kerubim)
co: The four colours of Malkuth: olive, russet, citrine, black (the four elements in their most manifest form)
dp: Saturday / Earth — the earth itself
cr: Onyx, black tourmaline, all earthy stones, obsidian, rock crystal from the earth
el: Earth (all four elements in their manifest physical expression)
bp: Root (the physical earth, the body, the feet touching the ground), crown (paradoxically — the exile and return of the Shekhinah)
af: The Kingdom is here. The divine Presence dwells in the physical world. The earth beneath my feet is the Shekhinah in exile, waiting to be recognised.
sh: The exile — Malkuth's great shadow is the sense of the divine as utterly absent from the physical world; the materialism that cannot see the Shekhinah in matter
ar: The Kingdom of Earth / The Shekhinah in Exile
cb: Kether (the Crown) counterbalances Malkuth (the Kingdom). The first and last of the Tree. Above and below. The infinite point and the infinite plane. Their reunion is the purpose of all of Kabbalistic practice.

do: The hidden Sephirah, the abyss, forbidden knowledge, the knowledge that was before the fall
at: Da'ath ('Knowledge') is the hidden, invisible Sephirah of the Kabbalistic Tree — it appears in the Abyss between the Supernal Triad and the lower seven Sephiroth. It is the knowledge that cannot be directly approached without crossing the Abyss. Associated with the union of Chokmah (Wisdom) and Binah (Understanding) — when wisdom and understanding are united, knowledge emerges. It also governs the gnosis of the fall: Da'ath is the tree whose fruit was eaten in Eden.
of: Silence at the abyss, the willingness to know what cannot be known safely, the crossing of the Abyss in advanced magical practice
an: None — Da'ath is the hidden Sephirah with no visible form
co: Lavender-grey, the colour of the Abyss at dusk — neither black nor white
dp: Pluto / Uranus — the knowledge that transforms through crisis
cr: Labradorite (the hidden fire), grey obsidian, black moonstone
bp: Crown (the knowledge from the highest), third eye (the gnosis that crosses the Abyss), throat (the spoken knowledge that was forbidden)
af: I stand at the edge of what can be known. The Abyss is not empty — it is the fullness of knowing itself. I cross with clear eyes.
sh: The Abyss — the crossing of Da'ath without sufficient preparation produces fragmentation; the knowledge gained before the vessel is ready destroys the vessel
ar: The Hidden Knowledge / The Abyss Crossing
cb: The entire Tree of the Sephiroth counterbalances Da'ath — the full structure of being counterbalanced by the knowledge that underlies and crosses it all.

do: The great creative serpent, water, the Dreamtime, creation and destruction, the wet season
at: The Rainbow Serpent is one of the oldest figures in Aboriginal Australian religion — present in traditions across the entire continent under hundreds of names. She shaped the landscape by moving through it, created rivers and waterholes, swallowed and regurgitated people as part of initiation, and continues to inhabit the deepest waterholes. She is simultaneously the creative and the terrifying.
of: Ceremonies at waterholes, ochre paintings in her honour, deep respect for water sources — never polluting sacred waterholes
an: Python (she is usually depicted as a vast python with a composite head), fish, freshwater creatures
co: All the colours of the rainbow, the deep green-black of still water
dp: Monday / Neptune — the rainbow and the still deep water
cr: Rainbow serpent ochre, rainbow moonstone, obsidian
el: Water / Earth — she lives in the deep water and shaped the earth
bp: The entire spine (the serpent path), sacral (creative and sexual power), crown (the rainbow's highest arc)
af: The oldest creative force moves through me. My spine is the rainbow serpent's path. I am shaped by what is most ancient.
sh: The swallowing that does not release — the initiation that consumes rather than transforms
ar: The Oldest Creator / The Dreamtime Serpent
cb: Baiame (the sky father) counterbalances the Rainbow Serpent (the earth-water creative force). The oldest polarity on Earth: sky and earth-water in their Australian Aboriginal expression.

do: Rain, clouds, the wet season, fertility, the law, the Dreamtime creators of the Kimberley
at: The Wandjina are the Dreamtime cloud and rain spirits of the Kimberley region — depicted in extraordinary rock art over 60,000 years old. They have no mouths (if they had mouths they would cause too much rain). They created the land and the law and then painted themselves into the rock faces, where they remain still living, still governing the seasons. Their rock paintings must be regularly repainted by custodian clans or the rains will fail.
of: The repainting of the Wandjina rock art by the custodian clan (Ngarinyin, Worrorra, Wunambal peoples), rain ceremonies, absolute care for the sacred sites
an: Fish (the rain brings the fish), the emu, the goanna
co: White (their bodies in the paintings), red and yellow ochre, black
dp: Monday / Moon — the rain and the seasonal cycle
cr: White clay, ochre, the painted stone of the Kimberley
el: Water / Air — the clouds and rain
bp: Crown (the radiating Wandjina head-glow), the entire body as rain-bringer
af: The ancient creators live in the rock around me. I repaint what needs repainting. The sacred site is my responsibility.
sh: The Wandjina whose painting has faded — the sacred law no longer maintained, the rain that fails when custodians forget
ar: The Rain Ancestor / The Living Rock Art
cb: The Rainbow Serpent (the earth-water creative force) counterbalances the Wandjina (the sky-water creative force). The serpent below and the cloud spirit above.

do: The creator, the wedge-tailed eagle, the law-giver of the Kulin nations
at: Bunjil (the wedge-tailed eagle) is the supreme ancestral being and creator of the Kulin nations of Victoria. He created the land, the people and the law. He and Waa (the crow) divide all Kulin social organisation into two moieties — everything in the world is either Bunjil or Waa.
of: The eagle feather as sacred offering, respectful observation of the wedge-tailed eagle, the living of the law he created
an: Wedge-tailed eagle (he IS the eagle), the bat (his helpers)
co: The brown and gold of the wedge-tailed eagle, white of the eagle's breast
dp: Sunday — the supreme creator's day
cr: Eagle feather (sacred), gold topaz (the eagle's eye colour)
bp: Crown (the eagle's supreme height), solar plexus (the eagle's hunting power), eyes (the eagle's legendary vision)
